I refuse to view any pack from the sims as 'good' considering my money has essentially been wasted. The game rarely works, yet any feature used to take money (like the new marketplace) runs flawlessly. They're priorities are clear. This is NOT a gamer friendly product. It's a way to milk a loyal base of lifelong Sims fans, like me. Save your money.

Now lets be sooooo for real now😂

The world is ugly asf, LA holly wood? more like getmethetfouttahere. They are really charging 40 bucks for a half baked expansion pack, no, this ain't even an expansion pack... Its a game pack. The fame system is new and only in this pack. There is no actual karma in base game, no real gossip, nothin...so what does this expand on? your loss of money in your account. 

im gonna stop joking....

Okay, CAS is ugly. I use like one or two hairs and maybe some clothing that aren't ugly. It's lacking, its ghoulish, it's tacky. I love tacky but it ain't good tacky. It's fine the dresses are alright. but it some of it is kind of ugly if it isnt your style. 

The world had so much potential, but nooooo...NO houses by the ocean, no townhouses. It couldve been a good pack if EA wasn't greedy. Like City living + Get together + Get famous. That wouldve been a good pack since penny is supposed to be social media famous and there's a dj booth in get together. 

The fame system and reputation bar is a cool addition to the game but its NOT worth 40 dollars when reputation couldve been in base game. It's a nice feature and you can opt out of being famous whenever, also you can gain fame for a lot of things in the game. 

DO NOT buy for the world ts needs a refresh. only buy it if you want your sims to GET FAMOUS [title drop] thats really it. just pirate (or buy from a reseller idk) this lame ahh pack.

For me, this is a disappointing one. Indeed it seemed to be excited to be a stars and influencers, but sometimes the fame system can made the gameplay annoying. Everywhere the famous sims visit, there's a bunch of their random fans and paparazzi spawn in the places, and that quiet affect the gameplay in other expansion packs.

There's one new career to choose, an actor, which players can follow them to work. It was quite a routine work, preparing your sims and performing on the marker as the tasks instructed. In addition, there's a bunch of new hobbies, mixing music and being a freelance influencer, recording a videos on working station or drone then edit and post. Mixing music made great amount of simoleons which I quiet like it.

The decorations are focusing on ornate and elegant stuffs. So, the price of the stuffs is pricey too. Else is focusing on props decoration which is fit for studio rather than using as decorations (Most of them is incredibly huge).

For the maps, we got only a few empty lots to build, which in my opinion, it wasn't worth the price.

Great CAS and build/buy, fame system’s meh, world’s a mess, and the characters are iconically hateable. EA’s cash grab strikes again with overpriced nonsense. Not a necessary pack.
Rating: 3/10

this game (with its all DLCs) might have more than 1000 bugs, and if so, the list (which you can see in the game's official forums) still keeps growing every Update and DLC release. See official forums so you can have an idea of how many: https://forums.ea.com/category/the-sims-4-en/ideas/the-sims-4-bug-reports-en

Also, this (vanilla) game's community might be one of the most toxic and vain ones. And childish too. I'm supposing this statement based on how the game's DLCs and content are boring and dull when they are based on the community's feedback, compared to very good content created by Sims game modders.

Another thing I'd like to add is that there are too many technical issues not resolved nor responded to by the community moderators or the support team. This also applies to the bugs. The feeling it gives is that there are only players in the forums, because the moderators do not show themselves too much. It looks as if they are not participating in getting bug information to send to the devs, because they rarely answer: so it gives the sensation you are talking to a blank wall when you reach for help. See forums, you'll notice there are a lot of people asking for help and many of them are not provided with solutions: https://forums.ea.com/category/the-sims-4-en/discussions/the-sims-4-technical-issues-pc-en

Here's the part of the game that I feel frustrated the most: a horrible problem of the game is the usage of F.O.M.O. and marketing to gain players. Why not give more content to the game like waves in Sulani so we can surf with Dolphins, fully loaded neighbourhood (or at least a cache system for the current neighbourhood so it's faster to visit a close house) instead of load screen dependant for each single house or apartment, bring cats and dogs a work/career system and make them controllable, add Get To Work version of default game jobs/careers, or start adding a multiplayer system like there's one in the cellphone version? I mean, there are so many fun components that could be added, even if it's a "DLC of a DLC". No, their conclusion to bring more players was: add an F.O.M.O. Timed Event system with some exclusive items that are bonded to your account only if you play during that time.

If they can add the Burglar, they can do a lot more too!

name: The Sims™ 4 Realm of Magic
description: WHAT'S INCLUDED IN THIS PACK About the Game Explore a supernatural world and master the art of wizardry in The Sims™ 4 Realm of Magic.* This countryside town may look and feel like other neighborhoods, but once the secret portal is unlocked, the true magic begins. Potions, wands, and even a floating world will charm your Sim, but beware of spells gone wrong! Learn the craft of wizardry, summon a familiar for protection, and immerse yourself in this spellbinding realm. Key features Discover the neighborhood — An unreal world awaits your Sim, and when you stumble upon the secret portal there are mystical discoveries at every turn. Interact with familiars, cast spells on Sims around you, and concoct potions for friends and enemies. Learn the craft — Study up on classic teachings, learn from sorcerous mentors, and become a Spellcaster by completing a quest. Collect and cast spells to make Sims fight, conjure food, or even turn others into objects. Create drinkable potions to make Sims fall in love, remove unwanted curses, or live forever! Get real familiar — An ethereal familiar — or protector — will help your Sim become the Spellcaster they were destined to be. Summon a dragon, a fairy, or the familiar that best suits your Sim’s personality as a personal guard and give it a creative name. Dress for supernatural success — Get in the spirit of sorcery with outfits that suit your Sim. Over-the-top attire is cauldron-ready, while a subdued ensemble is perfect for casting new spells. Collect wands and brooms to finish the look, then decorate rooms with enchanting objects.
short_description: Explore a supernatural world and master the art of wizardry in The Sims™ 4 Realm of Magic.

reviews.review_text:
I’ve never posted a review before. I wanted to post this specifically to tell people that it is worth it.

Having only Island Living, Snowy Escape, and Weather, I wanted something a bit more occulty. I finally decided on Realms and at first I was overwhelmed. The overcharging mechanic, the practicing, and the curses made me annoyed. Though, as I spent more time with it actually really like how it comes together. The magic (though OP) is very handy and fun to use. The dueling is actually very fun and you never feel like you’re certain on winning. The rewards from dueling aren’t bad either. I also like the training and learning new things, it feels much more in depth than most sims mechanics (compared to what I’m use to anyway.) I’m only a few hours in but I’m thoroughly enjoying it and can see myself being a wizard on most playthroughs. 7.8/10
While not as wide in content as Sims 3's Supernatural, I think this is a decent one. A bit pricey though.
I love this expansion! it is one of my favourite new worlds to explore and enjoy!!! I have like 3 witch families!
Magic? Yes please! Took a minute to figure out. Once I did, I found the Realms of Magic to be a great sandbox magic system

Probably the most fun I've had with this game so far!

I really like that spellcasters can just like, learn spells whenever instead of having to do repeated magic-related acts like in The Sims 3 Supernatural and waiting for the next level to unlock a few new spells, really makes it more smooth and less tedious to actually "upgrade" your spellcasters
The Realm of Magic itself is a fun area to explore, too

So yeah, me likey, that's all I have to say

Similair in intention to the Vampires and Werewolves. You don't gain much having a spellcaster, their a bit overpowered, awkward and not well balanced compared to Werewolves and Vampires. They should have had some negative trade offs... Or been balanced. 

As usual, I don't just judge the pack on the Occult they add, but the extras... And on its on the packs not worth it. Theres some stuff that fits in with the Vampires design, the "Get Famous" pack and "City Living", but overall, the extras don't add much without noting the presence of the Cauldron and other bits. 

The pack is very Harry Potter inspired and though theres some nice stuff here, its just not enough. Theres no "traditional" mage designs like the classic witch and wizard. It all feels like the pack has too much "modern magic" approach, again like Harry Potter... It would have been nice if there was some medieval, middle ages or Victorian era designs in the mix to add spice, but the pack feels like its missing too much. You can find a witches hat in the seasons related DLC, but you have to get creative with clothing. 

Overall, stick with Werewolves and Vampires, ignore the spellcasters. 

And if your buying it for the extras... You've really not missed much, theres nothing else to see. 

The biggest cheat is the cauldron. It can make food that sits there and never goes off so long as it stays in there. The cauldron can be used to make Mac and Cheese by anyone and that means you have 12 servings of it that can sit there forever. Who needs a fridge? Oh and some of the extras are cheaper then the base standard alternatives. Like the Werewolf pack providing a cheaper bed, City Living providing run down but cheaper countertops... Look at the bathroom stuff. So for less money, you have a cheaper and better looking alternative. 

This pack is just broken and unbalanced. 

And the neighbourhood, while I'm at it? Its on par with Werewolves and Vampires, but everything in it looks too mundane. And this overall is my big issues with this pack. Ib the future, I'd love to see in Sims 5 a more wizard-tower like build even if its 3 floors big or traditional witches hut in the neighbourhood maps... But alas... We get Harry Potter.

I really regret buying this. Have tried several times to get my spell casters to level up and nothing seems to work. I have absolutely no patients to take a whole darn day to ask npc in magic place about spells. I want to be able to play the game and level up my sims and get skills point to also level up if i cant even do that then why should i even play. Im just going to stick to my vampire dlc it at least works and leveling is way more fun.
The Spells of the Realm are more like uninspired cheats than actually inspired and creative magic. The Sages should be ashamed and go back to peddling snake oil.
ugli

to get  any spells you just talk to one of 3 npcs then click "ask to teach X magic" then lern ONE spell and wait about 25 minutes to be able to ask again, THERE ARE MORE THAN20 SPELLS! it takes forever

name: The Sims™ 4 Cottage Living Expansion Pack
description: What's included in this pack About the Game Delight in the quaint charm of The Sims™ 4 Cottage Living Expansion Pack with animal friends, garden-to-table meals, and a close-knit community. At home, cook with the family using the freshest ingredients. Explore the forest to befriend woodland creatures or search for morsels, or head to town to meet the locals. Whether Sims are hugging a cow, making friends (or enemies) with their chickens, or growing the biggest vegetables around, the countryside is abundant with new experiences and ways to connect with the world around them. KEY FEATURES Raise or Befriend Animals — At home, the cottage cow provides fresh (flavored!) milk for everyone while chickens lay eggs in the coop. Gather llama wool for crafts, and beware mischievous foxes—the pesky critters love eggs! In the forest, keep an eye out for wildlife like rabbits and birds to earn their trust for occasional gifts and help in the garden. Live Off the Land — Make cooking and gardening fun for the whole family. New recipes and Lot Challenges require fresh ingredients for each meal. Whether Sims grow their own produce, visit the village market, or search for morsels in the wild, fresh is best! Up for a challenge? Try growing oversized crops to enter into the competition at the Finchwick Fair. Embrace Village Life — Henford-on-Bagley is a cozy village where everyone knows everyone. So pack a picnic basket and cross-stitch hoop, and head down to The Gnome's Arms pub to kick back with the locals after a long day. Earn unique gifts and learn about the town’s lore from neighbors, or participate in the Finchwick Fair to show off your most prized cow or chicken.
short_description: Delight in the quaint charm of The Sims™ 4 Cottage Living Expansion Pack with animal friends, garden-to-table meals, and a close-knit community.

The Cottage Living Expansion Pack takes us to the countryside and encourages an off-the-grid lifestyle. Experience farm life with this expansion and take your Sim on a journey of wholesome, rural living.

The new build elements have found their way into almost every house I’ve built since getting this pack. The windows, thatched roofs, hedges, and fences instantly make your home look cosy and inviting.

The new clothes and hairstyles perfectly match the theme of the pack while still being stylish, so most of my Sims wear at least one outfit from this collection.

Now onto the new gameplay features. You can raise chickens for fresh eggs, keep a cow for milk, and own a llama for wool. You can participate in fairs and compete for prizes, show off your prettiest cow or llama, or take home the ribbon for the largest vegetable. It’s a fun challenge to win first place, and it will push your gardening skills to the limit as you try to grow the biggest crops possible.

Foxes will sneak onto your lot to steal eggs and threaten your chickens, so you either need to level up your Handiness skill to repair the coop or befriend the foxes to protect it. Speaking of wildlife, you can also befriend rabbits and birds, or enrage them. Talk to them, give them gifts they like, and benefit from their friendship.

I believe the best way to experience this pack is by starting with a brand-new household living off the grid. Alternatively, if you have a teen ready to age up into a young adult, this is the perfect opportunity for them to move out, embrace rural life, and make new friends at the fairs.

This is a delightful expansion that takes you away from suburban and city living into a calmer, more relaxed atmosphere. The possibilities feel endless: harvest your own fruits and vegetables, learn cross-stitching, sell your produce, and truly enjoy farm life.
Adds tonnes of gameplay
This pack has always been fun ^^
The buggiest pack yet! I love the vibes of everything but the price is INSANE for a DLC that breaks your game.
i hate that in this game your cows cant interact with eachother because no one understands the magic of cow friendship and its so sad.
I’m begging you, just fix the bugs!  Gameplay is so fun but it’s completely ruined by the bugs

First of all, this DLC is super nice add to the game. I have fully enjoyed it. It does not feel empty or useless. I don't know if I can play "modern" sim families anymore after this lol. 

Why negative review? Because it is BUGGY. VERY buggy. It is been 4years out and the worst bugs are still there. I don't know how many times I have needed to restart the game because of bugs or lost hours of progression because I had to immiatedly restart without saving to not make more damage. Worst bugs which definitely SHOULD be fixed and harms your gameplay:

1. After your cow or alpaca is taken away from their barn (you took them away or they died of old age) it is sometimes super hard to get new one in the barn because the Sim refuses to place the purchased animal there. You need to use cheats, restarts, travel somewhere and sometimes do all 3 together to place the barn animal in a barn. 

2. If you have kids they sometimes grow at least 3 ages in one birthday. This makes them older/same age than their parents. This happened to all of the children I had in my household so far. Only fix is to restart whole game without saving so unlucky if you played hours without saving between. 

3. Very often your Sim gets stuck washing a dog, the timer don't move and the dog wont get clean. So you need to repeatedly wash your dog again and manually check the timer works or your Sim keeps washing the dog forever. This seems so far only being a problem in the Cottage Living map. Very annoying if you have lot to do as farmer and you got multiple dirty dogs. 

If these 3 annoying, gameplay harming bugs gets fixed then I can only recommend.
lmao not worth the cash

name: The Sims™ 4 Vampires
description: What's included in this pack About the Game Transform your Sims into powerful vampires and live for eternity in an eerie new destination with The Sims™ 4 Vampires. Create a variety of vampires using all-new tools, and unlock unique Powers that give your Sims supernatural abilities. Escape to Forgotten Hollow, build a lair, and hang out with other immortals. Key features Create vampires — With all-new Create A Sim options, create vampire Sims that are alluring or frightening. Choose their fangs, add mysterious glowing eyes, even customize a unique Dark Form to transform into when enraged or spooking other Sims. Unleash supernatural powers — Earn and wield dozens of unique Powers that give vampires the ability to control Sims’ minds and conjure spiritual energy. Balance your skills by taking on weaknesses, and unlock new Powers by winning epic duels to advance your rank. Escape to Forgotten Hollow — The dark, mist-covered land of Forgotten Hollow is the perfect place for vampires to socialize and let loose. Visit the abandoned mansion to discover its secrets, collect ingredients for gruesome new recipes, or move in and build your own lair. Live the immortal life — Experience the life of a vampire and choose how to live. Will you drink from Sims or eat Plasmafruit to quench your thirst? Embrace evil and stir up trouble, or blend in and start a vampire family? Whatever you choose, always avoid sunlight and garlic foods.
short_description: Transform your Sims into powerful vampires and live for eternity in an eerie new destination with The Sims™ 4 Vampires.

name: The Sims™ 4 City Living
description: What's included in this pack About the Game Do your Sims long to leave the suburbs behind? Fulfill their dreams in the diverse city of San Myshuno, where they can discover exciting neighborhoods, move into a new apartment, check out fun festivals, and more. In The Sims™ 4 City Living, your Sims can experience the joys and challenges of sharing close quarters with other Sims. Work your way up from a starter unit to a penthouse as your Sims launch new metropolitan careers! Key features Explore the bustling city of San Myshuno — This densely populated city has enough unique neighborhoods to give your Sims plenty to explore. Get creative in the Arts Quarter, or sing your heart out at the Karaoke Bar in the Fashion District. Head over to the Spice Market to shoot some hoops, or invite a few friends over to play video games after a long day at the office in Uptown. Discover lively cultural festivals — Festivals are great opportunities to enrich your Sims. Let them watch street performers, enter competitions, and meet a wide variety of folks from every walk of life. Check out the Curry Challenge at the Spice Festival, or light fireworks with a hot date at the Romance Festival. Live in apartments and get closer with your neighbors — Lots of new neighbors mean all kinds of opportunities for relationships, both good and bad. Your Sims might make a new best friend or fall in love, then debate giving them a key to their place. And what about those noisy neighbors and their late night dance parties? You'll need to decide how to handle them — if you can't beat them, maybe you should join them. Work your way up from starter apartment to penthouse — Choosing the right apartment for your Sims can be a challenge. You'll need to balance the charm of your first starter unit with possible setbacks, like cockroaches, leaky pipes, or electrical problems. Help your Sims work their way up to living in a Skyscraper Penthouse with panoramic views and even a Talking Toilet! Embark on new, metropolitan careers — There are all kinds of new opportunities in the city, so make sure your Sims take advantage of them. As a politician, your Sims can make dramatic speeches while fighting for a good cause. Maybe they'd prefer a more refined career as a food or art critic. Or they could inspire the masses working in social media.
short_description: Take your Sims to the city and explore everything it has to offer, from bustling action outside your door, to unique situations that come only with apartment living in The Sims™ 4 City Living.
supported_languages: Simplified Chineselanguages with full audio support

reviews.review_text: can my neighbours shut up

I really love City Living, and if I dad to rate my favourite sims 4 packs, it would 100% be at the top of that list.

 The apartments are actually way more fun to play with than I thought they were going to be. the loud neighbors and different pests in the apartments created a lot of fun story telling moments in my game, which I really enjoyed. The world is really nice, and I like the addition of the different districts which all have unique styles. The festivals are also a really fun addition, and there's enough of content at them that it doesn't get boring but not enough for it to get overwhelming at all. 

i couldn't recommend this pack more, and even though I bought it on sale, I would buy it offsale as well.

Not the pack that you would expect to be the best but it really is. it comes with the least amount of bugs (as far as i can tell though it doesn't mean there aren't any at all). Out of all packs this is the one i end up integrating into my gameplay the most and even though its relatively old this packs world is the one i live almost every time.

City Livings main positive aspect out of the harbour, is its clothing selection. This adds a lot of clothing that make Sims look RICH. There are luxury looking items. That said, like a lot of stuff thats made with rich snobs in mind... The clothing is gaudy. These are still some of the best outfits in the game, and I'd say the pack is worth it for the clothing alone. This is one of the packs I'd buy alongside Vampire to just make Sims look wealthy even if they aren't, with the added bonus over the Vampires pack that the rich Sims can side step looking goth. 

Not just in terms of styles, but in general, the modeller(s?) who worked on them made most of the items look good overall. I'm not too impressed with bottoms, full dresses and tops though, good, good, good. 

It also adds some cultural stuff such as headwear, allowing ethnic minorities to have more choices to their Sims to reflect their culture in-game more. A minor bonus, but a huge bonus because most Sim packs are catered to a purely American audience and the base headwear you get isn't much. 

There is also some silly outfits for randomness which are fine, silly outfits are also nice in the CAS, for games where you just don't want to be serious. Their typical of the kind of things you find in the Sims franchise for silly games, the odd joke or what not. 

My one crib with this packs CAS selection is the hairstyles could have been better, they have this mid-1900s feel to them (which in general a lot of clothing in game feels 1900s) and there is no jewellery to go with the pack or makeup. It feels incomplete because of it. Like they begun a pack with some great intentions but didn't finish it. I know CAS changed over time and this is the basic set up for CAS since its an older pack, but this remains a problem for the pack itself. It adds a lot of great CAS options but doesn't complete them. Normally I'd not fuss, but this is the only pack where 75% of a CAS completion feels like a let down just because the CAS is some of the best overall. 

Added bonus; most packs tend to provide a lot for females and then 50% for males, but this one has a decent amount for both male and female designs and thats a bonus. 

Onto the main aspect of the pack, renting in the city... ITs okay... Sims 2 did it best still. I'd not just out of the window with joy over this aspect as its more annoying then practical. Weakest of the 3 games with renting options in, 2nd best after Sims 2. 

Onto my least favourite aspect...The pack offers almost identical versions of some household items to the base game, just their "used". This feels cheap and a complete cash-out. I get their cheaper and all... But thats not the point. The only good part about this is if you combine it with the Werewolf pack and the Vampire pack, you can add to this "run down" building look between the used furniture, the cobwebs from the Vampires and the cracks in the walls from both this pack and Werewolves, in addition to the things like a desk made out of a door from werewolves. So bonus, congrats, you want that poor Sims build, you've got it. 

I do lover the "room dividers" as they feel so funny to have in the game, like you put it into a teenager's room to seperate the half from teenager and their little sibling in a "thats your side of the room, this is mind" thing, again, also buying on the poor Sims build, mum and dad are so poor they had to split one room for two siblings.  

One bonus is the decorations has a lot of wall art based decorations... To the point I'd argue there is far, far too much and it feels its been done on the cheap. Like the same 3 framed wall art will have a different design...Feels like another cash out. And this is a problem, this entire pack has this "you stopped before you finished" feel to it, and it feels like to save time they just repeated a lot of the wall art to turn 1 design into 3 or more designs. 

As for the new furniture, there are some stuff designed to look exotic, like they came from India or Africa, this goes with the outfits I mentioned earlier... But while the outfits are a plus, the furniture is not, it looks VERY tacky and a little bit insulting to the audience that its trying to appeal to. Like, "okay, I'll add a turban, not here's a gaudy, tacky looking side table to go with your man your designing, so while we lift up your culture representative in-game one way, we'll bring it down another way". I don't know enough to know what a side table from India looks like... But this one looks like the stuff they sell to tourists worth £1 to create but they'd sold to some gullible foreigner for £50 in some random place. I don't know, theres just something not right with our they look. Don't sue me over this part of the comments, I'm saying the model makes the entire thing look like its for kids and it misses the mark its supposed to hit. Plus, theres just not enough of it... It feels like they just slapped it in to say "look my non-American gamers, a side table!". Yeah... I really don't like this one side table, No matter where I put it, it looks so wrong. 

That said, the city chic stuff meant to look like a 1980s and 1990s bachelor pad, is all good. Definitely stylish looking and design.

I am very disappointed in the selection of stuff related to building rooms and wallpapers/floors/etc, the actual house building aspect is the weakpoint of it. 

I brought this at the same time as "Life and Death" and surprisingly, both packs are good for what they give. But Life and Death stood out because of how much more building content it offers, whereas City Living is more geared towards CAS. 

Overall, one of the nicer packs, the renting sucks. But I like to say "what does this pack offer without its main gimmick" to additions to the game, because you know full dam well 5 months later your playing without that gimmick anyway. This pack offers enough to warrant buying it on sale based on items and CAS alone. 

Also, there are new careers for your Sims, which adds to the choices you can make your Sims enter. 

Screw the point of the DLC.

Edit:
Going to add, buying this at the same time as "Life and Death" feels weird because a lot of the same content they add is identical in direction. It feels like buying those two may have been the strangest combo, but they went creepy well together.

name: The Sims™ 4 High School Years Expansion Pack
description: What's included in this pack About the Game Be a good (or bad) student, join a club, start a trend, and make the most of being a teenager with The Sims 4 High School Years Expansion Pack. Navigate iconic teenage experiences like attending prom, pulling pranks, experimenting with identity and trends, and forming friendships and rivalries at extracurricular events. There’s a lot to do before graduation: teen Sims can attend class, interact with teachers, and even earn money as style influencers or streamers. KEY FEATURES: Experience high school — Experience all the ups and downs of high school. Attend classes in person, get to know your teachers, hang out in the cafeteria, and even decorate your locker. Your most treasured moments might happen while loitering around with your friends after school. Iconic teen moments — Dance the night away at prom and celebrate your graduation ceremony with family and friends (if you keep up with your schoolwork). In addition to those big moments, participate in after-school activities and teams, and experience the rollercoaster ride of puberty. Shake things up — High school is a time of self-discovery. Find the confidence to ask out your crush or the guts to skip class (don’t let the principal catch you). Teen Sims will explore their own likes and dislikes and have new opportunities to cause mischief. Pranks and sneaking out after dark can have consequences, so be careful you don’t get caught. Explore your style — Make your bedroom your own, plan outfits with clothes designed by Depop sellers, and become a Simfluencer. Teen Sims can earn money by selling outfits and hyping up looks they design on Trendi right from their bedrooms, which are now more interactive than ever. Use a tablet, read a book in bed, or even have a pillow fight!
short_description: Class is in session. Be a good (or bad) student, join after-school activities, explore aesthetics, start trends, find your identity, and make the most of being a teenager with The Sims 4 High School Years Expansion Pack.

reviews.review_text:
I bought this dlc and I don't regret that. Here are some pros and cons:

Pros:
- you can play as a teenager who goes to school, that is the point of this dlc, but it is not the only thing where your sim can go. It was nice suprise that you can go to pier (is it called pier? I don't play sims in english) and you can also go to bubble tea shop, where you can make and buy outfits.
- you can sell the outfits you make, so even if you play as a teenager and you don't have time for working, you can make some money.
- you can go to parties
- you can chat your sim's friends and you can make posts
- you can meet lot of another teenage sims way quickier than in base game
- you can edit the school
- this dlc also adds some aspirations for teenage sims and you can choose which clothes do you like or dislike
- and last, but not least: it adds cool new clothes, hairstyles and furniture

Cons:
- it is maybe not really worth the price
- just like in the base game, you have to do your sim's homework, but it is easy to forget it. Just being at school and attending lessons doesn't  make your grades better.
- it took me long time to find how to get to the pier, because you can't go straight there, but you can go there with your sim from park or house near the pier
- you can't do a lot of things at school
- you don't really know what to do at the football team's action
- the school ball is pretty short

In the end, I really enjoy plaing with this dlc. It adds a lot of cool things, not just school. You can make school of your dreams and you can make cool bedrooms for your sims. I recommend this dlc for everyone who wants to feel like a true teenager

While this expansion has some challenges, overall it is a fun addition for anyone who wants to play their Teen sims.

Beware, however, that there is often a set of teens who go to the high school and adding additional teens around will often fail to get more people in the classes. As a result, the school can feel a bit empty or your high school days can go by without the teens involved in your story ever showing up to your school.

It is a good experience overall but not flawless. I wouldn't pay full price for this but on a decent sale it is worth it.

Plus : the uniform, the new hairstyle with headband, after school activities, amazing to play as teen.
Minus : i think the schooling part is boring.
I really really really need the complete version of Prom Night Track 4🥺🥺🥺

This was my first expansion pack so i guess i'm a bit biased because of that but...It's a really great pack in my opinion, it's great if u want your sim to move out when they are a teenager, i don't even know are the teen jobs only in this pack or not but i love them, i love the fact that i can actually give my teen sims some character since before teenagers we're just children, but after this expansion they really got a lot of interesting stuff like acne and exams and crushes. And event's like prom, sneaking out and highschool graduation.

I looove this expansion, the CAS/buildbuy stuff are good and i always use them when i play. Absolutely would recommend this pack.

Changing my review after some more research. This doesn't add much in game play but you do get a new "world". If you ever have to do a reinstall of the game, and you have mixed DLC from EA and Steam, before allowing Steam DLC to install, make sure all your EA DLC is downloaded and working, then un-install the SIms 4 from steam and then reinstall with steam DLC included. Otherwise you will get unpredictable results with your DLC. When you uninstall, it should keep everything in the folder where steam installs your game, this way it's still working when you reinstall with Steam DLC. In Linux, this is not an issue, but windows might combine the two, it's better to seperate your EA install from your steam install. BTW, Steam DLC only works on steam, not EA.

name: The Sims™ 4 Growing Together Expansion Pack
description: What's Included in this pack About the Game Strengthen family bonds, make friends or enemies, and discover your Sims’ truest selves with The Sims™ 4 Growing Together Expansion Pack. The ways your Sims relate to those around them — from family members to Sims they’ve just met — are evolving. Both planned events like sleepovers and unexpected milestones like having a midlife crisis will shape your Sims and their relationships. KEY FEATURES A Lasting Impact — Key choices and milestones will help to shape who your Sims are. Unlock and change personality traits throughout your Sims’ lives as they cope with midlife crises, respond to family requests to move in, and more. Self-discovery is a lifelong journey. Social Dynamics — Sometimes Sims just click. Your Sim might have natural social chemistry with a Sim they meet at a party — or they might naturally clash. Your Sim will now have preferences that determine which Sims they are socially compatible with and which Sims are more likely to become their enemies. Family Ties — The bonds between your related Sims are important and complex. As they experience life together, there will be opportunities for both drama and unity that will affect family dynamics and Sims’ relationships with their family members. Perfect for Families — Located along an ocean bay, San Sequoia was once a small fishing village and has grown into a bustling town. It features quiet suburbs, a lively rec center, a tight-knit community, and even an influential local family — the Robleses.
short_description: Strengthen family bonds, make friends or enemies, and discover your Sims’ truest selves with The Sims™ 4 Growing Together Expansion Pack.

this game (with its all DLCs) might have more than 1000 bugs, and if so, the list (which you can see in the game's official forums) still keeps growing every Update and DLC release. See official forums so you can have an idea of how many: https://forums.ea.com/category/the-sims-4-en/ideas/the-sims-4-bug-reports-en

Also, this (vanilla) game's community might be one of the most toxic and vain ones. And childish too. I'm supposing this statement based on how the game's DLCs and content are boring and dull when they are based on the community's feedback, compared to very good content created by Sims game modders.

Another thing I'd like to add is that there are too many technical issues not resolved nor responded to by the community moderators or the support team. This also applies to the bugs. The feeling it gives is that there are only players in the forums, because the moderators do not show themselves too much. It looks as if they are not participating in getting bug information to send to the devs, because they rarely answer: so it gives the sensation you are talking to a blank wall when you reach for help. Why not give more content to the game like waves in Sulani so we can surf with Dolphins, fully loaded neighbourhood (or at least a cache system for the current neighbourhood so it's faster to visit a close house) instead of load screen dependant for each single house or apartment, bring cats and dogs a work/career system and make them controllable, add Get To Work version of default game jobs/careers, or start adding a multiplayer system like there's one in the cellphone version? I mean, there are so many fun components that could be added, even if it's a "DLC of a DLC". No, their conclusion to bring more players was: add an F.O.M.O. Timed Event system with some exclusive items that are bonded to your account only if you play during that time.

If they can add the Burglar, they can do a lot more too!

name: The Sims™ 4 Horse Ranch Expansion Pack
description: WHAT'S INCLUDED IN THIS PACK About the Game Work hard and play hard with animal friends and your own stretch of land in The Sims™ 4 Horse Ranch Expansion Pack. Raise, train, and form special bonds with majestic horses that are customizable members of your family. You can also care for mini sheep and mini goats, or make a living selling homemade Nectar. Carve out your own unique life on the ranch. KEY FEATURES: Make a Life — Get up early in the morning, look out at the big open countryside, and know that all your work is worth it. Spend your days keeping your animals happy and clean, harvesting Prairie Grass to feed them, collecting Horse Manure to fertilize your plants, and making your own Nectar to sell. Do it all on a ranch you design, whether that means a traditional cabin or a big red barn. Bond with Animals — Fill your ranch with animal friends and carve out a life together. Your horses – including adorable foals – are part of your household and have lots of special interactions with both Sims and other horses. You can also care for mini goats and mini sheep, and invite neighbors to come see how cute they are. Join a Thriving Community — Living on a ranch has its moments of peace and solitude, but there’s also a lively social scene. Your Sims can go into town to meet friends at the bar, blow off steam at the dance hall, or train at the equestrian park. They can also host events like cookouts and dances on their own property, and hire a ranch hand to help when the work builds up. Raise Horses — Horses are part of the family. Customize hoofed friends in Create a Sim, or get new horses by rescuing or purchasing them. Foals can also be born on your ranch. Sims can learn the Riding skill, and horses can train in the Agility and Jumping skills. Skilled horses and riders can even win awards at the equestrian center.
short_description: Work hard and play hard with animal friends and your own stretch of land in The Sims™ 4 Horse Ranch Expansion Pack.

reviews.review_text: Realy fun, but there should be wild horses.

This expansion pack is good in my opinion. I haven't noticed any bugs so far, and I'm really enjoying it. I'll explain to you whoever is reading this exactly what is good and bad.

Good: 
Littles to no bugs - I have noticed no bugs in my experience and it ran smoothly. So if your looking for a non-bugged horse game (although it isn't a horse game, just the expansion pack) I recommend this game in particular for pc. 
Create your OWN horse - You can create your own horse. Meaning there breed, there color, and if there large or small. You can also name your horse, give them character traits, and make there age! (like any ordinary sim, except an animal!) 
The Horse in General - The horse model is on spot and I can tell it was worked hard on. The animation is also on spot and worked hard on. You can interact, ride, practice barrel racing, practice jumping, and do shows! There is so much to do in this expansion pack. 
Nectar making - I don't have much to say about nectar making. I haven't tried it out yet since it doesn't seem fun, but it might be a different experience for you. 
Mini sheep and goats - Adorable. I recommend getting 3 at the most. The only thing I dislike about the goats and sheep is that they need attention. Every once and a while they need a bath or milk. Thats all for sheep and goats!

I have quite the merrier to say that is bad. The game in general is wonderful and well worked on, its just small details, and some large details, that need to be fixed or worked on more. 
Bad:
Horse "clothing" : Horse clothing is technically a large detail. The options to choose from are slim. There are only 3 saddles, 2 saddle pads, and 5-7 accessories to choose from. I fear this is not very good comparing to everything else in the expansion pack. There should be more saddles and saddle pads. And I forgot to mention bridles. 
 Small lots - Making a barn for your horses requires lots of space, along with the fencing and such. Chestnut Ridge surprisingly does not provide large lots. There may only be 2-4 large enough lots to offer enough space to make your barn along with paddocks and such. And you may think you can just go to a different map, and yes you certainly could, but it is not specially made for horses. For example: There won't be trails or other people riding horses, there won't be shows, and there will most likely be smaller lots depending on the map. You most likely will not be able to combine different activities like gardening. 

Sims 4 in general - Sims 4 doesn't offer enough slots to fit horses. In game, I like to combine the cats and dogs pack with the horses pack because I love that there is a dog around and it suits the vibe. But I can't even have 4 horses or I will reach the limit. 

Overall, this expansion is recommended to horse lovers. I would like to see more in the future like saddle pads and saddles. But otherwise this expansion pack is lovely.
i'm not sure why all the bad reviews! I love having the pet horses in my backyard.

this is by far one of my favorite packs it has so much to offer and has so much for the sims to do from training to competition the clothing and decor is adorable cottage core horse ranch and seasons are  by far my top picks i will never plays sims without these packs they add  so much
i like this dlc cuz i love horses so im biased but horsies r awesome soooo neigh

I bought this when it was 13.99

Its fun and i love it, having a family legacy of show jumpers is engaging and maintaining it through gens and breeding champoins... 

just dont buy it when its not on sale all of their crappy dlcs arnt worth a full game price...
Not enough things to do to be worth $40. It gets repetative after 10 hours if you're lucky
All the horses are missing from the pre made households. They appear only in the thumbnail when you start a new game, but if you enter into CAS they disappear.

As someone who played A LOT (over 10k hours) in Sims 3 and especially played lot with families around horses, this was a disappointment. Nice stable walls to build the stable itself, sheep, goats and nectar business are very positive add, but that's kinda it.

Decoration problem : There is barely any items to decorate stables, way less than in Sims 3 pets DLC. Only western tack, really? In Sims 3 pets expansion pack you could choose a lot to decorate from western or english tack, in general many more decorative items to decorate stables. I mean, this HORSE DLC offers ONE saddle decoration and not A SINGLE bridle decoration. The most common stable stuff is missing! Even the real tack to wear on horses is so lazy, two western saddles, one english saddle, two bridles that's it. 

Gameplay problem: It is almost impossible to level max level with horse, because they always stop training automatically on minimal failings or if they feel even little sad. The horses die of old age before maxing their levels, unless you sweat these spam clicks to force them to keep training and the whole gameplay is based on it. Even still, it is impossible to train then more than one to max. In Sims 3 horses would keep training so long until they were actually super tired. It was enjoyable for the player because you did not need to be there watching every second. 

It is also super lazy how in Sims 4 the horse jumps only one objective, when in Sims 3 you could make nice jumping tracks and there was multiple jumping objects to choose from. Guess how many this game has? 2.. Yep. 

The other annoying this is the lacking communication with horses. I feel there is not much you can do with your horses. You can not lead, you can not do that much to interact either. Yes the basics are there but again that's it. Sims 3 had so much more ways to communicate in general. 

There is nothing where the game evolved from previous game when it comes to this DLC, well except the sheep and goats. I feel like they put more effort in them than in the horses. It is just lazy at this point sorry. 

Just a big downgrade from Sims. Not worth 40bucks. If you're looking a better option for horse gaming, choose Sims 3.

name: The Sims™ 4 Home Chef Hustle Stuff Pack
description: WHAT'S INCLUDED IN THIS PACK About the Game Grow your culinary skills with new, small appliances, and turn cooking into cash with The Sims™ 4 Home Chef Hustle Stuff Pack. Design an efficient kitchen with all the tools you need to become a masterful home chef. Sims will look the part in stylish aprons and other kitchen accessories, and amateur chefs can even turn cooking into a lucrative side hustle with their own food stands. KEY FEATURES: Small Appliances, Big Upgrade — Outfit your kitchen with small appliances like a stand mixer, waffle maker, or countertop pizza oven to expand your culinary horizons. As Sims upgrade their skills, they’ll be able to do more with these new appliances and discover more delicious recipes. Feed the Neighborhood — Turn your love of cooking into a side hustle! Sims can open their own small business with the new food stand and sell tasty treats and mouthwatering meals to neighbors. Stands can be named and customized to reflect your Sim and the kind of food they choose to serve. Fresh Chef Styles — Your cooking adventure wouldn’t be complete without the right fashion. Kitchen-ready hairstyles, food-themed accessories, and aprons for the whole family will have your Sims looking like serious amateur chefs.
short_description: Grow your culinary skills with new small appliances and turn cooking into cash with The Sims™ 4 Home Chef Hustle Stuff Pack.

name: The Sims™ 4 For Rent Expansion Pack
description: WHAT'S INCLUDED IN THIS PACK About the Game Build Residential Rental lots in any livable World with up to 6 units per lot and households of up to 8 Sims per unit. Choose to live as a tenant or property owner in The Sims™ 4 For Rent Expansion Pack. Your Sim can now own and manage multiple Residential Rental lots or move into a home where they’re closer to their neighbors than ever (perfect for snooping!). From potlucks to evictions, there’s a lot to do and a bustling new world to do it in. KEY FEATURES: New Lot Type — New Residential Rental lots give you the chance to get creative with multi-family dwellings. Give your Sims a basement suite to rent out, a duplex to keep 2 families close, townhomes for multiple families, or even build apartments of up to 6 units (or more if you know the cheat). Each Residential Rental can be customized to your specifications. Property Management — Property owner Sims can be present property managers, living among their tenants, or they can live in a separate residence while generating income from several property investments. If your Sim doesn’t want to manage the whole property, then perhaps the new Handyperson Career might be better suited to them. Property owners should keep their Unit Ratings high because, if they don’t, they could face consequences like a Tenant Revolt. Closer Than Ever — Sims in Residential Rentals will inevitably get tangled up in each other’s lives. They’ll meet with other tenants at Social Events like a community potluck, in the garden, or other common areas at their Residential Rental, and have ample opportunity to discover their neighbor’s secrets by eavesdropping, snooping, or even breaking and entering. If you discover something juicy, you can blackmail other Sims for a bribe, too! Bustling and Beautiful — One of the most iconic scenes Tomarang has to offer is the Night Market where your Sims will savor new foods and add unique objects such as the pressure cooker and electric kettle to their homes. As you explore the world, you might even see children playing new games of marbles and hopscotch or discover the 4 new traits and 4 new aspirations unique to For Rent.
short_description: Bring the neighbors together — literally — and experience multi-family lots as a tenant or property owner in The Sims™ 4 For Rent Expansion Pack.

love this im still finding more things but i love it

I should of listened to the reviews, definitely crashes out often. I've restarted the game more than I've actually played on the expansion. Hope this is something that they'd fixed based off of others experiences.

WORST EXPANSION PACK EVER.
Highly recommend avoiding this sims 4 expansion pack purchase. I have played sims for a LONG time now and I have never tried to refund a DLC until this one... Every expansion pack adds some sort of useful depth to your gaming experience no matter what you prioritize (building, creating sims, etc.) yet this expansion pack felt more like a game pack that just allows me to separate individual people in a home. The furniture in the pack is mediocre, as well as the CAS items. Never been more disappointed with a sims 4 DLC until this one. I would literally buy ANY other pack in the game over this buggy mess that cost me $43!!!!!!

name: The Sims™ 4 Crystal Creations Stuff Pack
description: What's included in this pack About the Game Craft dazzling jewelry and give your creations a spark of power with The Sims™ 4 Crystal Creations Stuff Pack. Engage with a new skill that will have your Sim collecting materials and customizing pieces to wear, gift, or sell. These gems don’t just look radiant — when charged in the moonlight, they can affect Sims’ moods, relationships, careers, and lives! Jewelry By You — Fill your collection with colorful new crystals and metals that can be used to create sparkling jewelry. Aspiring jewelers can build up their new skill by using the Gemology Table to cut crystals and get creative with jewelry customization. All Charged Up — Search out crystals or pluck them off the Crystal Tree and get ready to supercharge! Charged crystals will affect Sims in a variety of ways, like altering their moods or even helping with their careers. Dark & Dreamy — Pair your shining creations with hints of mystery and intrigue. The clothing and furniture in this pack is perfect for a cozy workroom. There’s even a set of dreamy jewelry created in collaboration with Simmer Arethabee, available in CAS.
short_description: Craft dazzling jewelry and give your creations a spark of power with The Sims™ 4 Crystal Creations Stuff Pack.

name: The Sims™ 4 Lovestruck Expansion Pack
description: What's included in this pack About the Game Explore romance as you match with alluring Sims and plan exciting dates in The Sims™ 4 Lovestruck Expansion Pack. Create your Sim’s profile for the Cupid’s Corner dating app, and start meeting matches! New intimate interactions, like cuddling in bed or meeting for a seductive dance, are waiting. Whether your dynamic ends up steamy or strained will depend on what your Sim finds attractive. Pay special attention to those Turn-Ons and Turn-Offs! Plan Hotter Dates — Check the Cupid’s Corner app for matches that make your Sim’s heart flutter. Then, set up a date with the new date-planning system. You’ll choose where to go and what to do, crafting romantic rendezvous that’ll leave Sims swooning. Sims can connect by playing games, cuddling in bed, or taking the Romantic Blanket to a beautiful spot for a special moment. All About Attraction — What works for your Sim? Turn-Ons and Turn-Offs will determine how attracted Sims are to each other. They can include interests, interactions, and more! Discover these as your Sims get to know their partners, build relationships, and maintain Romantic Satisfaction. Since Sims will like and dislike different interactions, partners might have different satisfaction levels. How Sims interact with each other will also affect whether their Relationship Dynamic is wholesome, steamy, strained, or unpredictable. For Lovers of Love — Romance isn’t just a word; it’s a skill that unlocks seductive interactions. For Sims with romantic aspirations, romance is a way of life. Truly hopeless romantics can even become Romance Consultants and make a career out of loving love. As they rise in their career, they’ll earn new ways to promote love and maybe find some cute heart-shaped furniture in their inventories. Date Nights in an Unforgettable City — Fall in love with a gorgeous city made for romance. Ciudad Enamorada has 3 neighborhoods that exude charm with plenty of places for Sims to meet, flirt, and even WooHoo. There’s even a new Singles Hangout lot trait that’ll attract available Sims to come and mingle.
short_description: Meet your match.

I give the pack a 8\10

name: The Sims™ 4 Businesses & Hobbies Expansion Pack
description: WHAT'S INCLUDED IN THIS PACK About the Game Pursue new skills and live a life of accomplishment in The Sims 4 Businesses & Hobbies Expansion Pack*. Make your home in Nordhaven — a world of opportunity where you can chase your dreams among a passionate community. Create wearable art with the new Tattooing skill, and even earn Simoleons for it. Or, craft functional pieces with the new Pottery skill and charge for lessons as you improve. Savvy Sims can turn their wildest dreams into profitable businesses, so use your imagination to create something special. Run your business out of your home or purchase a Small Business Venue lot, hire employees, decide how to charge, and make the rules! KEY FEATURES Open Your Dream Business — Dream big and create a small business that will leave your Sim feeling fulfilled. Make and sell pottery or other crafts, provide a service like tattoos or classes, or come up with something unique. Use the selection tool to designate space in your home for doing what you love – and making a living at it – or purchase a new Small Business Venue Lot. With the new Ticket Kiosk you can run the business you want, charging customers for entry or for time spent. Run a family business and hire your Sim’s household, or meet the perfect candidate and offer them a job. Explore the possibilities with the new Small Business system. Make Your Mark — With the new Tattooing skill you can give Sims pre-made, custom, or dedication tattoos. As you level up your skill you’ll unlock more stamps and stencils, and increase the odds of excellent results. Body art is about freedom of expression and to celebrate that you can now freely create your own designs in Tattoo Paint Mode. Tattoos can even be layered. Share your custom Tattoo designs with other Simmers using the Gallery, or browse for Tattoos to use in your game. Mold Your Future — As you hone your Pottery skill using the Pottery Wheel, more designs – like functional tea sets, planters, incense burners, and more – will become available to you. Finish your pieces in the Pottery Kiln; there are plenty of options for glazes. At higher levels of the Pottery skill, Sims can use the Japanese art of Kintsugi to create beautiful pieces from broken ceramics. A World of Possibility — Build your future in a town steeped in history. Nordhaven is the perfect place to pursue the things you care most about, like creative and inspiring hobbies. Sims can attend Meetups to mix and mingle with those who are passionate about the same hobbies – they may even find someone to mentor them in their favorite skill. Sims can become mentors at high skill levels, or they can build their legacy by hosting Classes and Lectures. Listening to a Lecture is one of the ways a Sim may earn Mastery Perks. Hobbies are best when shared, so explore the new neighborhoods of Gammelvik and Iverstad to meet like-minded Sims! Your Business Your Way — You can run your business by the books, or cut a few corners, and as you build your Business Reputation, you’ll earn Business Perks along Dreamer, Schemer, and Neutral routes. You don’t have to pick just one! Sims with the new Idealist trait might prefer to run Dreamer businesses, whereas Shady Sims may enjoy being Schemers. These choices could impact Sims’ lives outside of their businesses.
short_description: Pursue new skills and live a life of accomplishment in The Sims™ 4 Businesses & Hobbies Expansion Pack*.

reviews.review_text:
While the name of this DLC is 'Businesses & Hobbies', this is really the 'Home Business' DLC. There isn't much about hobbies at all.

In this DLC, you can have a Sim start a business on their home lot. No need to buy a separate lot for the business.
Each Appropriately aged Sim can have their own business, so households with multiple sims can each have their own business on the same lot, although only one of the businesses can be open at a time.

Household members can be both owners of their own business and employees of the other household businesses at the same time.

In my experience, home businesses work best when providing a service/activity instead of a product (i.e. a bowling alley, a gym, a spa, etc).

Customer Sims are pretty good about doing the tasks the business is setup for, but they don't do them in any logical order and are not good at cleaning up after themselves. For example in a laundromat business Sims may leave their laundry. Or at a cafe they may order a drink, immediately set it down to do something else (thus forgetting they had it) and then go back and order another drink.

One of the businesses I made was a Reading Cafe with bookshelves, places to sit, and one of the new cafe counters that sells drinks and snacks. Customer Sims would forget their fresh drinks, leave books all over in random places instead of returning them to the bookshelves, and when the shop closed they would often walk off with the book they were holding. Eventually I had no more books on the bookshelves and had to order more.

You can sell items, but you don't have great control over the pricing (some items have default prices that are too low) and there is no restock option. If you want to make a retail store you are probably better off with the 'Get To Work' DLC.
Amazing, I love this DLC
It's ok

by far, this is the actually worth the money I would recommend this fully its literally the only dlc thats reallly worth it. If i had to list the things i like its the fact I could get a lot of money I dont even have to use my business degree nor logic when i open up a business. at first u will have to waste a lot of money but once you have an actual business up and running aka leave the business open and earn money that way from unsuspecting simmers they you got so much money. I could open one up and and make the guests wonder outside near the trashcans and theyll still pay for entry. Theres many non-logical things surrounding this pack but it works great when I want to make a quick buck. But omg, its so ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ annoying when my employees dont do ♥♥♥♥ for the entire day, eachtime i open up a restaurant or any small business idea theyre literally not doing ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥. I also think it could be bettered maybe adding a few things but sims will never actually make their game better and hear our the fanbase and will rely on the work of modders.

This pack is great if you want to run a business, and makes the Get to Work retail system near completely irrelevant. A small business is also a better way to run a food establishment than Dine Out's notoriously glitchy restaurants (though they will work more like a buffet restaurant). This pack combines exceptionally well with the activities from most other packs, to make highly varied businesses.

However, note that small businesses are designed entirely for your played Sims to run them. While you can set up businesses to visit with your active sims, the process is very awkward as you have to set up an entire separate household through live mode (/gameplay). Small business ownership, and thus ranks and perks and activities, DO NOT get saved to the gallery. So you must set up every small business every time in a new save, rather than being able to simply drop something in from the gallery and go as you can do with most other systems. So using small businesses as a customer isn't very good unless you have one or two specific businesses you want to focus on visiting frequently, or unless you're playing rotationally anyways and it's simply a way for your various households to interact with each other. 

I'm not personally a fan of the BB or CAS, but it's good quality models and textures if you like its styles. The pottery skill has a lot of interactive pieces you can make, like planter pots and incense/oil burners, so it's not just decorative clutter. I really like the pottery skill and what you can make, but I do wish the glaze options had better variety, because they're all very light/pastel colors. 

The custom tattoos allow incredible customization of sims, however there's also glitches if you use too many of them in a save (and the number is something stupidly low like 10), so I have not really felt like I can enjoy this feature since the game has so many issues that I don't want to make it even more unstable by using something that's a known issue.
I like the old thumbnails, and it makes my normally-balanced ocd go nuts. When I see it in the menus.
breaks "paint by reference" - turns the canvas blank. Not fixed by latest patch of "150 fixes". Bummer.

Out of all of the "expansion" packs this is the most useless.  It seems like EA was so dry of content they put this together and just hoped the money would roll in.  There is no new content, and what is added is just not fun at all.  Small businesses take too much money to operate and it's a system that just doesn't work.  Save your money.  Not even the build mode stuff is worth it.

Don't do it! 
While in theory the concept seems delightful and the teasers look fun, this expansion pack is problematic. Sure, some neat new skills to learn, but is it worth it when small businesses sometimes don't show without going into build mode, then back into play mode? Hired employees don't show up to work or don't show up as hired in the 'manage employees" menu, don't show up to work but can neither be fired nor quit, small businesses are opened and can't be closed, staying open for thousands of hours as the exhausted Sim small business owners work themselves to death... this one is frustratingly buggified.

Nothing new, only more bugs, do not buy , unless you want your save files corrupted.
Tattoos black out. My werewolves and vampires are unplayable now.

name: The Sims™ 4 Enchanted by Nature Expansion Pack
description: WHAT'S INCLUDED IN THIS PACK About the Game Live surrounded by the magic of nature in The Sims™ 4 Enchanted by Nature Expansion Pack*. Embrace a free-spirited lifestyle where you Forage and sleep under the stars, or bring nature’s gifts inside with versatile gardening pots. A charming world is waiting where you can craft Elixirs, cure Ailments, meet Mother Nature herself, and even become a Fairy! KEY FEATURES: Life Among the Trees — Step outside and make yourself at home in nature. With the Natural Living Skill, Sims can meet their needs outdoors without beds or plumbing! The natural world has a lot to offer, and Sims can Forage to uncover what’s hidden. Find everything from crystals to frogs to food! This way of life is perfect for Sims with the new Nature Nomad Aspiration. Sims can even bring the great outdoors inside with new pots for gardening on tables, counters, shelves, and more. The 3 new traits — Plant Lover, Mystical, and Disruptive — play a role in how Sims relate to their world. The Fairest Folk — Do you feel nature’s mystical pull? Embark on a special journey and become a Fairy! Fairies can develop abilities that let them nurture plants, change the moods of others, and a whole lot more. The new Fairy Stories Aspiration will have you choosing between Harmonious and Discordant Fairy paths. Create Fairy Sims with wondrous wings and make them unique by painting! Plus, add whimsical accessories, glowing tattoos, and more. Non-Fairy Sims can also have magical fun by using Fairy Dust to enchant plants, bring Gnomes to life, and more. A Balanced Lifestyle — Prolonged negative emotions cause imbalance, and imbalance can lead to Ailments with strange effects. They aren’t always bad (maybe you like lots of Gnomes at your house!), but if you want a Cure, you can turn to the new Apothecary Table and hone your Apothecary Skill. There’s a Cure for every one of the 8 Ailments, plus 14 more recipes with other fantastical effects. Sims who enjoy the Apothecary skill can become Naturopaths, a new 10-level career where you can cure Sims at home. They may also take on the Elixir Enthusiast Aspiration. Make sure to watch your Luck, which affects everything from cooking well to developing Ailments. A Land of Enchantment — Innisgreen is a beautifully lush, green world where Sims live in harmony with nature. There are 3 neighborhoods: a quiet waterfront town called Coast of Adhmor where they’re all about Gnomes, Sprucederry Grove where nature and civilization blend together, and the magical forest of Everdew. From meeting Mother Nature and starting your journey towards fairydom to using the Glaswood Tree statue to summon Spruce Almighty, spectacular adventures are waiting!
short_description: Live surrounded by the magic of nature in The Sims™ 4 Enchanted by Nature Expansion Pack*.

reviews.review_text:
Loved this expansion pack! 
This pack may not appeal to every sims 4 player out there, but if you love fantasy and drama (similar to the werewolf and spellcaster game-packs) then this expansion pack is for you! The addition of Fairies as an occult is primarily fun if you have other occult species in the game. This is due to their unique interactions with each specific occult, which is a new feature with this pack. (ex: fairies can calm werewolves down to prevent transformation) Additionally, I would highly recommend this pack for those that love nature builds because the furniture in this pack is AMAZING. 
Hope this helps reveal a bit of the magic in this pack. Definitely one of the top expansions in my book.

Love this pack. Fairies are my favorite occult type. The apothecary skill is perfect for one of my sim's storylines. The ailments can be slightly annoying at times but not too bad. The world, CAS, and build items that came with it are great. I do agree it might've been better as a stuff pack over a full expansion pack, I enjoy it very much. Don't buy at full price though. As a principle, try not to buy any of the expansion packs at full price if you can help it

It's a big pack with lots to learn. I was slow to start, but once I teamed my fairy with a spell caster, it rapidly came together, I recommend this DLC, and am learning the rest of the content about natural living (which is independent from the occult fairies... I was confused for a while).  
EA had some creative ideas for this one.

Not too bad, though I do wish there was more interactions incorporating the fairies' small form, like the Sims 3's Fairy Frolic and Playful Pester interactions. Also, I would think that if you give a sharp teeth option for fairies that you'd give them a mean or mischief interaction allowing them to bite.
This should have been way more expensive

I forgot to return it and haven't played since i bought it, I am very annoyed you can only return it after fourteen days but I understand, It is very laggy, and isn't worth the money at all in my opinion.
AWFUL JUST AWFUL!!!!!!!!!!! CAN'T GET IT TO WORK EVEN THOUGH I LITERALLY JUST PAID FOR THE DAMN THING!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!! FIX IT NOW OR I WANT MY MONEY BACK!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

After adding this expansion pack my characters constantly freeze in place I don't have mods in my pc and I had started a whole new game just in case it would not work well with the other expansions and a pre existing game. I really love playing the fairies but when you have to keep restarting and re verifying the game files it becomes tedious and a waste of money wait and see if they ever fix the expansion

Would not recommend. Ever since the "expansion" came out I have been unable to play the Innisgreen world, period. It white screens and does not finish rendering and I am forced to restart the game just to access the other worlds. I have tried reinstalling, rolling back to DXR9, clearing cache, removing any and all mods but this map is unplayable and its frustrating because I get zero access to the content outside CAS and BB if in another world. I wanted so badly to play the map and quests and build in this map and I can't and I cant just remove the pack from my DLCs because the game automatically tries re-adding it! EA seriously needs to fix the bug on this one because I paid for an unusable map and it made me quit playing for months out of frustration and praying they would fix it. But no, today it still white screens and refuses to render/load. I cant eve n blindly click a lot to force it open... I wish I could make a proper review about the expansion but I just cant. this is BS and I hate it

All Sims games are broken - we all know that - but I've not been plagued with too many issues. A lot of that is down to reading the reviews people leave and avoiding those add-ons that cause people big problems. This expansion has been the one that has given me trouble. 

Fairies were my favourite class in Sims 3, so I had always wanted them in Sims 4. It's just sad that this expansion has ruined my entire game.

As many have said before, the ailments system is awful and it just upends game balance. You can cure one ailment and it's immediately replaced with another one. This is bad enough in a fairy family, but even worse in a non-fairy family - especially a new family with very little money. Of them all, the gnome ailment is the worst. If you have seasons, you'll know how annoying the Harvestfest gnomes are. Well this ailment fills your house with gnomes destroying everything every single day, rebreaking things faster than an entire family of spellcasters can repeatedly cast Repario all day. And yes, I have the less ailments mod running. 

Fairies themselves seem to spend more time dealing with their needs than any other class. 

All of this might be bearable, if it wasn't so broken. Everything I try to interact with now is bugged. Sims stand and do nothing; cancel actions; or wave their hands in the air unable to find a path. It doesn't matter that these things worked before - they just don't work now. I now have to repeatedly use UI Cheats to debug items and sims, but this fix only works around 2 in 10 times and it always worked before. Invariably, I now have to sell the buggy item and buy it again, but even that isn't a guarantee. It also makes upgrading things pointless as the second time you try to use it, it's bugged again. 

And then there's the crashing. Since adding this expansion, I can expect to crash at least once every couple of hours. It's an annoying crash in that I can still pan around the screen and sims are doing their idle animations, but everything else is shut down. You can't click on things or sims and get options. You can't save and you can't quit.

Usually, with bad Sims DLC, the advice is to wait until it's on sale. With Enchanted by Nature, no matter how much you want fairies in your game, the advice is, don't buy it unless EA fixes it - which they most likely never will. Should they fix it though, remember that it will still throw your game out of balance and make the entire game unbearable, so the advice is still... don't buy it at all.. 

In conclusion then, only buy this expansion if you are a masochist who loves game crashes; who is thrilled at the idea of every item and sim in the game being bugged to the point where the best bug-fixing mod can't even help you; and you dream of every sim being forever infected by ailments.

And STILL wait for a sale.

name: The Sims™ 4 Get To Work
description: What's included in this pack About the Game You rule the workplace with The Sims™ 4 Get to Work. Actively control your Sims while they’re on the job, and determine whether they're headed toward a big promotion or becoming the workplace menace. Jump to the rescue and save countless lives as a doctor, torment your neighbors with mischievous inventions as a scientist, or investigate crime scenes and crack the big cases as a detective. If you’d rather be your own boss, you can even create, customize, and manage your own retail businesses and become a Simoleonaire! You decide how your Sims get to work. Become a doctor, detective, or scientist — Control your Sims in all-new career venues. Use your analytical mastermind to create crazy inventions as a scientist; see what secrets you uncover as you investigate crime scenes as a detective; or treat patients, deliver babies, and perform emergency surgery as a doctor. Create unique retail businesses — You rule your business! Create any type of retail business you desire, including bakeries, clothing boutiques, art galleries, bookstores, and many more. Customize every facet of your business with Build Mode, and watch the Simoleons roll in! Select what items to sell, manage your employees as you see fit, and schmooze your customers to close the sale. Learn new skills — Teach your Sims some new skills to create more unique objects to enjoy in The Sims 4. Capture the picture-perfect photo or bake and decorate a delicious treat with the new Photography and Baking skills. Display your creations at home or sell them to earn some extra Simoleons!
short_description: Become a daring doctor, fearless detective, mad scientist, or create your own retail business and become a Simoleonaire with The Sims 4 Get to Work.

the game will always prompt  error to load game once i get to a certain career progress. i cant load the world or continue the household. i never get to progress till the highest career level

I would recommmoned the pack, but ONLY if it's on sale. It's really not worth anything more than $25

It allows you to go to work with your sims, own a business (you can own a vet with Cats & Dogs!), and more drama!
