I like the gameplay loop, but I wish there were more mechanics in the game. Finished it quickly, but after I got to lvl 20 (max level) I had no desire to continue playing.

I loved this game and played almost daily over the last 3 weeks. I log on today and find out my game reverted to 15 days ago. I tried recovering the save files which is how i found out it was last saved on 3/31/26. We are now in 4/15/26 and I'm very sad. It says I've played 17 hours - it really has been closer to 45. Please fix this before other people have their stores DESTROYED! :(

"No" because it's not a simulator by any means, not even close. Practically false advertising.

Yes, it's fine IF you want a mindless, monotonous, repetitive, cozy game with literally no way to fail or play incorrectly aside from removing all movies from your store and losing employees because you can't pay them because you have no sales and no money. If you want a chill-out busywork game, then yes it's fine. If you want an actual store simulator / management game, then check out "The Walking Trade". Better game, more depth, more content, half the price, and significantly better grind, more agency, actual store / employee management, as well as being less monotonous.

[b]Lack of Agency and Control[/b]

Because this game is not an actual simulator, you have very little control. Your influence begins and ends at expanding the store, organizing the store, hiring employees (eventually), and buying and selling movies. You have no control over the prices, your existence is moving VHS tapes. Not managing employees, not influencing prices, customers, spending habits, etc. You put movie on shelf, movie goes to register. You can only eventually have a max of 2 employees that have static, random stats upon hiring. They never improve in any way at all, ever, and there is no employee shifts or roster. These same 2 employees will work 14-16 hour days, 7 days a week, without fail. On Sundays they only have to work 12 hour days, as a treat. Again, you have no control. You can't tell customers to leave, you can't even end the day if customers are in the store. 

[b]Grind and Repetition[/b]

The game is very grindy. There's 20 levels, and each level you unlock something new. New movie genre, new equipment, etc. At level 6 you can get your first employee, at level 20 you can get your second. Each "day" takes about 10-15 minutes, depending on how late your store is open that day (which you can't control), and each level takes about 3 days, so about ~30-45 mins per level. The goal of games like this is to maximize profit and ultimately achieve automation, but you can only achieve a high degree of automation at the end of the game when you're pretty much done with it. 

The game is simple and repetitive. Stock movies, rent movies, check in returned movies. It gets very repetitive and monotonous, but that can be a good or bad thing depending on what you're looking for.

[b]Employees[/b]

As mentioned above, you can only have 2 employees. Your 1st one is at level 6, your 2nd one is at level 20. The small, random selection of employees is hirable each day, each one demanding different daily wages. The more competent they are (faster work speeds, more passive skills), the higher wage they will demand. They have 2 skills, "Checkout" and "Return Station" that indicate the speed of their assigned roles. They range from different speeds:  Slow, Average, Good, and Fast. The employees are completely static, and experience no growth, no improvement in their skills or duties, or have any sort of rapport or morale management. Sometimes they ask for raises, you decline it, and there's a very small chance they quit. Most of the time they go "dang" and go back to their wage cage to work their 102 hour work week at the VHS mines.

Employees are also frustrating. Randomly they'll just fall asleep and you have to wake them up, or the customer will be literally handing the employee their money, and the employee goes "I gotta use the bathroom", then they'll proceed to ♥♥♥♥ their brains out for the next hour or two (in-game time.)

You can tell employees to work faster, which has a chance to cause them to quit if they don't have the trait to prevent that. Basically it makes them work at a speed that's 1 tier above their normal. "Average" will be "Good" temporarily, and so on. A fast cashier that you told to work faster will check out at a speed that's close to the player's checkout speed. Meanwhile, the one doing returns, even if they're "fast" and told to work faster, they are still ABYSMALLY slow. For a player, it takes about a second or less to scan each movie, and you can hold up to 10 in your hand. The employee, when fast and speed-boosted, still takes about 2 seconds per movie and can hold up to 5. When you get later in the game and you have more and more movies to return, if you don't help them, it will literally take all day (in-game) and then some to work through that day's returns. Even while writing this, I had my fast (mostly un-boosted) employee do returns and it took him about 30+ real minutes.

[b]Customers[/b]

Customers are incredibly basic. There's no management or anything regarding them. If they're in your store, you're guaranteed a sale and they will wait indefinitely until they've checked out. The only way to actually influence customers is the amount of movies and shelves in your store. More movies = more customers. Nothing else seems to matter. I tested this by removing the shelves (movies will stay on the shelves when you pack them up), to see if I could try and force customers to only buy limited edition, adult, and new release movies because they cost the most. All that did with drop customer traffic to almost nothing until I re-installed the shelves.

You can use fliers to drive customers into the store, but it's basically a 50/50 chance of them saying yes and going to buy something, or no and walking away. Ultimately this feels like a placebo effect, because it seems like all it does is immediately force customers in the store that would've gone in later, so ultimately there's no tangible benefit or change to overall sales.

[b]Store Management[/b]

As you level up, you will unlock things to help boost sales. Concession machines like popcorn, slushies, and cotton candy. Arcade machines, gumball machines, drink fridges, shelves, and a clearance bin where you can sell movies for 50% of their listed market value. Ultimately, as the game goes on, you feel little tangible progress financially. You make about about $200-$250 average per day, throughout the game, from near beginning to end. At the end, you might see $400 days, but while you make slightly more money, your employees (especially when you get 2) eat into that, so you're just making slightly more than when you started. 

[b]Conclusion[/b]

Skip this game if you want an actual management game. If you want a safe, cozy, simple busywork game, then you might be interested. The game is entertaining enough, but it's not what I was wanting or expecting. It's only a management game in the most superficial, minute sense of the word. You exist to yell at your employees, do their job for them, and put movies on shelves and that's about it. 

2/10 "Simulator". It BARELY qualifies as such.

3/10 price. This is a $5-$10 game marked as a ~$20 game, that's how little there is to this game. The slog-like grind just keeps you playing longer than you normally might and compensates for lack of actual content or mechanics or... anything, really. Once I got to level 20 and effectively beat the game, just to see how the game would change (if at all), I put it down and have no desire to return to it. It's a "one and done" type game.

6/10 Overall as a general "game." It doesn't offer much, but what it does offer is a sort of simple "turn brain off and play" experience, which I think it does well in that regard.  It's a mindless, reasonably fun, simple game. It has it's place.

EDIT TO REVIEW: 
Road map has just been announced, which would add a lot of features that I think would give the game a lot more depth. So while it's a full game, updates are coming out as if it is early access. Let it cook and come back in 6 months.

----

This is a "maybe" that I'm making a "no" because the game is overwhelmingly positive and it can take the hit from 1 bad review. 

This game is largely carried by nostalgia, and your enjoyment heavily depends on that. I liked blockbuster, but I was never like "heck yeah, let's go hang out at the sticky carpet store and find a video game". Gameplay-wise, it's a slog. There's 20 levels total. You need to be level 6 to hire one employee, and then 20 to hire the other. By level 6, you desperately want a staff member. It's a grind and there's a lot of monotony. You rewind and stock the shelves on a morning, open, serve people on the till (there's always a queue) and answer requests between people on the till and then you close, restock the shelves, finish the day. That's the loop. They do events and weather that kind of spices it up, but there's not much change. You never have enough money for shelves, and you never have enough tapes when you have shelves. It's a constant resource juggle. 

"You can decorate the shop though!" - yes, but it's painful. You have to paint small sections of wall at a time, and eventually I just gave up and left it because there was no "cover all" option. The decorations also aren't amazing for choice, and I found myself going "why would I bother with a life-sized suit of armour when I could buy another shelf?" constantly.

A few nit picks: Options menu is a bit weird. Stuff doesn't save when you save it and it reverts constantly and doesn't save on a fresh launch. Also the currency option for the till is pointless - all it does is change the $ to a £, but still uses $1 bills and 25c coins. Also this setting reverts itself mid game. The physics is also busted, and you will lose tapes and customers to the abyss frequently. 

I played 33 hours and got all the achievements, but about 15 hours of that were afk doing something else. Speaking of AFKing - that is the gameplay. After about level 10, I would start the day, stand on the counter so I could see the full shop, and then go AFK on another window while my one employee served people until the timer ran out, and then I'd put the employee on returns after the shop was closed. I was largely stood there. There is not much going on in this game at all, honestly. 

A friend even asked me if they should get it, and I was about to say "yes but wait until it's out of early access" and checked the steam page quickly for a road map on that - and I was surprised to see it is a complete game, because it feels very early access. There is not £16 worth of game here and it's not a finished game. A majority of the achievements felt like padding to make you play longer, because you reach level 20 in about 10 hours. 

Maybe it's the fact I've just finished esoteric ebb which was so incredible the next was never going to live up to it, maybe I'm just used to games like TCG having more, or maybe I've been spoiled by Rewind 99 - a similar concept game - just doing this whole movie rental idea better by letting you walk around a town and have side hustles. It's fine if you have the money spare and you want to watch youtube while you play, but I wouldn't break your bank rushing to play it without a heavy discount on it.

Started out fun, but the gameplay loop quickly got repetitive.  Even with upgrades like getting slushy machines and clearance bins found not too much changing day to day.  Got bored and put down after a little more than a day of playing.

Set out on a truly global aviation adventure with virtually unlimited missions across the planet. Fly head-to-head in the iconic Red Bull Air Races and the legendary Reno Air Races, including the new Roswell courses. Be a World Photographer and travel across our beautiful planet to capture awe-inspiring natural vistas, visit famous landmarks, and record these adventures with pictures in your travel book. Unique photo challenges test your creative eye along with your piloting skills while you strive to capture the perfect moment. Advanced Simulation Enhanced physics system allows 10,000+ rigid-body surfaces that enable the simulation of any shape of aircraft. Soft body physics supports cloth, ropes, balloons, and more. Improved ground and water handling further enhance realism. New, highly accurate aircraft systems including electrical, pneumatic, fuel and hydraulic systems, payload and passenger systems, and avionics like the Universal UNS-1 FMS and Primus Epic 2. Preflight inspections and walkaround checks add to immersion. Plan your flights with a groundbreaking flight planner which supports both IFR and VFR map layers, IFR charts, route planning, fuel and payload planning, vertical profile planning, and ETOPS planning. It also provides airport information including weather and NOTAMS and is available native in the sim, on mobile devices or web browsers for flight planning outside the simulator. Explore Our Visually Stunning Digital Twin Explore our most detailed re-creation of planet Earth to date. Enhanced digital elevation maps, over 500 TIN (triangular irregular network) cities and more than 100,000 square kilometers of countryside photogrammetry allow for visually stunning digital twin experiences. More than 150 airports, 2,000 glider airports, 10,000 heliports, 2,000 points of interest, and 900 oil rigs have been carefully hand-crafted while a procedural system generates all 40,000 airports, 80,000 helipads, 1.5 billion buildings, and nearly 3 trillion trees our planet. Land anywhere and, for the first time in Microsoft Flight Simulator, exit your aircraft to explore 27 highly detailed biomes with hundreds of species of vegetation, dynamically created details including grass, rocks, and flowers – all influenced by seasonal changes. The all-new photometric lighting system renders the world more accurately than ever before, and enhanced weather features such as new cloud types, auroras, and other weather phenomena produce fresh and compelling atmospheric experiences. The world comes alive with a vast array of land-based wildlife in their natural habitats and free-ranging livestock across the entire globe. The oceans and waterways are teeming with live, worldwide maritime traffic ranging from large tankers and cargo ships to trawlers, tugs, and more. A richly diverse, regionally accurate representation of humanity brings life to the airports, aircraft, and passenger cabins of the world. Enhanced real-world air traffic populates the skies and airport tarmacs with hundreds of accurately depicted aircraft models and dozens of officially licensed liveries. name: Gray Zone Warfare
description: Just Updated Update 0.4 introduces sweeping changes across nearly every part of the game, including major reworks, core system rebalancing, improved clarity, and expanded sandbox features. This update is designed to give players more agency and deliver a stronger overall experience. We focused on improving the core gameplay loop, making combat easier to read, and finding a better balance between realism and satisfying game feel. Step into Lamang, now richer, more dynamic, and more interactive than ever before, with more opportunities to explore, loot, and experiment with an expanded arsenal of weapons and gear. Key features: Redesigned the task system to create a replayable progression loop 100+ new Tasks and Contracts, plus 50+ updated tasks, bringing the total to more than 250 25+ new locations Rebuilt and polished existing locations Expanded lore 8 new weapons, 380+ weapon parts, and 150+ gear pieces AI improvements Revamped loot system Redesigned Tactical Map Reworked vendor stocks and economy Rebalanced ballistics Reworked core movement animations Refactored sound design Rebalanced health system New onboarding area and tutorial New Field Manual New Player Statistics HUD and controls improvements Visual polish Lighting improvements Technical improvements About the Game
short_description: Enter a high-stakes PvE-first open-world tactical shooter set on a quarantined Southeast Asian island torn apart by faction warfare. Deploy as an elite operator, fight solo or with your squad to carry out missions, earn reputation, and explore the truth behind the Event igniting the conflict.

reviews.review_text: Only at the beginning but this game is amazing. Combat is realistic, vast options of weapons, ammo, gear. Only played the PvE so far but thoroughly enjoying myself and it's even better with friends. The immersion factor is definitely there and I can already feel myself getting lost in this game for hours without even noticing. Be ready to use smart combat tactics, lots of fun gun play, beautiful graphics and weather that appears to effect your shots. So plan accordingly, don't forget to take in your medical supplies, food and water, keep your head on a swivel and dive into immersive guerilla warfare gameplay

It is in pre-alpha right now so there might be bugs and a few unbalance changes but it is still a very good gameplay loops, both PVP (for high intensity) and PVE (relaxing tactical dress up shooter). It is worth watching.

[b]TLDR: It's worth it.[/b]

If you're a new player thinking about jumping into Gray Zone Warfare, update 0.4 is a good time to dive in. I went in this update worried that an early access extraction shooter might feel half baked and not worth the money, but after dozens of hours both solo and with a squad, I can confidently say the game has hooked me hard. It's the rare title that actually delivers a blending Escape from Tarkov's tense looting and extraction, Ghost Recon's tactical squad play, and DayZ's open world survival chaos into one addictive package.

The core gameplay loop is where it shines right now, and feels rewarding thanks to the 0.4 overhaul. You load into a massive, beautifully designed map full of unique locations and layered points of interest - dense jungles, abandoned villages, fortified outposts, and sprawling industrial zones that, while reusing assets like any game does, the locations feel hand crafted rather than copy pasta. You pick your faction, hop in a chopper, and get dropped at a landing zone (LZ). From there it's fun time: you sprint, sneak, or fight your way through the world completing tasks, looting gear, hunting AI, and scavenging everything from basic meds to high tier weapons. The map rewards exploration—side paths, hidden caches, and verticality keep you on your toes. Once you've filled your bags the tension ramps up as you push toward a Combat Outpost (COP). Turn in tasks for progression, sell loot for cash, stash the good stuff for future runs, and gear up for the next drop. It's that satisfying "one more run" cycle that keeps you coming back. The redesigned task system in 0.4 added over 100 new missions with better map integration. PvE combat is a mixed bag—the AI can be hilariously dumb at times—but when they get the drop on you, those headshots are merciless and keep every engagement feeling dangerous.
Want even more spice? Jump on a PvEvP server. There are three factions roaming the same map, so you're constantly at risk of running into real players while doing your tasks or contesting COPs for control. Those faction clashes turn a standard loot run into an all out warzone in seconds, and the emergent storytelling that happens in those moments is pure gold.

Now, let's be real about the current rough edges because there are a few. PvP, in particular, still feels janky. Instant respawns mean fights can turn into "who can helicopter back to the fight fastest" spamfests rather than smart, tactical battles. You die, you immediately spawn at a nearby LZ or COP, and you're right back in the action before the dust even settles. It's fun in the moment, but it lacks the weight and permanence that makes great extraction PvP memorable. The community has thrown out tons of smart fixes (longer respawn timers, limited helicopter access, better spawn logic), and I hope the devs take those on board soon. That said, even with the jank, the PvP encounters are still exciting enough that I keep queuing up.
What makes me optimistic is how much the game has already improved and where it's headed. Update 0.4 brought better movement, richer sound design, new weapons and gear, and those beefed up tasks that make the loop feel more fresh. The map and locations are detailed and alive. Looking ahead, the official roadmap points to even deeper PvP systems, more lore to flesh out the world, and quality of life upgrades that will iron out the remaining kinks. This isn't a "wait for 1.0" situation it's already a blast, and the foundation is rock solid for something truly special down the line. I can easily see this replacing Tarkov for me. 

Bottom line: If you love extraction shooters, tactical PvE, or just emergent open world chaos with friends, Gray Zone in its current state is worth grabbing. The loop is strong, the world is gorgeous and fun to explore, and the devs are clearly listening and iterating. Grab your squad (or go lone wolf), pick a faction, and get ready for some good gaming.
Born in the fires of Tarkov but nothing prepares you for the jungle.

It has been two years and the game still does not have a sensitivity scaling, or separate zoom sensitivity. TWO. YEARS. This is a baseline feature of every single good multiplayer FPS game.

Alright this game is super fun (when it functions correctly) but if you are looking at this game as a PvP player do not even bother. The cheaters are a massive problem and it absolutely ruins the Warfare mode, PvE mode is fun atleast though. Stay in joint operations (PvE) unless you want to donate gear to the *many* cheaters that plague this game, seriously devs should just lean even farther into their PvE focus because this ♥♥♥♥ is terrible.

I base my review of GZW on the part that frustrates me the most as a PvE-only player. I appreciate that the developers have spent time on designing the game in such a way that it can be enjoyed without having to engage in PVP, but I think the PvE mode is fundamentally broken. The issue is that, at least at time of writing when the new update has launched, you are in full servers, where almost everything on the map is already looted or cleared of enemies. 

You will constantly experience the disappointment of crawling up on a cool new location, only to find out that all the NPC's are long dead, all the lootable containers are empty and the only thing you can do now is maybe "complete" a quest without any opposition whatsoever. Imagine how a game like Skyrim or Minecraft or any other open world game would be like if there was someone always just ahead of you taking all the good loot and killing all the interesting enemies. It is SO frustrating that this happens again and again, that I cannot in good conscience recommend this game if you intend to experience it as a solo/co-op experience. 

Maybe in the future, the developers can implement instances like in MMORPG's, so that everyone can experience the game equally. It is baffling that this is not already implemented for the PvE servers.

reviews.review_text: Hi I have bought a few of these cosmetic packs.  I like them, and I'm happy to purchase them.  They are purely cosmetic and have no impact on anything in game, however as I change outfit on my character as I travel to different reserves and environments these skins for weapons, tents and blinds add to that kind of roleplaying. It does encourage me to consider what I'm hunting on different reserves, and camo up appropriately.  IF I could get EW to bring in ALL the hunter clothing from Classic and the visibility dynamic from classic I would be very happy to but that as a DLC. For now I like spraying up my tents and blinds in different camo patterns for different locations on reserves. Additionally I spend more on beer that a camo cosmetic pack,  devs have families so I'm happy to contribute to their wages. Have a great day and if you get one of these packs or not, your money, your game, just enjoy whatever you do. Kind regards M.

reviews.review_text:
Covered in the wretched stench of Green Globalist Commies.

I grew up on multiple ranches in the Sonoran desert (this side of the border) and ranchers are 100x better at land management than any globalist with a brown uniform. The proof is reality. Those ranch lands look the exact same today as they did 5,000 years ago, just with improved water management (a few more trees). Ranchers accomplish this by building tanks that trap seasonal rain waters and having thousands of cattle fertilize the soil.

Remember when US Forest services took the wolves out of Yellowstone and almost killed every tree in the freaking park because the deer population exploded and ate every sapling? Or when they stopped all the fires as soon as possible so none of the cones could shed their seeds and grow the next generation? What's worse, they know less now than they did 50 years ago. First thing they do now is put up restrictive fences and paved paths that you supposedly can't leave, but the law does NOT support that. You can walk ALL OVER state and federal lands because they belong to the people, not the government. Leave the path and carry a gun because YOU CAN LEGALLY.

You want to cut fences down? Cut down US Park fences because they're flat out illegal. Ranch fences save lives by keeping livestock off the roads and on the private properties. If you thought hitting a deer was bad...

There are some dangerous lies being told to you and everyone else which effect one thing, and one thing only. The lie is that you're a stupid human that ruins the world, the effect is that you lose free and unfettered access to your ancestral lands. The government DOES NOT have the right. PERIOD. Ya'll better start pushing back, and soon.

reviews.review_text: It's a [i]cosmetic[/i] pack. Get it if you like them. There's a reason that it's an optional DLC. It's [i]cosmetic[/i]. The [i]cosmetic[/i] DLC's only add [i]cosmetic[/i] additions to the game, whoda thunkit. You want gameplay? Go buy a map. That said I'm willing to buy this pack to support that effort and I will have fun having custom versions of the weapons that I will use and feature in my lodges. This does not mean I will support a major uptick in this type of DLC in the future. I would like to see customization made to our characters. I'd also pay one time for that to happen as well and support that development.

Additionally I spend more on beer that a camo cosmetic pack  devs have families so I'm happy to contribute to their wages. Have a great day and if you get one of these packs or not, your money, your game, just enjoy whatever you do. Kind regards M.

name: theHunter: Call of the Wild™ - Hirschfelden Veteran Cosmetic Pack
description: Decorate your weapons, tents, and ground blinds with these unique cosmetics inspired by the Hirschfelden Reserve. This Pack Contains: - Hirschfelden Dark Brown Paint - Hirschfelden Green Paint - Hirschfelden Tan Paint - Hirschfelden Dark Grey Paint - Hirschfelden Spruce Spray - Hirschfelden Aspen Spray - Hirschfelden Stripe Spray - Hirschfelden Beech Wood - Hirschfelden Spruce Wood - Hirschfelden Woodbark Camo - Hirschfelden Woodland Camo - Hirschfelden Premium Wrap The contents of this pack are cosmetic only and applicable to all weapons, tents, and ground blinds in the game. They can be previewed in-game for free before purchasing.
short_description: Decorate your weapons, tents, and ground blinds with these unique cosmetics inspired by the Hirschfelden Reserve.

reviews.review_text: Some nice camos in this pack .

Hi I have bought a few of these cosmetic packs.  I like them, and I'm happy to purchase them. Have a great day and if you get one of these packs or not, your money, your game, just enjoy whatever you do. Kind regards M.

Based on Hirschfelden Which is maybe the best looking map in the game.So i think this is maybe the best Cosmetic pack to buy but its really down to your personal choice.i do like hunting guns to look real with wood so its good to be able to make any gun look the way i want.I got this as part of a bundle deal.
Would be nice if we could apply this to our ATV's and Character Outfits.
Great skins, better than the previous pack!
Should not have paid cosmetics in a game that paywalls everything else.
Pretty cringe DLC tbh, I thought it was like, those dudes with Ghillie suits put on their guns. the grass stuff. nope, just weird paint. makes the gun look WORSE honestly.

name: theHunter: Call of the Wild™ - Wild Goose Chase Gear
description: With the appearance of Hirschfelden’s first field fowl, the Canada Goose, it was time for Connie and Doc to order some new items for the store. Strecker SxS 20G: The little sister to the 12-Gauge Shotgun, the 20-Gauge’s lower velocity rounds and lower weight mean less recoil, and better handling: the right tools to deal with fast-moving prey. Quick to fire, easy to operate even from the hip, and great to show-off, the Strecker SxS 20G is the way to go for any discerning hunter of small and upland game. Ammo: 20 gauge birdshot These 20-gauge shells are designed to be the perfect companion for upland game hunters. 20 gauge buckshot Ideal for small game and varmint hunting, this 20-gauge shot offers a greater number of pellets than its 12 gauge counterpart. Most effective at close range. 20 gauge slug Versatile and powerful, this slug gives the 20-gauge shotgun enough stopping power to take down even medium-sized targets with a single shot. Variations: The Strecker SxS 20G comes in four different color variations: Scarlett, Fiona, Hazel and Raven. Decoys: Get your hands on three extremely realistic Canada Goose Decoys: Feeding, Sentry and Searching. These custom-painted, full-body decoys will fool even the most skeptical of Canada Geese. Once deployed, these decoys increase the chance of geese breaking off from high flying flocks and landing. Incognito Deluxe Layout Blind - Field Camo: The ultimate blind for bird hunting in pastures and fields. Once deployed, it provides excellent visibility concealment for hunters looking to get the jump on fowl.
short_description: With the appearance of Hirschfelden’s first field fowl, the Canada Goose, it was time for Connie and Doc to order some new items for the store.

reviews.review_text: Perfect for Salzwiesen Park

By far the easiest and quickest way to get kills, and money! Just set up, wait, and shoot! Dead zone or not, they'll come! I'm on my second day of owning this DLC, and I've already killed probably like 40-50 geese! I know that i mainly bought this for the double barrel, (because who doesn't love a good side by side), and the 20GA WAS definetly cool, but the goose equipment worked SOOOOOOOOOOOOO good. (By the way, I got it on sale for half price). All in all: 8.5/10.
I like shootin them geese
Needs a fishing minigame.
I personally love this DLC. Geese come readily to the decoys and callers, and that makes them great for racking up XP. Also, the 20 gauge is my new personal favorite for shooting birds.

The only thing that's wild about this goose chase gear is the fact that it's a damn dlc.
You give us a map to hunt goose on for free, but the equipment for them? No that you have to pay for. 
Because buying the base game just isn't good enough.

Making this and the duck equipment into dlc's is honestly disgusting.
It's good but should be base game
geese are currently broken

This DLC corrupted my save and I lost everything lol

don't buy this!
Basically every DLC should be in the base game, you'll need it if you wanna play the game to its fullest.

name: theHunter: Call of the Wild™ - Trophy Lodge Spring Creek Manor
description: Every experienced hunter ends up amassing a sizable trophy collection that deserves to be presented in an appropriate fashion. Spring Creek Manor is perfect as a Trophy Lodge, so show off your prized harvests in a true rustic style. Spring Creek Manor: This spacious British mansion from the 1800s is the perfect location to showcase your favorite trophies. With its exquisite wooden interior, the Spring Creek Manor is the Trophy Lodge of your dreams. Rustic, yet classically furnished, and sporting two floors for you to decorate. So come on in, feel the Persian rugs under your feet and drink in the quiet, nostalgic feel of this lovely country home. Trophy Lodge: Tracking down that elusive quarry has never felt more satisfying as you can finally give your trophies the presentations they deserve. In your very own Trophy Lodge, you will be able to decorate the walls and rooms with your prized kills, creating a visual representation and journey of your best hunting moments. Invite your friends to your lodge at any time and share your evolving collection. If you want to theme your layouts without tearing down your existing decorations, you can create a duplicate of any owned lodge and showcase a completely different animal selection! Plaques & Platforms: Choose how you wish to present your trophies: mount them on a plaque or position them on platforms as full body mounts around the lodge - creating the perfect centerpiece. Either method comes with several poses, allowing you to create the ultimate compositions in each room. And if you feel like it’s time for a change, you can freely remove, replace and reposition your trophies.
short_description: Every experienced hunter ends up amassing a sizable trophy collection that deserves to be presented in an appropriate fashion. Spring Creek Manor is perfect as a Trophy Lodge, so show off your prized harvests in a true rustic style.

reviews.review_text:
Gonna put it simply. This lodge is, in my opinion, the prettiest one. The thing is, it depends on what you’re looking for. Do you want to display bigger animals like bears, bison, buffalos? Then I’d say go and get the Saseka Safari one instead, because this one doesn’t really have that much space to show off bigger animals. Especially if you’re doing multi trophy mounts, you’ll have no space to put them.

Deer antlers MIGHT NOT have enough space and will go through walls.

Now, this lodge, in my opinion, is PERFECT to display birds. So if you’re into birds and you want space to show them off, this one is perfect for it.

So yeah, it depends on what you’re looking for. If you want a “universal” one, yet again I will recommend the Saseka Safari DLC instead.
rustic and large

1 Some of this kind Lodges should be free to everyone! 
2 Its fun to collect different species and display them! 
3 Should have some kind of weapon racks aswell!
Needs a fishing minigame.
Nice lodge
Just get Saseksa safari lodge and skip this.

The default trophy lodge included with the base game, Layton Lake Trophy Cabin, is so gorgeous and I was honestly genuinely impressed by the level of detail it includes. I honestly adore it. All that to point out that this, a DLC they expect you to pay for, feels like it had about 25% of the time and effort put into it, if that. It feels totally lifeless. You can't even go outside! And I wouldn't even complain about these things if they hadn't given us a way better looking (albeit much smaller) trophy cabin for free. It's really got that "default sims 4 house" aesthetic going - but at least those usually have paintings or something.

As for the trophy mounts, there are a lot of them, but it seems like the majority of them are for small animals, so if you don't want to line all your walls with ducks and rabbits (which tend to look terrible on plaques anyway) you still have a pretty limited ability to customize your cabin. It feels like about as much thought went into placing the trophy stands and plaques as the furniture - that is to say, it was crudely slapped together and declared "good enough". 

Unfortunately it's basically a necessity if you want to display any trophies of species from the DLC reserves. I wish I could just put my mule deer and bighorn sheep up in the Layton Lake cabin, honestly. I can't really recommend it in good conscience. TL, DR: I find it boring and ugly.
Like most DLC for this game, It's worth it but should have been added to the game in some form of 'free to play' capacity with better versions available for purchase.

Whoever designed this lodge REALLY loves chairs.
I counted and there are 64 chairs in this lodge (Which is exactly 1 minecraft stack of chairs)

A lot of these chairs make 0 sense and many of them are just 3 chairs stuck together and pointing towards other chairs.
My favourite is the literal cuck chair, that can be found in the corner pointing towards the bed.

I also like the office room? where you have a desk where someone would obviously be writing and then behind the desk two chairs. What are these for? The cheerleaders that cheer on the writer?

I really think theres more furniture out there than chairs and putting just random chairs in every room is really lazy.
I see theres assets inside the lodge that if used right could have made the whole lodge look nicer.

But I guess we have a real chair-lover as a designer instead.

The library is horrendous aswell.

The other lodge is way better, both furniture wise and size wise.

This one is absolutely lifeless, no bathrooms, no outside, just multiple rooms with fireplaces, tables and couches. 1 Library and 1 Bedroom though. Everything is plastered with mounts and taxidermy spaces, good to show everything, but just feels lika storage area in that way. Cannot show weapons aswell.

name: theHunter: Call of the Wild™ - Duck and Cover Pack
description: A fowl has found its way to the Layton Lake District, and Doc’s Outfitters have stocked up with new equipment to best hunt these Mallards. Nordin 20SA: The perfect companion for a hunter with a full day planned, the 20GA Semi-auto is a versatile arm for a demanding shooter. The Nordin 20SA expertly regulates gas pressure for reliability and fantastic handling on all of its loads. Autoloading action, paired with a 3 shell capacity, ensures several shots on target, maximizing the yield from any hunt. Be it small to medium sized-game, upland game or waterfowl. Ammo All previously released 20-gauge shotgun ammo is compatible with the Nordin 20SA, along with one new ammunition type specifically designed to hunt ducks (the 20GA Steel Birdshot, read more below). 20GA Steel Birdshot Lead-free, non-toxic ammunition, tested and adhering to all local and overseas hunting restrictions, while still designed to be ideal for bird hunting. Variations The Nordin 20SA can be found in three different color variations: Serviceman, Clandestine and Artisan. Decoys: These stunningly lifelike and handcrafted Mallard Decoys come in two variations: Drake and Hen. Providing the impression of a lush, fertile and safe feeding zone, these full-body decoy will trick even the most cautious of Mallards, causing them to break formation and land close by. Waterfowl Blind: The perfect blind for hunting Mallards close to the lakeshore. When deployed, it allows the hunter to blend in better with the surrounding scenery, while still retaining a high degree of mobility and freedom to aim.
short_description: A fowl has found its way to the Layton Lake District, and Doc’s Outfitters have stocked up with new equipment to best hunt these Mallards.

reviews.review_text:
Let's get straigh to the nitty and gritty of this beauty.
The waterfowl tents? they work nicely, they're not good housing ideas tho, so, just use em to not spook the ducks when you go and catfish them with your freshly bought resin duck model, which, to be honest with you, might as well be a dollar bill press because you'll be making credits out your behind when you get the hang of this.

So, in short words, the waterfowl tent is okay, about as good as the other tents but can be placed in the water
the Duck decoys, well, they ain't bad, they do their job and they do it honestly well. Think of these tools like a STUG Tank, you have a casemate, not a heavy tank, if you expect it to do all the work for you you'll be left disappointed, but if you know your placement, your main cannon, and the sidelines, then you'll find pleasants result to be common.

The guns.
Now we're finally getting to the good part. This pack comes with 1 gun and 3 reskins. This thing's a 20ga semi auto shotgun with a capacity of 3, this means you get one more as opposed to a double barrel, (which holds as many rounds as the chambers it has, duhhh) And you might think about shying away from this, but i'll tell you, while it's seen less rounds than the other .20ga double barrel you get with the goose chase DLC.

This thing's pretty neat on it's own. I'd like to say it stands out for reasons other than it being a tube fed semi auto 3 round shotgun, but it really doesn't sell much else than that, other than the fact it's in 20ga and the ammo's so cheap you wonder if someone's uncovered a fresh cold war depot of 20ga birdshot.
You can probably use it for other beasts too, i mean, it comes with buckshot AND slugs, so, go crazy i'd say, maybe you can even try to hunt boars with it or deer, or whatnot. I personally used it mostly against waterfowl and nature's little messerschmits, A1 Douglases, spitfires, and yak-3s so, in layman's terms, quails, turkeys, ducks, geese, you name it, it's been poked by this, and gathered by me. 

So, for a scattergun it's definitely the mailman of scatterguns (erm ackshually it's a smooth bored long arm), you're the sender, the bird is the receiver, and if the distance's not too long, just like a mailman it can do it, else, you're going to have to give up your dream on long range shots with birdshot bucko.

So, The NORDIN 20SA Is in many ways a honest shotgun, it won't let you down if you know it's limits and respect them, same as any other tool. i'd rate the pack a whole 7/10, the gun is honestly the biggest thing about this, it's pleasant to use, it does it's job, it doesn't sell itself short neither does it lowball you. It treats you like you deserve to be treated. It's honest, in every way it possibly could be.
Only for small game hunters. Amazing paired with Salzwiesen
INSANE DLC must buy its the best in the game

The Nordin 20 gauge is an excellent weapon that can take anything class 1-7, semi-auto means quick shots, and the 3 round capacity means the reloads aren't too long if you suddenly need to load slugs for a bear or deer. It never falls out of my weapon rotation because of how useful it is. The duck blinds are very useful because it can be used anywhere a normal ground blind is used, it has less vision obstruction than the ground blinds do, and it's totally silent to enter unlike the ground blind. The decoys are obviously only useful for ducks, imagine that. Duck hunting is fun but not very profitable, but the Nordin and the blind are useful anywhere
Its okay but not worth your money when you could just buy the same and get the same in the game without spending any actual money
Not worth being a DLC at all. Should have been a free update.

This is continued example as to why EW is a rip off company that does not actually care about the game they make. They would rather paywall a feature that should be in game to nickle and dime their own playerbase. Disgusting behavior that gets repeated over and over.

Imagine having to pay for duck decoys, I guess ya'll are allergic to things that should be in the base game?
No seriously, why do we have to pay for duck decoys... If ya'll are gonna make it a dlc, atleast fix waterfowl.
refound

This DLC is pure trash; While the automatic 20 gauge is a nice starter gun, its whole raison d'tere, Mallard Hunting, is just plain unworkable unless you have the Revontuli Coast DLC which is a map tuned specifically for waterfowl hunting. Otherwise, being a backfill to Layton Lake District, the waterside terrain can't accommodate the lures and layout blinds and the spawns yield a lot of troll flocks (which are unaffected by lures and never land) and the ducks keep targeting the blinds when they land, effectively spooking themselves. Which is really weird because they got the Canadian Geese down perfectly for Hirschfelden. If you want a quick way to earn money just get the Wild Goose Chase DLC instead. The geese are worth more and it gives you the same .20 gauge shotgun just in a double barrel configuration.

name: theHunter: Call of the Wild™ - Smoking Barrels Weapon Pack
description: A new shipment has just been delivered to the wardens, containing new top-of-the line hunting weapons, like the Hudzik .50 Caplock, M1 Iwaniec and Miller Model 1891. HUDZIK .50 CAPLOCK: Graceful and sophisticated, the Hudzik is a high-end civil-war era caplock rifle. This near-perfectly maintained muzzleloader still offers incredible versatility to traditional hunters, if they can master its lengthy and complex reloading process. A perfect weapon for the confident hunter, the Hudzik is as challenging as it is rewarding. 3 Colour Variations: The Hudzik .50 Caplock comes in 3 variations: Terra, Luna and Sol M1 IWANIEC: The M1 Iwaniec is a rifle of wartime heroes, characterized by the iconic “ping” its en-bloc clip makes when it gets ejected. Reliable, balanced and semi-automatic, it’s an all-rounder masterpiece that has proven its worth in the hands of soldiers, target shooters and hunters alike. Just be careful to not pinch your thumb when reloading it! 3 Colour Variations: The M1 Iwaniec comes in 3 variations: Veteran, Reserve and Collection MILLER MODEL 1891: A firearm as tenacious as they come, this lever-action shotgun follows the legacy of the gun that won the west, delivering unrelenting power with its 10 gauge brass shells. With the largest bore in any commercially produced shotgun, the Miller Model 1891 leaves little to be desired when it comes to close-range stopping power. 3 Colour Variations: The Miller Model 1891 comes in 3 variations: Rancher, Deputy and Sheriff
short_description: A new shipment has just been delivered to the wardens, containing new top-of-the line hunting weapons, like the Hudzik .50 Caplock, M1 Iwaniec and Miller Model 1891.

reviews.review_text: Easily one of the better weapon packs in the game, with truly unique weapons. They all drop their specified classes without issues. Worth on sale, just for the M1 Garand alone.
The M1 and the 10 Gauge have become my staples for every map. Never leave the cabin without them.

When I first tried the Curman, I thought to myself "This takes 40 minutes to reload after a single shot, why would you ever use this?" I didn't understand the draw of muzzle loading rifles.

That changed as soon as I fired the Hudzik .50 for the first time. Everything from the sound, to the ability to equip a scope, to it's ability to double lung a bison from 200m makes this a super fun gun to use. I'm definitely joining the mountainman squad.

If you're anything like me and you kind of miss the good ol' CoD World at War and Battlefield 1943 days, you're gonna love the M1 Iwaniec. This gun is a close mirror of the iconic M1 Garand. 
With it's ever popular and satisfying [i] ping [/i] its high precision and the fact that it uses .30-06 ammo (the best class 4-8 ammo imo) this is one of my favourite guns in the entire game.

[spoiler] you can skip the Miller 1891 [/spoiler]
The Hudzik caplock with the Minie ball ammo is the BEST 4-8 rifle in the game hands down.  My only complaint is that it should be ethical for class 9s as well.  It sure hits hard enough.

The garand is more disappointing than I thought, but the muzzleloader caplock is a beautiful gun. Amazing for ranged take downs at about 250-300 meters (maybe even longer but I've got really good shots at those ranges) and it does a good deal of damage. If you shoot your prey in a vital organ like the lungs, it will drop only a few meters from where it stood.

I'd say the only bad thing about the muzzleloader is the very slow reload and a possible improper ammunition debuff on your score depending on the game & the ammo variant used, but it has to be the best rifle I've ever used and the main gun I've been using on all my hunts. 

Only thing I really have to say about the garand is it has expensive ammo.
M1 is cool but not worth the pack.
The Garand is missing a couple rounds in its en bloc and is not advertised with this artificial limitation
The caplock rifle's sights are misaligned and the M1's sights are extremely skewed, such that I'm not sure how the devs want me to aim with it. They really need to be fixed.
you cant even shot level 9 animals

The game is super buggy and has been for ages now, and the devs do not do anything about it, not recommended.
The game is sadly abandoned.

name: theHunter: Call of the Wild™ - Weapon Pack 3
description: The wardens have just sent word that a new shipment has been delivered, containing quality hunting weapons like the Eckers .30-06 Bolt-Action Rifle, the Vasquez Cyclone .45 Big Bore Air Rifle, and the Andersson .22LR Semi-automatic Pistol. Vasquez Cyclone .45 Big Bore Air Rifle: The Vasquez Cyclone is the poster child for a new generation of big bore air rifles. Designed from the ground up for hunting, this rifle has more than enough power to take down coyotes, wild boars and even medium-sized deer. Silent and Precise: The Vasquez Cyclone .45 Big Bore Air Rifle is much quieter than other firearms and will not frighten nearby animals from as far when a shot is fired. Though its range is shorter than other hunting weapons, it makes up for it with a high capacity magazine and very low recoil, allowing skilled hunters to take precise shots in quick succession. Eckers .30-06 Bolt-Action Rifle: The Eckers is a high-end bolt-action rifle chambered in .30-06. This ever popular cartridge is perfect for hunting large deer and antelopes at ranges up to 300m. The Eckers .30-06 complements this versatility with high precision, top-of-the-line reliability and a beautifully crafted ergonomic frame. It's no wonder that this rifle has become a favorite among discerning big game hunters all around the world. 3 Colour Variations: The Eckers .30-06 comes in three different color variations: Prestige, Elegance and Eminence Andersson .22LR Semi-automatic Pistol: The Andersson .22LR is a small caliber semi-automatic pistol. Its compact size, negligible recoil and impressive firing rate make it practical for a wide variety of applications, from target shooting to small game hunting and personal defense. This .22LR pistol shoots as beautifully as it looks and features a variety of customization options, as is customary for Andersson firearms. 3 Colour Variations: The Andersson .22LR comes in three different color variations:Woodsman, Marksman and Defender
short_description: The wardens have just sent word that a new shipment has been delivered, containing quality hunting weapons like the Eckers .30-06 Bolt-Action Rifle, the Vasquez Cyclone .45 Big Bore Air Rifle, and the Andersson .22LR Semi-automatic Pistol.

reviews.review_text: Killing rabbits was never that fun

Just got this for the 30-06 cause that's the caliber rifle my dad had when I was growing up. My first shot with it was a successful organ shot to a wildebeest (gold trophy). Had to message my pops to tell him the news. we started talking about old hunting games and what rifles we enjoy using in real life and in games. I love this damn game dude.
air rifle is goofy, ruger is cool.
I like the 30-06 and air rifle but not the pistol good dlc tho
Decent weapon pack, 30-06 rifle is quite nice and its fun to have a silent rifle so i'd say its worth it
Its a peace of trash

I was pretty mixed on this review because i do love the 30-06 which is about the only gun i used from this pack, however with the introduction of the 7mm bolt action theres really no need for this pack anymore, save your money and get the 7mm pack
Air Rifle should not be nerfed
I can't seem to access the weapons pack in the game, it says it'sin my library . don't know what I am doing wrong
The 30-06 is good but the rest idk not amazing and you can get other weapons in 30-06 so its a pass on this.

name: theHunter: Call of the Wild™ - Scopes and Crosshairs Pack
description: Master long-range hunting with new precision scopes and a customizable reticle system designed for superior accuracy. Track and hunt game during the darkest hours of night with advanced night vision and gain more choice over your loadout with the new customizable reticle system and options. This pack contains: Angler 4-8x100 Night Vision Rifle Scope Experience unparalleled clarity in darkness with the cutting-edge Angler Night Vision Rifle Scope, which features a wide 100 mm lens and modern white phosphor technology. Odin 4-12x33 Rifle Scope Change optics less often with the all-purpose Odin Rifle Scope, which boasts a magnification range of 4-12x, enabling you to seamlessly transition between close encounters and long-distance shots. Hermes 3-7x33 Handgun/Shotgun Scope Redefine the capabilities of your handguns and shotguns with the Hermes 3-7x Scope. This innovative scope isn’t just an upgrade from the usual 1-4x magnification - it also cuts down on your backpack weight. Customizable Reticles Gain more choice over your loadout with five new reticles: Duplex Center Cross, Mil Dot 02, Reticle Crosscut, Target Dot, and Chevron. You can customize each reticle with any of the game's scopes to suit your playstyle.
short_description: Master long-range hunting with new precision scopes and a customizable reticle system designed for superior accuracy.

reviews.review_text:
Really should have been included with the base game. Objectively contains the best scopes available, though.

Pick it up on sale.
This game is like Asian MMO game. Yet with discount this DLC is  worth it. The night scoop is amazing and Odin is not bad either. !Buy on sale!
It's so good but why would they make us pay for scopes??
must have DLC
any crosshairs in one red dot soon?
Unusable with the current zoom state, 16x you are the flash and 1x you are moving a turret from ww1
i have not recvied the pcak of  weapon

Wow... A dlc, for some RETICLES?! You guys are worse than EA at this point. If this dlc didn't make the hunting experience so much easier, I wouldn't have spent a CENT on it. I guess we pay for small things around here, you guys are ridiculous. Absolutely no reason this couldn't have been free, and same for the "Premium trophy" mounts. Smdh.

And it's $4500 for a reticle change, not a one time payment either, every single time. And the reticles I've tested on the handgun scope doesn't work either. This dlc is a literal cash grab.
All 3 scopes are great but I wouldnt say this is worth full price

TLDR: You may need it eventually but buy only on 50% or more sale and ideally as part of a bundle too.

It's good, unfortunately it's almost a must buy and for that I'm giving it a negative review. This, along with the ATV pack, is something that should have been a free DLC to the base game.

All of the scopes are good, I use all 3 of them in almost every load out.
They have either no non-DLC equivalent or rarely a non-DLC equivalent that takes quite a while to open up (And that feeds into one of the most unrealistic immersion killing parts of the game... skill requirements to open certain extremely common ammo and guns).

While this company normally does a pretty good job of NOT making the DLCs necessary, this and the ATV are exceptions.
This essentially becomes, like the ATV, a must-pay-to-win DLC.
So that being said... try and buy it for pennies on the dollar because you're paying for one of only three or four of their many DLCs that should have been free.

name: theHunter: Call of the Wild™ - Saseka Safari Trophy Lodge
description: It’s time to present your prize trophies in the fashion they deserve. The luxurious and exotic design of the Saseka Safari is perfect to show off your single and multi trophy mounts, or to showcase your favourite weapons on gun racks. Saseka Safari: This modern yet down-to-earth safari lodge encompasses beauty through simplicity. Utilizing natural light to the fullest, Saseka Safari’s mix of wooden and thatched interior makes it the perfect canvas for trophies. The homely furniture and clean open spaces allow you to shape the room, putting your prized animals and weapons center stage at all times. Trophy Lodge: With your very own Trophy Lodge, you will be able to decorate the walls and rooms with your prized harvests, showcasing your victories and best hunting moments. Present your individual trophies using plaques, platforms, or create a scene with several using the multi trophy mounts. Choosing between different poses, you can create the perfect arrangement in each room, whether by highlighting a single animal or by creating a dramatic scene with several. Invite your friends over to share your evolving collection and create multiple lodges for all your decorating needs. Matmat's Multi Trophy Mounts: Set up the perfect hunting moment using Matmat's World Class Taxidermy multi trophy mounts. Placed on any platform or plaque, you will be able to position multiple animals in several realistic poses, visualizing a climactic moment involving your favourite trophies. Recreate incredible moments from the wild or tell an unseen story as you create the ultimate centerpiece for any room in Saseka Safari. This style of mounts will be made available in all trophy lodges. Gun Racks: A hunter’s weapons are just as valuable as the trophies they collect, which is why they deserve to be showcased in the best possible light. In Saseka Safari, you will be able to place your favourite guns on designer stands, and once they are displayed you can view the stats for each.
short_description: It’s time to present your prize trophies in the fashion they deserve. The luxurious and exotic design of the Saseka Safari is perfect to show off your single and multi trophy mounts, or to showcase your favourite weapons on gun racks.

reviews.review_text: Really good but why did they make us pay for a trophy lodge?

Compared to the Spring Manor trophy lodge, it is not as pretty. BUT it has a lot more space, meaning bigger platforms to show off bigger animals (bears, bison, buffalos, etc.), which with the Spring Manor lodge you can’t really do.

I think this one is very universal if you just want to display whatever you come across in the game, and it gives you more options than the Spring Manor lodge.

Now, there IS A LOT of gun displays. Let’s be honest, I don’t even think there are that many weapons in the game, so if you want it to look good you will end up placing the same 7 weapons that you actually use all over the place, and then it’s just gonna look goofy. So in my opinion, there didn’t need to be so many of them, and I wish you could remove the red wall weapon frames if you’re not using them, because even if you don't display any weapons, they’re gonna stay there anyway. So you’re basically forced to put something there, otherwise it's gonna look goofy, and it’s lowkey annoying.

So I’m kind of torn on whether or not to recommend this, so I’m just gonna put it this way - want just something pretty and you don’t really care for bigger animals? Spring Manor is for you. OR do you want to show off whatever you come across? Yeah, then this is the one that you want, regardless of the annoying weapon displays. (I really hate those lol)
W
rjfh
Perfect
When I First Got The Lodge, I Loaded In And My FPS Dropped From 90FPS on Max Graphics to 23FPS. Waste Of Money.
Coolest trophy lodge by far, however, 4€ for this ??

Besides the issues this has caused which are awful, I can't imagine losing all my progress or trophies because of this lodge, I also just don't think it's a very pretty lodge. It feels cold and empty even when filled with trophies. That's a personal opinion though.
It should come with the actual map, games like these are just scams, the base game is boring so you need to buy individual DLCs wasting your money and time.

A trophy lodge is a must have to properly enjoy this game. Animals can have rarities and you want to show it off, you could have somehow survived a bear attack and want to keep that bear as a trophy. There is SO much a lodge can do. The fact that a lodge is not part of the base game is awful. I didn't *want* to buy this DLC. I feel like I *had* to buy this DLC. At this point just make the game $25 instead of $20 and include a lodge.

name: theHunter: Call of the Wild™ - Assorted Sidearms Pack
description: Add three versatile handguns to your hunting arsenal with the Assorted Sidearms Pack. These sidearms are ideal for self-defense against wild animal predators and a reliable alternative to your usual arsenal. This Pack Contains: - 10mm Davani (40A & G4LS): Semi-automatic pistol with a high-capacity clip for close- and mid-range use. Comes with a modified reflex sight. - .243 R.Cuomo (Competitor & Champion): Break-action single-shot handgun for accurate long-range hunting. - .45 Rolleston (Cavalry & Frontier): Classic Old West Americana revolver with great stopping power. Suitable for hunters who want to embrace their inner cowboy. - New Ammunition Types: 10mm Auto Jacketed Hollow Point, 10mm Auto Hard Cast, .45 Flat Nose, and .45 Colt Full Metal Jacket.
short_description: Add three versatile handguns to your hunting arsenal with the Assorted Sidearms Pack.

reviews.review_text:
I would argue this weapon pack is worth picking up for the not-a-Glock alone.

In terms of performance, the .45 Rolleston feels like a slightly-worse version of the Focoso 357 with an excruciatingly-long reload time. However, it cannot be denied that using the most-recognizable firearm of the Old West is very fun. Its animations are also full of personality. Great for a cowboy cosplay or personal challenge.

The 10mm Davani an indispensable defense against aggressive animals like packs of wolves. It also boasts the highest magazine capacity of any firearm in the game. Not a fan of the iron sights? This pack also includes a micro red dot sight exclusive to the 10mm Davani. I always have it as part of a loadout.

Finally, the odd one out. The .243 R. Cuomo fills the niche of entirely buying out the Handgun perk tree but being disappointed that you can't use any of the scopes available to rifles. It is essentially the Ranger .243 in a compact and lower-weight package.

Overall, a welcome addition to theHunter: Call of the Wild's lineup of firearms.
After just 15 minutes with Davani I want 50 round mag in Arzena

Want a better 243 that only weighs 1 but shoots one at a time? Want a glock to defend yourselves against wolves? This is it. 

Should not have been a paid dlc imo but allows for much more versatility with handguns and general loadout viability. Get on sale.

There are three kind of handguns in this pack. I'll break it down for you buddy, because the hard pressed iron of these has been a trusty companion of the waistband of the very concrete virtual hunter pants I wear.

So, let's break it down, The first handgun, the .45 Rolleston.
This is the kind of handgun that makes you think there's two types of players in this game. Ones with a 6 shooter that know the cowboy load, and ones who need a speedloader because their shot doesn't count. You get it, you're the quick, but you're also the accurate. When you fire you don't miss, and when the boar challenges you, for you it's high noon at any hour. If there's something you learnt, is that the unapologetic companion to the lever action or double barrel you don, is this beautifully crafted brass or steel framed six shooter that will never let you down. If 6 shots are enough to kill anything that moves, you're even better because the cowboy load forces you down to 5.

The 10mm Davani player:
You're likely the kind of man to have a shoulder holster, or under the armpit holster, you probably keep a laminated card of your favourite bureau in your wallet, and you'd flash it to the moose in Finland if you could, except it's not one of the many federal states you'd have jurisdiction in. Yeah, you get it, this gun's a cry for those who miss the 10mm age of the FBI, the ones that wishes they were special agents sent in the midwest who'd look for clues about their investigation in their coffee. There's a lot of peaks with this gun, To start off, an unapologetically large magazine as you strategically decide which animal is a threat to the stability of the government, and which one will just have to remember there's a man in a suit doing business with nature, the No1 terrorist. Anyhow, my dear federal affectionadio, This piece's for you, smell the polymer, it's your duty to protect these lands from overpopulation.

The .243 R.Cuomo player, the final one sitting down at the table of poker. One's got 5 in their hand, Another's got more than 10, and the cuomo player? well this guy has 1 in their hand. It's like a badly rigged game of texas hold em. But the cuomo player has something most don't have. A next to unlimited reservoir of money, (because the .243 round in game is free) and a possible skill that can be either before or after the bell. 
Yeah, here's the thing about this piece of metal, it's a breech loaded, hammer struck pistol, So, you're practically embarking in naval fights against your foe, except it's more like shooting at a merchant ship that will only retaliate when threatened, so their only move is ramming. If you know where to fire, lungs and heart, this thing will only need one round to mark it's boundaries. If you need more, then you just blew the one chance you had.
If anyone's ever had to train for a speech this gun might be the ultimate testament. Miss your shot and your prey flees harder than your stand up comedy crowd when you crack a poor joke.

The rounds:
.243:
it's the starter round, only the breech loader handgun and the main boltie rifle use it, do I have to tell you what it is? really? because odds are it's the thing you're still using now when butterflies come out your wallet.

.45 Long colt :
If something's ever decided to get in your way and you needed an answer pronto, well, here it is. it packs a punch, medium to small game's bound to appreciate your will to show them You're Bad, You might be good on some days, and their end will definitely be ugly if they try to nibble you. Ask the wolves in siberia, They probably have more .45 lead than snow in them.

10mm:
A bit like the long colt i suppose, both are intended for personal defense anywho. It is suitable from small to medium game as well, except i reckon this one has a bit more punch to it too. it just can fit in a double stack mag is all. This is also a good bring along when some animals decide to bother you, like the wolves in siberia. Again, this leans real hard into that retro 1980s fantasy.

Given all i've said, this pack was done so well, had it been a tax form the IRS would've probably sent cake to your address and a congratulatory plaque. Yeah, the developers of this pack should give themselves a pat on the back, this much like an eclipse, is a one in a lifetime event that will turn your jaw into an air ram intake.
Good

They tricked me into buying this DLC with the cowboy talk for the .45 Rolleston revolver, but it's weaker than the already existing .44 magnum in the base game. The glock is nice but that is not why I bought this pack and I am feeling let down. It is much more rewarding to save up 24k in game credits and buy the magnum if you want to really feel like a cowboy.
Love me some Glock but not for 4 dollars.
dont buy it waste of money there are better dlc packs to buy
Bought it and it is no where to be found in the game not in inventory or store? any help please i have verified game files uninstalled the game and reinstalled it still nothing like whats going on?

I bought it because I want to have all the guns. That said its not a very useful pack. The .45 sits in a weird category where its to powerful for smaller game but not strong enough for big game. The 10mm is cool but how useful is a glock and eotech for hunting? COD style hunting? The .243 is novel in that you can attach most scopes to it but its a single shot break barrel. It also is too strong for fox and smaller game. So really its an under powered wolf and deer gun. 

I would say unless you're a completion'ist save your money.

name: theHunter: Call of the Wild™ - Parque Fernando
description: You’ve wandered through valleys and hills, savannas and frozen forests but now, it’s time to explore our latest reserve in South America. Parque Fernando has asked for your aid in turning an otherwise wild slice of Patagonia into world class hunting grounds. Roam through the gnarled trunks of the dead forests, traverse the swamps and hills, and make your way across the grasslands as you open outposts, build lodges and prove yourself to the reserve’s fierce owner, Carolina. Only time will tell if you can turn the reserve into a hunter’s paradise. PARQUE FERNANDO’S FAUNA Buzzing with life, Parque Fernando sports a large range of wildlife. Get ready to spot a vibrant Cinnamon Teal taking flight, or a Blackbuck pronking as you make your way across the reserve. Not to mention the fact that the park nourishes some the biggest, most exquisite bucks you’ll ever hunt, like the Mule Deer, Red Deer and Axis Deer. Just remember: you are not the apex predator here. That job belongs to the Pumas, and they don’t like to share. This majestic feline can be spotted stalking and chasing after its prey and if you’re especially quiet, you might even find catch one feeding on its prize. LUSH AND VIBRANT FLORA Framed by hills, Parque Fernando houses a variety of environments to explore. Stroll through the snags of the dead forest, wander the steppes and lupine meadows in search of game, or make your way to the tranquil lakes that pepper the park. MISSIONS AND MEASURES Like all previously released reserves, Parque Fernando stretches over a massive 25 square miles (64 square kilometers). It’s up to you if you want to explore off the beaten path and discover the secrets of the reserve, or if you decide to take on the captivating new story, with 16 new narrative missions, and 39 side missions. Through the years, Carolina met the demands of leading her family and her real estate business, but at a price. While she never had enough time to tend to the unfinished reserve, she was always determined to one day finish what she and Fernando started. That day has now come, and she has called for your aid, hoping to turn her slice of wild Patagonia into a world class hunting reserve. SHOW OFF YOUR HUNTING SKILLS Assist Carolina in building the new lodge for Parque Fernando, and decorate it with your collection of diamond trophies, one from each species of the reserve. Or visit the state-of-the-art archery/shooting range, where you can put your aiming skills to the test. Make sure to keep an eye out for the reserves challenge targets, which are scattered across the grounds. SHARED EXPERIENCE As with our previous paid DLCs, if a player doesn’t own the content they can still join a session in Parque Fernando at no cost, simply by finding players that own it and joining them. It’s up to you if you decide to compete or cooperate. All players, regardless of DLC ownership, will have a whole new set of clothing available to purchase for in-game currency.
short_description: You’ve wandered through valleys and hills, savannas and frozen forests but now, it’s time to explore our latest reserve in South America. Parque Fernando has asked for your aid in turning an otherwise wild slice of Patagonia into world class hunting grounds.

reviews.review_text: Very good
EGREWRE
its mid but the hate is unjustified
The animals are fun to hunt and its rather easy to hunt. The animals let you get really close which I like.

8/10
Great DLC, top 5-7.
. Huge map with tons of lakes and dense mountains, very realistically representing Argentine Patagonia.
. Many animals appeared here for the first time.
. It has the largest deer; you'll hardly find so many Level 8-9 deer anywhere else.
. It presents a high hunting difficulty due to the terrain's inclination across almost the entire map and its dense flora, making it complex to get good shots. Therefore, I wouldn't recommend it for beginners.
this map just straight up sucks

Can't overstate how ♥♥♥♥♥♥ it is that the dev's had locked the archery range behind a paid DLC instead of releasing the range as part of the base game. It's part of their ongoing monetization tactic of inventing problems in the base game that you can solve by opening your wallets for them.

Not the biggest fan of this one. Pass on it unless it's on a big sale or you've played out the better Reserves.

The ground clutter isn't just a challenge it's a functional buggy inconvenience, including getting stuck in the ground randomly near quest areas. The zone geography messes with the ATV something horrible, although being insta kill catapulted almost a sub zone away for hitting one small rock was humerus. One of the free weapons doesn't appear in your store list like ones from other DLC's do, telling you you can earn it, so you can end up buying the same gun not knowing you're getting a free one with this DLC. I will begrudge the other weapon, and partaking in the challenge circle target shoot side quest, is somewhat cool and fun challenge but it has a similar issue as well.

Is it 75% or more off? Yeah sure pick it up. 50-65% off? Maybe, especially if you've played most the other better Reserves.
Less than half off? Skip it and try one of the Reserves with better reviews than "mixed", which this one unfortunately earns for a reason.
Nature are not look good small amount of animals and the thing only bring me to play this DLC is a puma

I love this game, but I honestly dislike this map very much, to the point it's my least favorite map. My main issue has been the foliage. It is too dense, to the point you can't see an animal right in front of you because of how dense the grass and bushes are, but they can see you because, for some reason, most of it offers zero cover, which is something I don't understand. This added to the very extreme terrain depressions in several parts of the map makes hunting in said areas a punishing experience. It doesn't help that the brushes make so much noise you have to tread carefully or avoid them, specially to avoid triggering a very annoying bug that makes brush noises continue even when outside the bushes. It's not like I need a shooting gallery, but maps with dense vegetation, like Layton or Hirschfelden, don't feel like this.

The only reason I'm even playing the map is because I'm attempting the last achievement I have left, which is tied to a mission inside the map, but the moment I'm done, I'm done with the map. It's just not enjoyable to me. It really hurts me to have to say this, but it is one of my favorite games, I have bought every map and multiple DLCs and I always go into this game when I want to chill, but I can't handle this map.

name: theHunter: Call of the Wild™ - Cuatro Colinas Game Reserve
description: Strolling across dazzling fields and through soft rolling hills, this part of Iberia is a romantic setting for any reserve. It is no wonder that warden & custodian Alejandra considers her soul entwined to the very land itself, this hunting ground of kings. From the oddly graceful marble quarry, soft hills and crumbling monastery, to the meadows covered in sunflowers and lavender alike, Cuatro Colinas Game Reserve will take your breath away. CUATRO COLINAS’ FAUNA The romantic landscape of Iberia has an equally diverse cast of wildlife roaming its various biomes. Chase after the European hare in the brushes, walk alongside the majestic red deer or temperamental wild boar, and catch roe deer grazing in the fields. Keep an eye out for the silent Iberian wolves and search the cliffs for the daring Mouflon Sheep. For those of you yearning for a challenge, you have the Spanish Grand Slam, which is made up of the 4 Ibex breeds - the humble Ronda, the agile Southeastern, the strapping Beceite and the daunting Gredos. IBERIAN FLORA If one had to describe Cuatro Colinas Game Reserve’s ambience, it would be with one word - regal. The region exudes both rich history and flourishing nature, ripe for your exploration. Its most distinguishing feature is its vast open fields, covered in sunflowers, olive groves, and lavender, surrounded by soft mountains. Framing the open areas are broadleaf lush and dry pine forests, providing perfect cover for cautious animals. The land is far from untouched though, as the marble quarry’s sharp angles create a stark contrast to the surrounding area. MÅRTENSSON 6.5MM BOLT-ACTION RIFLE The Mårtensson 6.5mm, the name while unusual is a tribute to a great foreign weapon master, is a modern bolt-action rifle manufactured by Spanish weaponsmiths. Designed to satisfy the needs of hunters and range shooters alike, this rifle features a smooth and reliable action encased in a lightweight ergonomic frame. Paired with the low recoil and flat trajectory of 6.5mm ammunition, the Mårtensson 6.5mm Bolt-Action Rifle boasts impressive accuracy even at long ranges. The rifle is available in three colour variations: Lightning, Thunder and Rayo. MISSIONS AND MEASURES Cuatro Colinas Game Reserve spans a massive 25 square miles (64 square kilometers), and it will be your call whether you take off exploring the reserve and go your own way, or if you pick up the unique story with 14 new narrative missions, and 55 new side missions. The land of Cuatro Colinas Game Reserve has had many different patrons, from the Kings of Europe who have wandered across the rocky dehesa, to modern hunters, equipped with the best of today's equipment. Now, however; Doña Alejandra del Bosque Garcia is straining under the responsibility that has occupied her family for centuries. Amidst troubling animal attacks, disgruntled farmers, family tragedy and hostile corporate takeovers, she must find a way to protect the very essence of what makes Cuatro Colinas a hunting ground worthy of kings. It is her destiny, and it could be yours too. SHARED EXPERIENCE As has been the case with previous paid DLCs, if a player doesn’t own it they can still join a session in Cuatro Colinas Game Reserve at no cost by finding another player who owns it and joining their game.
short_description: Strolling across dazzling fields and through soft rolling hills, this part of Iberia is a romantic setting for any reserve. It is no wonder that warden & custodian Alejandra considers her soul entwined to the very land itself, this hunting ground of kings.

reviews.review_text: over all good map but the best part is the iberian wolf

Not my favorite reserve but beautiful and unique and thus satisfying.  Its rustic fields, orchards, and vineyards offered a nice change of scenery and challenge. The plethora of mountain goats to hunt was a refreshing change after many deer heavy reserves, and the wolves were satisfyingly menacing. The main story was good, though it didn't touch upon all areas of the map... thankfully there were many side-quests. Sadly still no revamp to the leveling/skill/reward system... it continues to lack a sense of progression at the too quickly reached max level.
yep

Good DLC, one of the most visually beautiful in the game. The mountainous regions, the orchards, the farm land, it all works well together with a good selection of local creatures to hunt
Definite buy on sale, even a good buy normally if you're needing a new reserve.
