Do know the weapon that comes with this DLC is only available after a long and challenging quest line that can't be started until the main quest line is done.

This DLC single-handedly saved me from uninstalling COtW. Layton and Hirschfelden are rather unforgiving to a new player and this map, with a combination of story-driven xp gains and very forgiving open spaces means a new player actually has a chance to sight a target from a decent range.

This is one of my favorite maps out of the available DLC.
Five years and the final mission to unlock the exclusive rifle skin is still bugged.  I'm five Diamond trophies in and not a single one has registered.

This map plays like a sleeping med.
The longer I hunt on it, the sleepier I get.

Context to keep in mind for my review: I have only played on the following reserves across my 130.5 hours played of CotW:
- Layton Lakes
- Hirschfelden
- Silver Ridge Peaks
- Yukon Valley
- Cuatro Colinas

Let me start this review by stating how I genuinely feel about this reserve:

I love the map. The scenery is beautiful. The scale feels grand on foot and adequate on the ATV. The variety of wildlife is fine, with the selling point being how cool Iberian wolves are, which are probably the most aggressive animals I've encountered in the game thus far — and yes, that includes gray wolves. Although...I was once killed by a moose while AFK to eat dinner, so there's that. I digress.

Interestingly enough, this reserve has allowed me to rack up some serious in-game credits/currency in a very short time frame. Not only is wildlife abundant, but so are the missions, especially side ones. This alone could be a solid reason to consider making the purchase — particularly for those still needing to grind out their balance for various purchases.

When I saw that Cuatro Colinas had lavender fields (and lots of them), I was taken right back to my trips to the Provence of France and how much I love that region, instantly selling me on this reserve. And when you find yourself in the more arid parts of it, you almost feel like you've been transported to California, which is a state I've felt should receive a reserve of its own — likely centered around the Sierra Nevada. The meeting of the above factors actually quite adequately represents what the peninsula of Iberia is reminiscent of, having visited Spain a couple of times myself.

So, if I love it so much, why would I not recommend this DLC?

Simple: What many others have pointed out in their recommendations —or lack thereof— rings entirely true; the stability of this map is absolutely horrific. Not only do I get slight FPS drops on my slightly-above-average device (graphics set to ultra with everything maxed out is typically between 75 and 90 for most reserves, but only 60 to 75 on Cuatro Colinas), which are a little annoying in and of themselves, but the game also regularly crashes while playing on this reserve. For context: I had spent roughly 105 hours on the other four maps prior to buying and immediately jumping into Cuatro — all without a single crash. Within a mere 5 hours or so, I'd experience my first CotW crash on Cuatro Colinas. After having spent probably about 16 hours or so total playing on this map, I've experienced three more such instances, for a total of four times — about one crash for every four hours played. Mind you, I've also spent additional time on the other maps mentioned at the beginning since, and I am yet to experience a single crash across all four of those with probably about 115 hours played combined.

Thinking about buying? Proceed with caution. These crashes can and will occur while you're tracking down a potentially important kill. I do think it's a pleasant experience, though, whenever it works, which is why it pains me to say I cannot in good faith recommend Cuatro Colinas, when I truly wish I could.

Replaying the reserve for the second time because the main mission arc achievement didn't trigger on the first playthrough. Again, narrator started to repeat the lines at some point. Really annoying bug that wasn't fixed 5 (!) years since the DLC release. Too bad I prefer to get all achievements before moving onto the next reserve. Wasn't a problem with the first 3-4 reserves. Real tiredom since Parque Fernando...

This content does not reach a decent quality to recommend it.  It does not run properly... bugs and glitches ...are doing their job . I loved this reserve ... but now it's 2024.. no repair and no interest at all to fix it as it seems! Quite disappointing.

name: theHunter: Call of the Wild™ - Rancho del Arroyo
description: Generations of cowboys have eked out a living in these badlands, through revolution, war and times of struggle. From the abandoned mines and dusty roads to the surrounding mountains that overlook the heart of the desert, the Wild West lives on in spirit in Rancho del Arroyo. Brave the heat of the sun, experience a place that teems with life, and discover remnants of the past that were thought to be long forgotten. HUNTING HOTSPOT Watch the Whitetail Deer, Mule Deer, and Bighorn Sheep scuttle across the plains in search of shade. Head towards the river for a chance to spot the Rio Grande Turkey, and keep your finger on the trigger as you come close to the Ring-Necked Pheasant, Collared Peccary, and Coyote. When night falls, hunker down in the desert shrublands and observe the elusive Mexican Bobcat stalk Antelope Jackrabbits. COUSO MODEL 1897 Introduced in the late nineteenth century, the Couso Model 1897 16-gauge pump-action shotgun is a respected piece of iron among hunters, police officers and soldiers alike. It can carry five shells in its tubular magazine and fire multiple shots in quick succession, making it a versatile weapon in the hands of any skilled hunter. RIGHT OLD WRONGS When warden Salvador Soto Muñoz returns to his childhood home to take over the family ranch, he makes a decision that will forever alter their legacy, selling the cattle and turning the land into a biosphere reserve. Now, with the grand re-opening of the ranch mere weeks away, Salvador needs your help to ensure that it makes for a viable hunting ground. Along the way, you’ll uncover long-lost secrets and solve the century-old mystery that tore a powerful family apart. SHARE THE EXPERIENCE You can hunt in the reserve even if you don’t own the DLC by joining someone in multiplayer who does. This will give you access to everything except the narrative missions.
short_description: Hunt unique desert animals, explore a landscape full of secrets, and solve a century-old mystery that tore a powerful family apart in the latest reserve for the immersive hunting game theHunter: Call of the Wild.

reviews.review_text: WARNING: This map is so fun you might die of an excitement and/or good times orverdose! hunt at your own risk! 10/10

6/10
An interesting map if you get it at a good price. It's entertaining because it has its own unique fauna with an exclusive animal. It doesn't offer much more, and part of its map is complicated with very dense forests and very boring plateaus. It's still beautiful, like all of them, but it's one I'd recommend buying if you already own all the others, because it doesn't really offer much on its own.
Needs a fishing minigame.
Desert hunting locations in this game aren't very common and this is one of the coolest.
Very chaotic running into a random cactus now and again. Enjoyable nonetheless.

If I were the devs I would work on figuring out how to bump the render distance of animals up at least as far as one can see (1000+ meters). As is, animals wont render through daytime binoculars until they are within about 420 meters.

This makes maps with long sight lines feel dead, especially Rancho De Arroyo, because glassing this map gives tons of false info. It looks like a lake is a postcard / has no signs of life until you actually walk it. Quite frustrating and immersion breaking.

So, get it if you are nostalgic for Mexico / High Dessert Biomes like me (I was born in New Mexico). But otherwise pass.

There is a severe lack of yellow filter, I just can't immerse myself into the map without it. Halfway through I started thinking I was in Spain, until I remembered this is mexico.

Overall it's okay :)
Not to much animal

I thought the reviews saying there were no animals were exaggerated, but I could not have been more wrong. I was on for an hour this morning and saw 3 turkeys.

One mission line that’s glitched in the end and has nothing to do with hunting - more like a children’s mystery game, or errand simulator.

I was really excited about this map, but I wish I hadn’t bought it.
This is the first DLC from this game i am refunding. Their is far too much foliage and far too few animals.

name: theHunter: Call of the Wild™ - Treestand & Tripod Pack
description: Our wardens have just received some heavy looking boxes containing exciting new hunting equipment. With this new pack, you will be able to create the perfect ambush using the Deployable Treestand and Deployable Tripod Stands. Through their more stealth-like approach to hunting, your impact on the environment will be reduced, ensuring that your hunting pressure is lowered. So pack your bags hunter, the wild beckons! Deployable Treestand: The staple of a patient hunter, the Deployable Treestand allows you to bide your time until the perfect opportunity to catch the animal unaware. Check your environment for a fitting tree for your ambush, place your stand at an appropriate height and climb up to your new perch. You can also easily adjust and replace it if you are unhappy with your initial placement. Once you’ve ready, sit back, relax and watch as you’re giving the opportunity to take that perfect shot. Additionally, it reduces your impact on your surroundings, reducing the hunting pressure significantly. Variations: The Deployable Treestand comes in three colour variations - Conifer, Timber and Arctic. Deployable Tripod Stands: With a 360° rotation capability, the Deployable Tripod Stand allows you a better vantage point even when there are no trees around. The Tripod Stand is an excellent way to gain an overview of your surroundings and allows you to spot animals from a much greater distance. On top of that, they will keep you concealed, are lightweight and easy to deploy and lowers your hunting pressure on your environment. Variations: Much like the Treestand, the Deployable Tripod Stands come in three variations - Conifer, Timber and Arctic.
short_description: Our wardens have just received some heavy looking boxes containing exciting new hunting equipment. With this new pack, you will be able to create the perfect ambush using the Deployable Treestand and Deployable Tripod Stands.

reviews.review_text: Extremely useful
Get this for the tree stand really. It is nice to have a high vantage point and not spook the animals.
Good
Game changing pack!! Very fun to shoot from the trees and overall very op stands/pods!
its ok

This is a very nice DLC. Please, be aware, that deploying and removing either a Treestand or a Tripod will create enough noise to spook animals in a 100 to 150 m radius. Which for me is counter productive. I am not much of a camper, am much of an explorer, this is what I call a True Hunter so I initially thought, if I see a beautiful buck, GREAT, I will deploy the Tripod for a nice shot, ERRRRRRRR, WRONG!!!! If you do that, Animals flee!!!! It makes sense within 50 to 80 mts. You know, IRL, no hunter would dare to make such a lousy installation to spook a baddie looking deer, LOL!!!!!! and by the way, it's the same when you dismount/remove/collapse the Treestand or Tripod. Something else, I started noticing, there are rendering issues while carrying the Treestand/Tripod. This DLC is a great Fit for Campers and Blinders, not so much for HUNTERS. I am currently on my BOW era soooooo much of my hunting is done by Bow and Crossbow. I only use my 7 mm Rifle when I feel... Romantical. (Winks in Spanish) (By the time, this review was done, on the item description there is no indication that this DLC makes noise, and does state: "This is a lightweight item" That gives off the idea is meant to be used as a stealth weapon. If the game developers were to change 100 to 150 m Spook area to 85 mts. I would surely give this DLC a MASSIVE thumbs up!
Like most DLC for this game, It's worth it for convenience but should have been added to the game in some form of 'free to play' capacity with better versions available for purchase.

This is amazing, but why is this a dlc?
Having something that every other hunting game gives you as a base element turned into a dlc is crazy.

The base game is already bland enough as it is, could have alteast given us these for free.
But alas, gotta make everything a dlc. Even things that should just come with the base hunting game.
it says i have to but them even though i got the dlc

This completely overwrote my tents and blinds.
Can't figure out how to recover.

name: theHunter: Call of the Wild™ - Te Awaroa National Park
description: There’s something truly magical about Te Awaroa National Park, something that has captured the imagination of movie directors and hunters alike for generations. Rolling hills, temperate rainforests, and deciduous forests combine to create a microcosm of the entire South Island of New Zealand. Watch the landscape shift before your very eyes as you trek across this beautiful reserve. While hunters make their way here in search of Sika Deer, Chamois, and Feral Goats, Te Awaroa National Park has also seen an influx of tourism due to the movies recently filmed in the area. It’ll be up to you and warden Kiri Taylor to ensure that no permanent damage is done to the splendour of the reserve, and that its native flora and fauna are preserved. Tracking the numerous animals that roam Te Awaroa’s stunningly diverse environments is a favorite among hunters. For some, it’s the rush of stalking a prized Sika Deer in the beech forest that draws them here, while others come in hopes of catching a rare glimpse of the Feral Pigs, Feral Goats, and Chamois. And for those looking for a more relaxed experience, there’s plenty of Red Deer, Turkey, European Rabbits and Fallow Deer to hunt. It’s of no surprise that Te Awaroa has been used to depict fairytale landscapes, as it's ever-changing ecosystems are truly a sight to behold. From the steep, impressive mountains that divide the reserve, to the sparkling beaches and dense temperate forests, the region transforms under your very feet. There are also more familiar hunting grounds available, such as spruce and pine forests. What the F.L. Sporter .303 lacks in condition, it more than makes up for in performance. The bolt action is smooth, reliable and easy to cycle, and it’s a proven rifle for both hunting and target practice. With a wide selection of compatible sights, and a great effective range, it can take down a broad selection of animals without problem. Stretching across a vast 25 square miles (64 square kilometers), Te Awaroa National Park has always been a place to get away from it all, both for the first Polynesian settlers and the hardy men who made their names hunting deer from helicopters. Times are tough though, and when warden Kiri Taylor reluctantly agrees to allow a series of Hollywood films to be made in the reserve, it goes from sleepy backwater to daytripper hotspot almost overnight. Now, with the reserve's delicate ecology threatened, Kiri has had enough. With you at her side, she’s going to show the world that New Zealand isn’t all just haka and halflings. Across 16 new story missions, you’ll deal with local menaces, patch things up between old friends, and discover the truth behind a long-forgotten legend. You’re able to hunt in Te Awaroa National Park even if you don’t own the DLC by joining someone in multiplayer who does. This will give you access to everything except the narrative missions.
short_description: Nestled at the foot of the Southern Alps, this paradise is home to both temperate beech forests and steep alpine peaks. While some are drawn here in search of Sika Deer, others come to trace its trails of history. You can rest easy knowing your next adventure awaits in Te Awaroa National Park.

reviews.review_text: I don't know anything about New Zealand but I like this map.  It's a lovely place to walk around and take irresponsible shots at 30-50 feral hogs.  I don't know what the guy complaining there's no tahr was talking about, because I bagged one just this afternoon?  Maybe they added 'em in later, who knows.  It's good.

Te Awora National Park is a beautiful map that really shows off what a semi native new zealand mountain range can look like, this map has got a few issues but they're mainly just bugs such as slightly floating roads and bushes, with the only major one being the terrain sliding unintentionally sometimes. Now when it comes to the animals on the reserver there is a little lacking, while the current selection is good it just needs like 2 or 3 more species to really elavate it, for example they could add a few species of elk and waterfowl as nz is known for these when it comes to their hunting.

Overall really good map that has lots to give, but i would not reccomend this as a first dlc

Te Awaroa is more of a grinding map than anything but it can also be enjoyed without grinding. There isn't much going for this map but it does come with one of the best 4-8 rifles in the entire game, that being the Lee-Enfield .303. Arguably the best Deer map as there's several species and very easy to hunt. I also got my only G1 on this map, that being the Red Deer with only 273 kills. Got extremely lucky.

I really do like the map. Especially the weather changes are awesome. Don't know why people complain about the animals. Sure there is no real threat and exotic animals. But there are a lot animals around. So great map for leveling and just having fun.

A reserve that surprised me with how much I ended up liking it. The firearm that comes with it is easily attained, it's just a reward for completing the main quest line which is pretty casual. The zones are generally nicely done with a fair selection of creatures to hunt, though the hunting can be challenging. Between fog and rain being common and the dense foliage, it can be something of a jungle adventure. I enjoyed that as an alternative to say SRP's wide open fields but it's not every hunters cup of tea. Definite buy on sale, maybe buy full price if you're wanting a new reserve that's quick to get into.
A very mid map with a mid storyline. Worth for red deer grinders but only on deep discount.

You can pick it up at a third of the price. Even for that, its kind of bland .
And it totally messed up my cloud sync
And the park owner needs to STFU.

No opition to hunt her with dum dums

On any given day, it's a coin toss between this and Mississippi as to which is my least favourite of the DLC maps, not helped that, when launched it was so bugged that it crashed my game if I even had the DLC active. EW took a long time patching that one, too.
What's wrong?
The mission still has bugged mission items, invisible items, and unclear objectives, so find a play-though on YT as this won't get fixed now. The scenery is well done, when you can see it, because between the mist and the rain, you won't get a clear view very often. Some of the locations (underground cavern) they clearly spent time on, all for you to never visit again. Indeed, the choice of where to place the map borders is curious, given some of the out-of-bounds areas are areas you will be trekking through to get from one POI to the next. Likewise the variety of species is good, but a lot of the map is pointless as they are easier to hunt in some places than others (looking at you, thick foresty undergrowth). Oh, and at time of writing the backfilled species of Tahr is still tagged as Himalayan. It used to be the map for fallow grinding, but not sure that holds up, either.
For me, the main mission cycle is okay, and the gun you get for completing most of it is one I actually like using, but other than that, very disappointing.
The Mokka Moose :(. I did not knew that you have to decide between the picture and the trophy. I took the foto and he was gone :(. Plssss fix this and give me the moose back.
Small, boring, every zone looks the same, only interesting animal is a red deer. There is no need to buy this. Better get Tiga, Savanna, Revontuli Coast, Parque Fernando and Yukon Valley map.

name: theHunter: Call of the Wild™ - Mississippi Acres Preserve
description: Nicknamed the “Magnolia State” – a nod to the beautiful Magnolia Trees that grow in the region – Mississippi Acres Preserve holds a special place in the hearts of hunters. Not only because of its stunning landscapes, where distinctive wetlands and bluffs meet pine forests and murky rivers, but also for its characteristic wildlife. Wherever you decide to go, experience exceptional hunting and outdoor adventures in Mississippi Acres Preserve. DELTA HUNTING Stalk the Gray Fox as it gracefully traverses the forest floor, or flush skittish Northern Bobwhite Quails and Eastern Wild Turkey from their hiding spots. Catch the nimble Eastern Cottontail Rabbit as it darts through the underbrush, swiftly moving to avoid the Feral Pigs, Black Bears and Whitetail Deer that roam nearby. In man-made areas, don’t be surprised to see the Common Raccoon raid garbage cans and cause havoc. These little bandits are no joke. But most importantly, never lower your guard near rivers or freshwater – the patient American Alligator lurks in the murky depths, waiting for the perfect moment to ambush its prey. KULLMAN .22H This classic bolt-action rifle is the perfect tool for small game hunting. Chambered in .22H, and available in three distinct variations, it is both reliable and powerful enough to keep small predators away from your property and livestock. Thanks to its lightweight yet robust frame, the Kullman .22H makes for an ideal rifle to carry with you during long hunts. BUMP IN THE NIGHT Mississippi Acres Preserve has been left devastated by the worst flooding in a decade, swamping entire communities and forcing its residents to flee their homes. Members of two local rival hunting clubs have chosen to form an uneasy alliance in an attempt to help the community get back on its feet. However, things aren’t what they seem, and when warden Immi Davis finds herself in the middle of a sinister conspiracy, things quickly take a turn for the worse. Can you help her uncover the truth? SHARE THE EXPERIENCE Play Mississippi Acres Preserve without owning it by joining someone in multiplayer who does. This will give you access to everything but the narrative missions."
short_description: From its stunning landscapes to its characteristic wildlife, Mississippi Acres Preserve holds a special place in the hearts of hunters. Now, after devastating flooding, warden Immi Davis needs your help to get the community back on their feet – and figure out what’s been going bump in the night.

reviews.review_text:
I don't know how I didn't try this reserve for such a long time. It's alright. I don't get the "mixed" reviews (it's probably the classic "we've played it on release and we found all the bugs", which this game is famous for (and you should definitely NOT play any reserve right after release), e.g. it seems that the boats were not available for easier/faster crossing of the waters on release). You'll get the obligatory Southern accents, slide guitar blues theme, rich white people drama, racoons grubbing trash bins, n' all that immersive stuff.

There's also, unsurprisingly, cotton fields. There ain't no Sharps rifle in this game, but you could try to pretend you're re-enacting this scene with the Hawken .50 caliber muzzle-loader (paywalled) as well:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rqUcZ8fRdKs

1o slaves made free / 1o.

P.S.
I almost forgot to mention that the USA is probably the most unique rich country on the Earth — where climate change and the civil rights movement are "way too controversial". *massive facepalm*

This is my first and maybe only review. I felt compelled to say something after reading the other reviews for this map. I haven't regretted my purchase for a second. As a Florida man (don't judge), I have to say I very much enjoyed the scenery and wildlife. It reminds me a lot of home and I see myself spending many more hours in this small but detailed map. I love hunting whitetail and hogs in the American South and I've gotten a whole bunch of joy out of it thus far. Before buying I read the reviews about Miss Chicago but it didn't make me hesitate for a second. I shut off the dialog years ago as soon as I heard the stereotypical folksy baritone of the wannabe Sam Elliot that came with Layton Lakes. Honestly I don't understand why anyone leaves it on. It's a game about hunting, not listening to grandpa talk about whatever grandpas talk about... Buy this DLC if you want a similar experience to hunting in the American Southeast. It's really a lovely place if you take the time to appreciate it, and it's super fun to pump a gator full of 12 gauge slugs. I haven't experienced one single moment of misery because I've ignored the dialogue from the very beginning. That said, if they hired John DiMaggio for a future map I'd reconsider my position.

I enjoyed Mississippi Acres, but it was made more enjoyable for me with the German Shorthaired Pointer dlc. Overall, it's a fun map that I plan to revisit. 

+This map brings beautiful areas with my favorites being the grasslands. 
+The bobwhite quail were fun to hunt with the pointing dog dlc. 
+The foxes and rabbits were a fun challenge. However, I never would have been able to find them with the Pointer dlc. 
+I enjoyed the story for this dlc.

This is my favorite map they've released to date, I LOVE it.  I really like the selection of animals, and I love the swampy biomes.  The story quests were fun and well thought out, and the game is all super fun to hunt.  I think it's a huge shame that people don't like this map, I really love the swamp, and hunting gators, racoons, birds, and pigs, and hope to see more like this.  I think small game is underrated and is a lot of fun.

For years I've had Mississippi as my 2nd least favorite map of cotw and it still would be, if feeders didn't get added. Since feeders got added it's now a top tier whitetail map because of how the whitetail home range is and it's been quite fun for me. Also with post feeders working on rabbits It's been fun to hunt them too.

I got it on sale for 63% off, which made it just shy of $3. For that price, I guess it's decent and wouldn't say hard pass at $3, as I love the environment. 

The storyline is race-baiting trash. All southern whites are bad; all hail female POC! That is the storyline. 

The map is very small, so not a ton of exploration. Not a lot of variance, either. 

Unless you get it on a heavy discount as well, pass on this. Even with a heavy discount, it's nothing more than acceptable at best.
Love this DLC but it crashes way too much, i turned down my graphics and it still crashes.

Yeah...the negative reviews are pretty much spot-on. And really, again, a Chicagoan math teacher? What're we doin' boss.

Its fun to hunt alligators from time to time, coming back here and setting up on a river can be satisfying, and you probably will make decent money, but...it feels like you're just visiting, there's no grounding to the environment and the culture around it except for when you get put in the ground by a 'gator. Which is a shame because every DLC I have played 'til now has been very much the opposite, even the Finnish one which seems to have substantially less dialogue than I was looking forward to feels substantially more intimate and entwined with the world and culture: this one is somehow 10x less grounded, and kind of just feels like you showed up without a permit to some poor abandoned region of Mississippi and just started poaching.

I really don't get why the narrator is both a Chicagoan and a math teacher as well...it seems no one can understand that internal decision, and it really negatively affected the experience of the map just because...like...who is this lady in my ear talking about what's lit and what isn't? Ma'am, I'm 20 feet from an alligator and I left my Glock 17 at home, there is literally no way you could help me other than by watching a nature documentary RIGHT NOW or opening up wikipedia and trying to feed me potentially relevant information.

If its on sale, its worthwhile just because of the atmosphere of some spots, I will say that much, and 'gator hunting is very fun if you're as goofy as I am, but even being the embodiment of goofy and someone who is easily entertained will only keep you here 3 hours at a time (with friends mind you). Whereas I have sat atop a single mountain in Silver Peaks for like 7 hours before I ever moved any further than my kills were from my seat in the sky. Layton lake likewise, Yukon likewise, Medved likewise, Nepal likewise...honestly, even just a Georgian or Mississippian woman as the narration would have made this DLC about 3 - 5x better, and had you let her write the script, I'd have bought this DLC before any other.

If you want a diamond alligator taxidermy, this map makes it easier than others. Buuuuttt the map is super small, feels lazily slapped together, the storyline sucks and feels very unfinished, and certain aspects of the storyline glitch or dont work right. 

If its on sale for like $2, get it. If not, don't waste your money.
[h2] this DLC IS HORRID [h2] too much brush, feels like there is only 3 animals, and everything else is bad.

name: theHunter: Call of the Wild™ - Yukon Valley
description: Referred to by some as “The Last Frontier” and “The Land of the Midnight Sun”, it’s hard to deny that Alaska is a place of untamed natural beauty. Under the watchful gaze and devoted care of warden Jim Murray, Yukon Valley has become a haven for knowledgeable hunters and ecologists, looking to maintain the delicate balance of the ecosystem. With its breathtaking environments that stretch from the rolling hills of blood-coloured moss, to the snow-covered mountains framing the reserve; Yukon Valley is spectacular to behold. YUKON VALLEY’S FAUNA The dazzlingly coloured biomes of Yukon Valley are teeming with equally vibrant wildlife. Catch the Plains Bisons, Caribou and Moose grazing in the soft light of the dawn. Silently stalk Red Foxes and Harlequin Ducks, before they are startled and take flight. Stay out of sight as you track a roaming Grizzly Bear, always wary that it might charge you if it feels threatened. Stick around, and you might even hear a pack of Gray Wolves howl under the glow of the moon. LUSH AND VIBRANT FLORA Considered one of the most visually stunning places on Earth; the Yukon region sports a series of dramatic landscapes, serving as a backdrop to a rich history. From the lush spruce forests to the red foothills, every corner of this reserve is filled with colour and life. It pays to be wary of the weather though, as the familiar scenery can quickly transform into a blanket of freshly fallen snow. As you trek through the eerie, twisted remains of a burnt forest, the seemingly endless rolling plains and the crisp yellow leaves of the woodlands you can’t help but admire the magic that exists out there. .300 CANNING MAGNUM Developed by Alaskan gunsmiths for moose hunting, the .300 Canning Magnum is the go-to rifle for large game in the Yukon Region. Favored by marksmen and hunters alike for its accuracy, the .300 Magnum cartridge is effective for long-range hunting up to a distance of 400 meters. MISSIONS AND MEASURES Yukon Valley, just like its preceding reserves, will span a massive 25 square miles (64 square kilometers) and it will be your decision whether you decide to simply explore the reserve, carving your own path, or if you pursue the thrilling new story with 10 new narrative missions, and 42 side missions. Through the knowledge, instincts and hard work of its warden Jim Murray; Yukon Valley’s ecosystem and inhabitants have survived under his care. The balance is a delicate one though, and the reserve’s fate is uncertain, even with Jim’s care. With the permafrost retreating from Alaska, predators beginning to unbalance the delicate natural order, and tree-eating parasites threatening forests recently damaged by a forest fire, Jim is struggling to keep up. It will now fall on you to help him maintain the health and unique, untamed appeal of Yukon Valley. SHARED EXPERIENCE Just like our other paid DLCs, if a player doesn’t own it they can still join a session in Yukon Valley at no cost by simply finding players that have purchased it and joining their game. It’s then up to you whether you compete or cooperate.
short_description: Referred to by some as “The Last Frontier” and “The Land of the Midnight Sun”, it’s hard to deny that Alaska is a place of untamed natural beauty. Under the watchful gaze and devoted care of warden Jim Murray, Yukon Valley has become a haven for knowledgeable hunters and ecologists, looking to maintain the delicate balance of the...

reviews.review_text:
This is a beautiful map. Areas like the burned forest and the orange fields are some of the most memorable in the entire game. But that's where the strengths of this map beging and end for me.

The grey wolves are cool, but outside of them, the species selection is very forgettable. The biggest disappointment for me were the grizzly bears. They are 1) Really ugly 2) too passive, common and unthreatening. In the mornings, you can just find them sitting in the middle of a herd of caribou drinking, like they are just another passive herbivore. They could have been way more scary if they were rarer and they could actually pose a threat to you and the animals around them. The boring species selection is the reason I have not returned to this map once after completing the story and unlocking all the structures.

Another pet peeve I have with this map is the weather system. The occasional snowfall is beautiful, but it should be toned down a bit. It's not uncommon for the snow to come in, melt and come back 5 times or more during one in-game day. It's immersion-breaking and almost silly at times.

Despite all of this, I still recommend this map for everyone as it is really beautiful and my gripes were rather personal.
Awesome extra area!

Probably the most fun map. Fending off packs of wolves with a shotgun in the middle of the night is a top experience. This map has a great variety of animals on it. Also, the snowfall mechanic is great.
BEST MAP EVER it has a TON of animals to kill and I love how it looks
The map is beautiful. But the grizzlies look disgusting. I was very dissapointed. Still worth it, because the views are unbelievable.
bug after bug after bug.....
Geese listening simulator.

Meh... gorgeous new reserve but lacks additional unlocks for longtime players to strive for. The main missions didn't lead players through much of the zone, the story seemed to be getting interesting but ended abruptly and unsatisfying with no climactic hunt. Goose hunting was great, and being stalked by grey wolves was very satisfying, but grizzlies seemed weak, dumb, and timid. The weather effects were cool but I wished the snow was more impactful.

please delete this airplane, i can hear him motor sound trow the all map, its enoing me
пожалуйста удалите самолет с карты, звук его мотора слышен на всей карте и это очень раздражает
hi .. does this Yukon Valley have 300 CANNING MAGNUM RIFLE ???

name: theHunter: Call of the Wild™ - New England Mountains
description: Welcome to New England Mountains! Grab your gear and get ready to explore the majestic Appalachian Mountains! Pit your wits against a new Great One, discover three refined animals, and check in with an old friend in this beautiful location. Hunt in stunning surroundings Covering roughly a quarter of the state of New Hampshire, as well as portions of Western Maine, the White Mountains sit in the northern Appalachians. Featuring rugged and varied landscapes, this exciting map gives Hunters a wonderful new area to explore. Spend some time in the woods, out by the river, or at the lakeside. Then find a good spot and hunker down to scope out the wildlife – and just enjoy this perfect opportunity to sit back and enjoy the unique environment around you… Discover refined prey and rich countryside On the uplands and through the trees of the New England Mountains, Hunters have the unique chance of laying eyes on three Great Ones. Not only that, the reserve also features some beautifully refined prey. These improvements can be seen across the noble Whitetail Deer, the beautiful Red Fox, and the majestic Moose. That doesn’t make things easy though – prey is offered a hugely varied scenery to hide in. Mature forest, flowing water, and a landscape shaped by glaciers offers a backdrop of deciduous woodland, and dramatic outcrops. Whether you choose to wait out the wildlife in alpine meadows, or in deciduous and evergreen woodland, the setting of New England Mountains gives you the chance to take some time out and relax. Plan your trips and tactics New England Mountains is teeming with life. Hunters will need to plan carefully when heading out onto the peaks. Whether you’re exclusively going after a Great One, or just wandering into the wilds to explore, players can customize the look of their favorite weapons with the two included cosmetics to give them a distinctly New Hampshire feel. This is a perfect way to show off their custom gear before venturing out in search of that elusive and prestigious prey.
short_description: Welcome to New England Mountains! Grab your gear and get ready to explore the majestic Appalachian Mountains! Pit your wits against a new Great One, discover three refined animals, and check in with an old friend in this beautiful location.

reviews.review_text:
This is my favourite DLC map for this game. It was also the first one I bought.
My reason is simple; the [h3] VIBES [/h3] are simply unmatched. The map is beautiful and slow-paced, and it has a good variety of animals.

But that´'s a very subjective reason for a recommendation, right? Let me try to be a little more objective:
This map is not a grinder's map. It's simply too thick and animals are not abundant. A lot of the open areas are steep hills surrounded by forest, making it difficult to set up zones with good visibility.
If you're a grinder who is always looking for the next top rated trophy, you're probably not going to have a fun time on this map. 
But if you're anything like me and you just enjoy a nice hyggelig walk through a beautifully crafted landscape, letting hunting oppotunities find you, this map is the best in the game.

A lot of people have had their complaints about this map, but I really think it deserves a fair shot. I actually had a blast with it, especially as someone living in New England. There are a bunch of fun activities to do and some cool landmarks to explore. Sure, it doesn’t add any new animals, but it makes up for that with a good mix of game options, from little ducks and rabbits to bigger ones like moose and bears.

As a North-north eastern new Englander, this map feels like home. When I step outside in fall, this is how it looks. I can smell the air, the leaves, feel the wind, smell the streams. People may not like it because you are so loud on the forest floor, or that animals are everywhere and get spooked before you can see them. But that is the North East New England woods! If you came up where to hunt, this is the experience you can expect (minus a few in game features)

Hunting Whitetail is just how you would find it in game, they walk up to you curious to see what is sitting there- until you move then they dash away.

Bobcat move and listen for things for a long time until they are sure, and the range of when you spook them is accurate.

I love that turkey's fly through the tree branches and how good their camo is.

Over all, very happy as a New Englander to have bought this DLC and more so that it is here, not many accurate New England maps in video games.

I'm convinced that people who hate this map can't hunt on thicker and more hill-y terrains. This map is almost perfection and by far the most beautiful map in the entire game. I'm a bit biased because I'm a New Englander myself, but it comes with an amazing firearm and a good amount of species as well. The same people who hate this map are the same people who are hurrr durrring on Silver Ridge Peaks with the Arzyna.
GF

This is another very pretty reserve for this game; however, I have to say I was let down quite a bit by it. It is extremely similar to the base game reserves with basically identical animals to hunt. Additionally, the main missions for this reserve are the shortest I've played so far (only took me 2 hours) and has zero actual hunting involved. There is hunting side missions, but nothing in the main. I love this game, but I just can't give this DLC a positive review. I just want the newer reserves to push the boundaries a bit more with missions, species, and environment.

-Warning-
BUGGED MISSIONS UNFIXED FOR YEARS. 
-Warning-

This is the only DLC I recommend NOT buying under any conditions, avoid even on sale or bundled.

It has bugged uncompleteable quests that the developers have chosen not to prioritize fixing in several years now.

If you chose to buy this, play the missions at your own risk or spend time researching how to avoid the bug.

Lots of bugs in this map, and sadly they are still not fixed.

When quick-tavel to "Forgotten Pond Cabin" you instead are placed at Willey Hut.
Cannot complete final stap in "Cats and Cradles" quest as i cannot interact with camp-fire.
Cannot complete "New england Landmarks" quest as multiple landmarks dont trigger when traveling to quest marker.
Also north-east outside map borders 6x watch towers and 3x cabins markers on map you cannot visit.

Suggest you skip this DLC.
"New England Landmarks" mission still bugged after 2.5 years of a very known issue (and also discussed), the devs still dont wanna fix this after all this time and feedback, I find it unbelievable.
Bunch of main missions dont work because they have not fixed them yet. I would not recommend.

name: theHunter: Call of the Wild™ - Hunter Power Pack
description: Take your big-game hunting to the next level with the Hunter Power Pack. This pack provides hunters with three unique bolt-action rifles known for their long-range precision and powerful performance. New Weapons Each rifle is compatible with two ammunition types and a variety of sights. Tsurugi LRR .338 Get your hands on one of the most revered long-range rifles around. Ideal for long-range precision shots and fitted with a detachable 5-round-capacity magazine, this is a great choice for taking down big or dangerous game. Malmer 7mm Magnum Arguably one of the most popular hunting rifles ever made. The synthetic stock with a carbon steel barrel makes for a lightweight rifle, and combined with its tried and true performance, this is a highly versatile firearm that hunters across the world have come to rely on in the wild. Olsson Model 23 Every inch of this modern bolt-action rifle is designed and manufactured with the utmost care and premium components. Chambered in the popular .308, and sporting features such as a fluted bolt design for smoother operation, a carbon fibre stock, and a stainless steel barrel, this is an excellent pick for any medium to big game. New Cosmetics The Hunter Power Pack also includes new Paints, Sprays, Materials, and Camos to customize your weapons.
short_description: Take your big-game hunting to the next level with the Hunter Power Pack, which contains three bolt-action rifles known for their long-range precision and powerful performance.

reviews.review_text: The best bolt action rifles I sot in the game
Malmer 7mm is a better version of base game 7mm. Tsrugi is a better version of the base game 338. Get on deep discount.

Unlike their Reserve DLC's which can be buggy messes they never fix, you can count on these firearm DLCs to work right.

Then when asking is one of the weapon DLCs worth it you're asking what you'll use of the usually 3 items. 

In this one oh yeah, you'll likely use 2 if not all 3 of the guns. It's fine for it's cost. 

The Tsurugi and Malmer are straight improvements over previous guns, one you may not have (was a Reserve DLC gun IIRC).

The Olsen... it's not the best at what it does but the better one is from another weapon DLC you may not own and it's better in one category than that gun so even if you have both the Olsen isn't a waste.

It's not pay to win, you don't need these, but it's a pay for slightly improved performance at a very fair price (note: On Sale! Wait til a sale).
yes
Malmer 7mm is amazing flexibility. Almost too good. Kills game from 4-9. Has 3 shots over the 1 shot from the base game 7mm

Posted a bug report but the .338 in this pack is bugged to where it barely breaks skin until then if this is the reason you are getting it I would hold off and use the single shot .338 until the bug is fixed.

When I bought it I thought it was good but now I realise it is nothing special. The ammo is way to expensive like we are talking for 10 338. rounds its about 1000 and for the 7mm its about 800. This is only for expierienced players, new plays will miss some shots and get some bad shots. And every time you pull the trigger with the 338. 100 comes flying out the barrel and if you miss there's 100 gone. Just get the high caliber pack or Modern rifles
i wouldn't recommend it you can get weapons better then it within an hour of playing.

This DLC is mostly a scam.

The 7mm in this pack is only a 3.5 not a 4.5 and as such is not capable of shooting higher tier animals. 

it is a total piece of junk.

Many reviews are saying it's a 4.5 but i just bought the pack and checked in game and its only a 3.5.

I suspect the devs are smurfing reviews as the ammount of false information here is appalling.

The guns are awesome which is why I hate this pack so much. 

Normally it would take you hours of grinding on the .243 Ranger to unlock the 7mm and ages to unlock the .338. But with this pack nope, here you go. Get your Class 9 gun for free with polymer tip rounds to boot at the start of the game. Oh and its bolt-action to boot so you can make follow up shots or butcher entire herds. 

This pack is the very definition of pay for power up.

name: theHunter: Call of the Wild™ - Weapon Pack 2
description: A brand new shipment of weapons has just been delivered to our wardens, and they’re excited to present an incredible combination of new world finesse and old-school reliability with the Grelck Drilling Gun, the Solokhin MN1890 and the Mangiafico 410 / 45 Colt Revolver. Grelck Drilling Gun: Versatile and elegant, this three-barreled combination gun sports two side-by-side 16 gauge shotgun barrels atop a single rifle barrel chambered for the powerful 9.3x74R big game cartridge. If you’re hunting waterfowl and happen to find a moose, or stalking red deer and stumble upon a goose, this gun has you covered. Versatility: A shotgun and rifle wrapped up in one, the Grelck Drilling Gun allows you to quickly swap between the 16 gauge barrels and the 9.3x74R to maximize your chances of downing the spotted animal. Utilize the 16 gauge birdshot, buckshot and slug to down small-to-medium sized animals, or switch over to either the Soft Point of Polymer Tip for the 9.3x74R to handle larger and more dangerous game. Falcon 3-9x44 Drilling Scope: Quickly dial in your shots from a distance with this premium, custom-made scope for the Grelck Drilling Gun. Weatherproof and built to withstand shot after shot of high-powered cartridges and shells, this scope covers has you covered for most hunting situations. Classic, Select and Carbon Fiber Colour Variations: The Grelck Drilling Gun comes in three different different color variations: Classic, Select and Carbon Fiber Solokhin MN1890: The Solokhin MN1890 is a combat-tested rifle that has left its mark on the pages of history books across the world. Tested in harsh environments, weathered by over a century of use, and a great hunting companion to this day, the Solokhin MN1890 is bolt-action reliability perfected. Capable of hitting targets at vast ranges, with a well-deserved notoriety for deadly penetration, this is a rifle to swear by. Range and Power: The Solokhin MN1890 is renowned for its reliability in any situation, offering a powerful weapon designed to take on medium-sized targets from an impressive distance. With a maximum range of about 550m and a 5-shot magazine capacity, the medium recoil is offset by the high target penetration. Combine with the Ascent, Hyperion, or Argus rifle scope, and you will be a hunter to be reckoned with. Assembly Line, Frontline, and Defense Line Colour Variations: The Solokhin MN1890 comes in three different different color variations: Assembly Line, Frontline, and Defense Line Mangiafico 410 / 45 Colt Revolver: This sleek, modern revolver is the ideal companion for hunters looking for versatility at short distances. Load with .410 shotshells for hunting small game and waterfowl or with .45 Long Colt for species such as roe deer and springbok. A small handgun for all small game. Optimal for Small to Medium Targets: The .45 Cold hard-cast bullet is both powerful and accurate, making it especially suitable for small to medium-sized game at close range. The .410 gauge shell, on the other hand, is advantageous for rapid short-range shooting, aimed at small, fast-moving targets such as rabbits and ducks. With its flexibility and small size, the Mangiafico 410 / 45 Colt Revolver offers the huntings variety of our bows at a fraction of the cost. Polished Steel, Muertos and Flat Black Colour Variations: The Mangiafico 410 / 45 Colt Revolver comes in three different different color variations: Polished Steel, Muertos and Flat Black.
short_description: A brand new shipment of weapons has just been delivered to our wardens, and they’re excited to present an incredible combination of new world finesse and old-school reliability with the Grelck Drilling Gun, the Solokhin MN1890 and the Mangiafico 410 / 45 Colt Revolver.

reviews.review_text:
Worth it. Definitely worth it on sale, which it often is, definitely worth it when it's part of bundles.
The drilling gun is a unique multi-gun-in-one weapon.
The .410's ability to shot a shotgun shell in a pistol is a unique trick as well (and it might be compatible with some new ammo from a newer pack, unsure yet, but even if not, it's good as is).
I've never been big on the Solohkin but I owned some other guns with a similar purpose I found superior, I know other players who don't own as many weapon packs/DLC's as I do who've written well of it, stats are solid, and it suits their needs.

Given this pack is almost always on sale or sometimes sale and bundle, it's worth the buck or two it costs.

Very disappointed that there is only 1 round for the mosin, which has very meager penetration.  It is equal in stats to the starting rifle's worse round, which is a much smaller caliber than the mosin. It should be a much more powerful round, similar to the .30-06.  Bummer.  I purchased it for the disappointing mosin, but the other two firearms are neat and fun.

I guess I can recommend it if you want a couple of gimmicky, fun guns and a very sloppy, sad mosin. 

2 out of 3 stars.

Do you need a drilling rifle, no. Do you WANT a drilling rifle YES! The .410 was quite disappointing, but the solokhin Is VERY usefull though, and has probably the cheapest ammo in the game. (besides .22). All in all: 3 stars
Needs a fishing minigame.
good aim
I can't access the guns or cosmetics, even after it says it's installed.

This pack is a perfect illustration of power creep; Where certain game features rise and fall in value due to overarching changes in the meta and the subsequent releases of other features/weapons. While the weapons in it are interesting they are completely outshone everything that has come since its release that the only reason to get it would be completely your collection, play with some unique toys but only on a 60% off sale.

Its no better embodied by this pack's lemon, the Solokhin which once upon a time was thought to be the most powerful rifle in the game due to its ability to score one hit kill spine shots. However within the change of the scoring system which now emphasized humaneness, it has fallen in value to near worthlessness. While heavy as most generalist rifles, its ammunition is underpowered for its rated Classes; Its meant to take stuff from small deer to bears but statistically the .22H, a varmint rifle round, hits harder. Its a rare weapon with actually no redeeming qualities.

With the recent class changes the Mangiafico I feel just got a new lease on life since several classes of animal that used to inhabit Class 3-4 have fallen to Class 2-3, which is a peculiar void in the game's weapon lineup. More or less it allows you to carry a birding gun and a varmint gun in one tiny package , albeit at steep compromises in performance. That said the .410 is no .12 gauge, having a much smaller pattern, that the argument can be made to use a .22LR rifle or pistol instead unless you are hunting pheasants that need to be shot in flight. The .45 Colt bullets are strange in that while their Penetration leaves much to be desired, their expansion is comparable to that of some rifles. Which means that you will have to place your shots carefully but if score on a vital the animal is guaranteed to drop then and there. 

Finally we have the Drilling which is pretty much the raison d'tere for getting this DLC; Its a one-gun solution that covers Class 1-9, allowing you to carry every lure, blind and gadget you want alongside it, at the cost of some severe tradeoffs. For Classes 1-4 you will have to rely on the shotgun, so brush up on your stalking since you'd only be effective at roughly 50-75 yards while for 5-9 your rifle is essentially a 7mm but with a class 5 floor capped at 150 yards. Also the gun is currently suffering from a bug where switching between functions causes the ammo count to zero unless said ammo was loaded using the inventory screen and not the ammo wheel (yeah really weord bug), negating one of its selling points which is being able to follow up your rifle shot with two slugs in quick succession. Wait for EW to fix it before even considering this pack.

The drilling rifle is completely broken. The front sight disappears whenever you switch to the rifle barrel and the rear sight flips up, so there isn't actually any indication of where the gun is aiming. Seriously needs to be fixed.
Extremely buggy game not being fixed.
Absolutely saddened I bought this expansion pack. The Rifle doesn't work, which is the reason I wanted it in the first place.

name: theHunter: Call of the Wild™ - Weapon Pack 1
description: The wardens Connie, Doc and Alena have received a shipment with brand new weapons - the Virant .22LR Semi-Automatic Rifle, the Crosspoint CB-165 and the Houyi Recurve Bow. Virant .22LR Semi-Automatic Rifle: Developed by Slovenian gunsmiths, the Virant is the go-to rifle for varmint control and target shooting. The .22LR’s low recoil and superb design makes this rifle a perfect starting point for people learning the basics of shooting. Small Game Hunting: In addition to it’s low recoil, this semi-automatic rifle allows you to fire multiple small rounds, making it a great choice for rabbit hunting and target practice. Perfect for Jackrabbits and other small game, the Virant is not used to hunt larger animals. Sight Compatibility: By default you can use the Virant’s iron sight: However, for improved aiming at a distance this rifle supports multiple scope attachments: Red Dot Sight, 1-4x24mm Rifle Scope, 4-8x42mm Rifle Scope, and 8-16x50mm Rifle Scope. Klasika and Express: The Virant .22LR Semi-Automatic Rifle comes in two different different color variations: Klasika and Express. Crosspoint CB-165: With 165 foot-pounds of draw weight, this precision-crafted crossbow sports enough power to take down even the largest game animals. With a beautiful woven carbon fiber barrel, a built-in rope-cocking device, and a 4-arrow quiver, the Crosspoint CB-165 is ideal for short-range hunting. Hawken 1-5X30 Crossbow Scope: This premium scope is designed for crossbows shooting 300 feet per second or faster. With three duplex crosshairs and variable zoom settings up to 5x, this is the perfect crossbow scope for any situation. Range and Power: The Crosspoint CB-165 Crossbow is more powerful and offers greater range than any other bow currently in the store. However, as with everything in hunting, it’s a trade-off. This crossbow is rather large and heavy, requiring significant space in your backpack. Additionally, reloading the crossbow is slower than most other weapons so make sure you make each shot count! Classic, Arctic, Dark Star & Warden: The Crosspoint CB-165 comes in four different different color variations; Classic, Arctic, Dark Star and Warden. Houyi Recurve Bow: In Chinese mythology, when 10 suns rose from Earth and turned the world into a barren wasteland, the archer Houy shot down 9 of the 10 suns, leaving only the last one. This bow takes its name from that myth. Based on the designs of traditional Chinese Qing recurve bows, the Houyi Recurve Bow has ample penetration power and can take down medium game at short ranges. Spook fewer animals in your surroundings: This recurve bow gives you an unparalleled advantage compared to rifles, handguns, shotguns or even crossbows. When taking a shot, the longbow is much quieter and will spook a smaller number of animals within a limited radius. But as always, this is a trade-off; although the longbow is currently the quietest weapon in the game, it offers limited range compared to many other weapons. Hidden Dragon, Moonlight, Snakeskin and Default: The Houyi Recurve Bow comes in four different different color variations: Default, Hidden Dragon, Moonlight and Snakeskin.
short_description: The wardens Connie, Doc and Alena have received a shipment with brand new weapons - the Virant .22LR Semi-Automatic Rifle, the Crosspoint CB-165 and the Houyi Recurve Bow.

reviews.review_text: It helps make my own bow and I love the models
Worth just for the virant and crossbow, on deep discount. Otherwise should have been a QOL/feature update in the game rather than a paywalled DLC.
Virant .22LR. The best weapon for class One is worth already. Good luck shooting rabbits (:

The Crosspoint. What is there to say, the one crossbow option in the game. 

Excepting a rare few missions, useable in any situation you'd need a bow. Given this is one of the older DLC's, is very often on sale with extreme mark down, and occasionally bundled for even less, that alone is worth it for your arsenal. That it comes with the crossbow scope as well, which takes some adjusting but once you get the hang of it is useful, is icing.

The bow and the rifle, especially as the rifle is surpassed by later .22's that were added, are more give or take but they're just filler on top of the crossbow and sight and there's nothing wrong with having access to them as well.

If you like this game, wait until it's on a 50% or more off (often a bit more, iirc I got it at 65-75%), grab this and enjoy the option to crossbow hunt.
Not really worth it but I love me a .22LR

Development team is extremely active, they're just doing their own thing instead of listening to their fan base. There's a request with thousands of people backing it for the game to add a pause function. The game is built on the engine that Just Cause 3 runs on, and that game has pause. This game for some reason does not have pause. That one glaring deficiency makes it an extremely inaccessible game to people with anything other than unlimited uninterruptible time to play games on their hands. And the style of game makes it so that if you can't pause when you've got a shot lined up and you have to get up for whatever reason, you lose your shot and it takes a long time to get back to where you can get that shot.
I did not get any of the sights with the DLC purchase.  Very disappointed as the description says it comes with scopes
It isnt worth it for the amount you get from it. Only buy when its on discount.
10/22 is okay but the AR15 in 22lr is better imo less ammo capacity by 1/3 of the 10/22 but I think the AR15 in 22lr is better again imo though.

The scoped crossbow is great but the recurve bow is hard to aim. The semi auto .22LR was useful but the .22H is better. This pack is no longer good because of the newer packs having better alternatives.

name: theHunter: Call of the Wild™ - Tents & Ground Blinds
description: The reserve wardens Conni and Doc have received a new shipment of equipment and thanks to Robert Sommer’s connections, they were able to get their hands on a tent and a ground blind. These new items will make your hunting experience in both Hirschfelden and Layton Lake District even more complete. TENT: ELITE 2 TENT Setting up tents will create fast travel points almost anywhere you wish (although remember that in-game camping fees apply) which makes it great for exploring new areas and traveling to parts of the map that are particularly dense in the animal species you would like to hunt. The ELITE 2 TENT is a two-man tent suitable for all weather conditions. The patented high-tech Mosquito Wall 2.0, tested under severe conditions in northern Scandinavia, is proven to decrease user blood loss by almost 5% compared to its previous generation. That’s a lot of blood! This award-winning product also includes a large storage container that'll turn your mobile home into a fully functional outpost. The tent makes it possible to access the store and purchase new gear for your inventory as well as rest up after a hard day of stalking and tracking. Just remember that placing, as well as fast traveling, to a tent creates noise which means you might spook some of the nearby animals. Additional Characteristics: A maximum of 8 tents can be placed in each reserve at the same time. You can dismantle and move a tent at any point. Available in: White, Blue, Pink, Green and Camo. GROUND BLIND: INCOGNITO DELUXE GROUND BLIND The ground blind offers the ultimate portable camouflage but it also functions as weather protection. Once deployed, it provides excellent visibility concealment and unrestricted shooting angles in almost every direction. The most notable features include the ability for it to be deployed almost anywhere, decreased visibility, and room for up to 2 hunters inside. Please remember that setting up a ground blind does create noise and might spook nearby animals. Additional Characteristics: A maximum of 16 ground blinds can be deployed in each reserve at the same time. You can dismantle and move a ground blind at any point. Available in: Timber and Conifer.
short_description: The reserve wardens Conni and Doc have received a new shipment of equipment and thanks to Robert Sommer’s connections, they were able to get their hands on a tent and a ground blind. These new items will make your hunting experience in both Hirschfelden and Layton Lake District even more complete.
supported_languages: Simplified Chineselanguages with full audio support

reviews.review_text:

I see that some people have given this DLC negative reviews only because they see that this should be a free feature in the game. I say that these abilities work like dream (no bugs, glitches or any other problems), so paying some euros for these abilities is absolutely not a problem. Ignore this barrier compared to what you will receive!
Pretty solid dlc, def worth it

the game is basically unplayable without this or atv, its too much of an advantage over base game

Pretty much a required dlc sadly. Absolutely zero reason to be a paid expansion.

name: theHunter: Call of the Wild™ - High-Tech Hunting Pack
description: Hunting in a stealthy fashion has been easier thanks to the latest equipment delivery to the wardens - the Night Hunting Pack. Through a selected mix of high quality tools you will now be able to track your target with precision in the dark. 8X50 Night Vision Binoculars Watch the reserves come to life at night through the Night Vision binoculars, designed to increase both visibility and contrast in the dark while leaving depth perception and shapes unaltered. From the largest of predators to the smallest of prey, all animals near to you will have a hard time hiding from your keen gaze. GenZero 1-4x20 Night Vision Rifle Scope: Featuring a high-quality image intensifier tube, a compact design and waterproof battery compartment, the GenZero 1-4x20 Night Vision Rifle Scope will allow you to see beyond the expected in night-time conditions. A long-range IR illuminator completes the package allowing you to acquire your targets from safe distances; but be aware that the effectiveness of this technology is particularly affected by fog and adverse weather conditions. Koter CB-65 Bow With performances that are exactly in-between the Bearclaw Lite CB-60 and the Hawk Edge CB-70, the Koter CB-65 is a versatile compound bow that can be used in every situation. With a delicate balance between recoil and accuracy, this bow gives its best when hunting medium sized game, but can also be deadly accurate against small and big game, if used with the right arrows. Brightsight Rangefinder Bow Sight & Tracer Arrows: The technology of this sight helps the hunter with the biggest challenge in archery hunting: distance estimation. With the single press of a button, the Brightsight calculates the distance of the target and projects a precise, virtual LED pin that is only visible to the archer, without the clutter of multiple physical pins. At night, the Tracer Arrows will allow you to track down your prized harvest once you’ve made that first successful shot, thanks to a flashing red LED on the nock. In the off chance your prey manages to escape, you can simply follow the red light to its source and take the animal down. Variation: The Koter CB-65 bow is available in two variations: Orpheus and Hephaestus.
short_description: Hunting in a stealthy fashion has been easier thanks to the latest equipment delivery to the wardens - the Night Hunting Pack.

name: theHunter: Call of the Wild™ - Labrador Retriever
description: Say hello to the Labrador Retriever, your faithful new friend and hunting partner! A partner you can count on Your canine friend can fetch any rabbit, hare, waterfowl, or upland bird on land or in water, allowing you to continue hunting without interruption. When you command your companion to retrieve, it will diligently pick up harvests around you by order of priority, starting with the one you are aiming at. Discover the wilderness together and share the joy of hunting! Build a lasting bond Strengthen the connection to your dog by petting it, giving it treats, or playing with it, and grow its efficiency by hunting together. When it’s time to head back to the lodge after a good day’s hunt, you can count on your faithful companion to be there wagging its tail. Your unique companion Give your dog a name, choose its fur coat variation, and assign it traits that match your hunting style. You can even add multiple dogs to your roster, making it easy to pick which one to hunt with. The Labrador Retriever is available as both male and female, in seven unique fur coat variations: Black, Yellow, Chocolate, Silver, Charcoal, Fox Red, and Champagne.
short_description: Introducing the Labrador Retriever, your faithful new hunting partner! This popular and versatile breed can fetch a variety of small game on land or in water.

reviews.review_text: I personally Like this DLC, Mostly just biased as i also have a Black Lab IRL, but yeah the dog is a bit buggy but im not that big of a D*ckhead to leave a bad review over bugs,
Invaluable for bird hunters, bugs were fixed. Get on sale or with bundle.
Worth the two euros, but only for small game hunters
it is very useful hunting waterfowl A.K.A (Salzwiesen park)
so bad
As it turned out, unfortunately, it was a useless purchase.

Out of the three current dogs, this one is undoubtedly the most utterly useless. It's more of a RP thing, almost...? It can fetch hares, rabbits, and birds - anything that's a class 1 - and bring it to you. You know, the thing you are perfectly capable of doing on your own? :/ Really the only benefit is that it's cute and, if you upgrade their swimming ability, thing thing can [i]haul butt[/i] in the water so that you don't have to wait for waterfowl to slowly drift to shore

Also, the face of this dog does not look like a lab at all. Nor really any identifiable dog breed...? I cannot fathom why they didn't reference the actual dog and thought they could just sorta... wing it like this. The paws are also SO bad, I cannot stress this enough. There's entirely too much detail on the toes so that they stand out wicked bad and look like they go all the way up to the wrist! The face I might be able to tell myself I can tolerate, but hot dang, those paws..... stuff of nightmares

$6 bucks is not only highway robbery, but also a spit in the eye while you're at it. If you must have this dog, know that it frequently goes on deep sales where you can get it at like $2. Even that is too much :v

name: theHunter: Call of the Wild™ - High Caliber Weapon Pack
description: Take on the biggest game with the High Caliber Weapon Pack. This package contains three top-grade weapons crafted for pure firepower and maximum range as well as five new cosmetics. This pack contains: - Arzyna .300 Mag Tactical : Designed for both the hunter and marksman, the Arzyna .300 Mag Tactical offers stellar performance. It melds the flexibility of the AR platform with the long-range power of the .300 Magnum, setting new standards in hunting and precision shooting. - .45-70 Jernberg Superior : The Jernberg Superior stands as the go-to pistol for sharpshooters and hunters alike, featuring a powerful single-shot, break-action design. Firing impressive .45-70 caliber rounds, and catering to custom needs with its adaptable scope mount, it's more than a simple tool — it's a testament to fine craftsmanship and precision. - Strandberg 10SA Executive : Whether you're stalking prey in wetlands or participating in competitive shooting, the Strandberg 10GA Semi-Automatic Shotgun ensures unparalleled performance and reliability — every time. - Five New Cosmetics : This pack also includes a sleek mix of exclusive Material and Camo cosmetics that can be applied to selected weaponry and gear in combination with other cosmetics in your inventory.
short_description: Take on the biggest game with the High Caliber Weapon Pack. This package contains three top-grade weapons crafted for pure firepower and maximum range as well as five new cosmetics.

name: theHunter: Call of the Wild™ - ATV SABER 4X4
description: The wardens recently met at a conference for wildlife management, and realized how they have all noticed a buildup of visitors at the entry points of their respective hunting reserves. Due to frequent requests from hunters and to encourage full exploration of the reserves, the wardens have approved the use of All Terrain Vehicles (ATVs) within them – but strictly as a mode of transportation. Using the ATV, hunters and hikers alike will be able to discover more of the hidden beauty lying in wait - and have a great time doing it! ATV: SABER 4X4: The SABER 4X4 gives you the ability to traverse rough terrain with unmatched comfort and style. Experience the rush as it takes you across fields, bogs and hills at breakneck speeds. This ATV is great for exploring and traveling through the reserves in a fast and fun way. The SABER 4X4 can be spawned for free at any outpost or tent. Sharing the SABER 4X4 As owner of this DLC, you can easily share the all-terrain experience with your friends in multiplayer. Hosting or joining a game will allow all participants to spawn and drive vehicles. Everyone can explore the hunting reserve together or for themselves, all thanks to you. Once all DLC owners have left the multiplayer session, no new vehicles can be spawned, but remaining vehicles will stay available for the duration of the multiplayer session.. Steering the SABER 4X4 Whether you want to fully immerse yourself, driving in 1st person or rather have the complete overview of your surroundings in 3rd person, the SABER 4X4 is the vehicle for you. You can change the camera position with the press of a button (default: “C”) which will be saved for your future rides. Show off your ATV from all angles using 3rd person or share your driving expertise from 1st person. We can’t wait to see your results! Jade, Crimson & Charcoal The SABER 4X4 comes with three different color variations, Jade, Crimson and Charcoal. Every hunter can have one active vehicle at a time with no cost for retrieving or respawning. Rather go out with your crimson ATV today? It’s all up to you.
short_description: The wardens recently met at a conference for wildlife management, and realized how they have all noticed a buildup of visitors at the entry points of their respective hunting reserves. To encourage full exploration of the reserves, the wardens have approved the use of All Terrain Vehicles (ATVs) within them – but strictly as a mode of...
supported_languages: Simplified Chineselanguages with full audio support

reviews.review_text: There's no better feeling than using an ATV to chase down and mag dump the local fauna!
Great!

Massive quality of life improvement.

This probably should have been included with the base game. Every other hunting video game I've played had some kind of vehicle for mobility's sake.

That's not to say that this game's reserves don't have great scenery to walk through, but you will be walking for a very long time on foot.

For convenience's sake, pick this one up. Ideally, you pick it up on sale.

2026 REVIEW:

When it comes to essential DLC's to purchase if you are playing "theHunter: Call of the Wild", it's an ATV SABER 4X4.

Some of these DLC Maps are VERY LONG and sometimes these developers of these DLC map add ons conveniently (for them) to put your location so far from where you need to go.  

Sure, you can walk it and using quick teleport and spend a ton of time doing so.  But the ATV SABER 4x4 is a big time saver.

I don't like wasting my time and efficiency is important for me and so this ATV SABER 4X4 is essential.

Also, if you have a dog, riding on an ATV, the dog will follow and no matter if you are traveling super far, the dog will follow and helps the dog increase their level.  So, another plus of owning the ATV.

And it's important to note that there are times where hitting trees will damage your ATV and do it enough times and the ATV will blow up and catch fire.

If you are back into a rest area, you can respawn the ATV, so don't worry about losing a vehicle.  And I like the fact that you get several colors for the ATV as well.

Last but not least, just because you are in a quick ATV doesn't mean you will not get hurt.  Those mountain lions are quick and will take a quick swipe at you and when you see a stampede, if you are caught in that stampede on your ATV, you will more than likely die.

The only thing that I didn't like is if you are on a cliff or steep hill and you have your ATV reposition, sometimes the spawning areas are in areas that you can't reach.   Dislike that.

But for the price you can get this ATV on sale and the time saving you get to get from point A to point B and also improving the skill of your dog, who follows you no matter how far you go, it makes the ATV worth your time!

Recommended!
