name: theHunter: Call of the Wild™  - Ambusher Pack
description: This pack introduces the innovative FOXPRO X360 electronic caller, the powerful Moradi Model 1894 lever-action rifle, and the sleek Stenberg Takedown Recurve Bow - the perfect set of weapons and equipment for effectively ambushing prey in the wild. FOXPRO X360 Electronic Caller Gain the upper hand with the FOXPRO X360, an advanced calling device that comes equipped with a remote for convenient control from a distance and pre-loaded with a wide library of sounds for attracting over 60 species in the game with realistic animal calls. The FOXPRO X360 also uses groundbreaking licensed technology: FOXFUSION, which enables you to simultaneously lure two animals, and FOXBANG, which immediately ceases all calls upon detecting a loud noise. Moradi Model 1894 Get up close and personal with the Moradi Model 1894, where classic design and versatile rail-mounted optics meet the power of .44 Magnum cartridges. Plus, embrace the innovation of modern compatibility by seamlessly sharing ammunition with your revolver sidearm. Stenberg Takedown Recurve Bow Silently hunt your prey with the Stenberg Takedown Recurve Bow. Slightly lighter in draw weight compared to its competition, this short-ranged bow offers excellent handling and accuracy for ethical hunting.
short_description: Experience a strategic new approach to hunting with weapons and equipment designed for effectively ambushing prey in the wild.

reviews.review_text: i bought the dlc and nothing showed up in game can someone help me,but the base game is still good

I get this pack for the electronic caller. Works like a charm and so handy to have pretty much any animal available in 1 unit. I got a red fox 2 minutes after setting it up. Just check the sign for what animals are in the area and good to go.

The weight of the unit is not an issue for me as of yet. There really is no need to use a blind with this for most animals. The drawback as far as hooved animals is that the mating call is used, meaning you will attract females generally. Maybe you draw in a male who want to claim his territory, but generally females so far in my experience.

I have not used the rifle or bow from this pack yet. I generally use modern rifles because they weigh less. Well my preferred ones do.
One of the few packs I believe deserve to be a paid dlc. Get on sale or part of the bundle.

The "star of the show" being the e-caller, is very useful. Not having to carry around a billion manual callers and switch them out on every map is extremely helpful. The range is 200m, though for me animals have come in from up to ~230m away, they react almost instantly but usually dont trot, but slowly walk towards it. The only nitpick i have with it is that it can't actually call all animals, no calling tahr, feral goats, kangaroos and nearly all other animals that dont have callers by default. Its annoying, since its supposed to be an electronic device, but i can look past the cons over all the pros it has. [b]It weighs 3,5kg[/b]

The Moradi model 1894 is in my opinion one of the best rifles you could get your hands on, it has a whopping 70 penetration (with hard cast bullets) and a range of classes 3-8. The handgun bullets provide crazy penetration, BUT its extremely annoying to have to grind 2500 handgun score if you dont use them much, wich is my case. It zeroes only up to 150, but i think that's a good trade for the sheer power it has, and with some skill you can of course take farther shots. [b]It weighs 3kg[/b]

Lastly the recurve bow - personally i'm a big fan of bow hunting, so of course i LOVE this. It's extremely light, [b]weighing only 2kg[/b], its a 65lb bow so its pretty powerful AND it can equip bow sights unlike the other recurve bows (for example - the longbows). 

All the items combined weigh 8,5kg, wich [i]can[/i] become tricky without a backpack or the "pack mule" skill, especially if you carry around a tent like i do, but i think this dlc is 100% worth it.
Not worth paying for, but it's very useful. Paired with Salzwiesen, if you set up this with a waterfowl blind, then birds will just pour around you.
The caller is ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ and the gun is even worse, can't even hit a stationary deer from 70m away
I like the moradi, but the recurve and the caller are just meh.
Very unique design, but weights too much and so why never using it. ://

This is a really great weapon pack, I love the leveraction and I think it's a great weapon.
I don't know why this useless electric box came with it though, but I am sure I can find a use for it.
I recommend it!

name: theHunter: Call of the Wild™ - Bloodhound
description: Meet the Bloodhound, your new best friend and the first ever hunting dog in theHunter: Call of the Wild™. Use it to track the scent of blood from injured prey, match its traits to your hunting style, and forge a bond that will last a lifetime. The Bloodhound is the most efficient and loyal sidekick a hunter could ever want. A NOSY PARTNER The Bloodhound is famous for its outstanding sense of smell. It has roughly 300 million scent receptors, and can pick up blood trails even in the thickest of brush with ease. Putting your dog to work gives it experience, which is used to progress through its 30 companion levels and 15 tracker levels. The more you hunt together, the more efficient it becomes. HUNTER’S BEST FRIEND Your dog’s ability to focus is directly connected to the bond you share. Rewarding your companion by praising, petting, or giving it treats strengthens your bond, so make sure to show your four-legged friend some love after a good day’s hunt! ONE OF A KIND You can name your dog, choose its fur coat variation, and pick what traits it should have. Add multiple dogs to your roster, and pick one to go hunting with. When it’s time to relax at the Trophy Lodge or nearest Outpost, rest assured your active dog will be there to greet you. The Bloodhound is available as both male and female, and comes in six fur coat variations: Black and Tan Fullcoat, Black and Tan Saddle, Liver and Tan Fullcoat, Liver and Tan Saddle, Red and Black Pigmented, Red and Liver Pigmented.
short_description: Adopt a clever new approach to hunting with the Bloodhound by your side. This canine-companion has an incredible sense of smell, tracking scents over large distances.

reviews.review_text:
Okay, so, you know the frustration when you follow the blood splatters and the trail and then they seem to all point back and forth contradicting one another, to the point you can't find any other clues and you're forced to abandon the tracking? It's not often that it happens, but it is beyond frustrating, especially early game. Well, if you know that feeling and hate it as much as I do, then BUY THIS DLC!! I'd been running with a black lab because, well, I used to have a black pit/lab mix that looked all lab. 

But I haven't gotten around to bird hunting yet. So the lab wasn't really doing anything. I'd had the bloodhound DLC from a bundle I'd bought some time back, but had never used it. Until I spent 30+ minutes trying to find an animal that was basically running me around in circles. I was like "why the hell isn't my dog telling me where to go?" then I thought, hey, maybe the bloodhound will track since the lab doesn't. 

Previously I'd assumed that the dogs were all the same, just basically different skins. And that was my ignorance for not looking at the descriptions. I looked through the traits available and realized, each dog has different specialties. And so I swapped the lab for the hound and went off on a hunt.

I head to a lake and see a moose across a small lake from me. I take the shot, was like in the 280-300 yard range. And I work my way around the lake. I know the trails should be easy to find since it was at the water when shot. Sure enough, when I reach the rock formation near where I had wounded the animal, I found blood. Then I go to the dog menu, tell it to track...and...I literally just followed the dog. I didn't look at the phone tracker. I literally just followed the dog, and kept following. And she took me to the animal. It was great.

Every time she leveled up, I would pet her. Every time she would assist with a kill, I'd pet then play with her. She'd follow me whether I was on foot or on the ATV (and she levels up quickly when you're on long ATV rides). I'm personally dumbfounded how anyone is giving this a thumbs down. Maybe they're using mods that affect the AI or such? I have no clue, but I LOVE this DLC.

As you can probably tell from my experience above, I absolutely recommend this DLC (and in my opinion, it's the best dog DLC option overall, though the others are better for specific circumstances). Don't think about it, just get it.

A 2026 REVIEW:  

"theHunter" is a hunting simulation game developed by Expandisove Worlds (originally by its parent company Avalanche Studios).

In 2015, "theHunter: Primal" was released but the game the company is known for is "theHunter: Call of the Wild" released in 2017.

A first person hunting game, the game has a plethora of maps, weapons and many other extra things that make your hunting experience much worthwhile.

With the addition of dogs, these dogs will grow with you in skill but you need to maintain that bond by petting it, playing with your dog and sharing the occasional biscuit.  But you need to keep your dog happy!

Because this dog is essential!  I own all three but my favorite is the Bloodhound.

Some people like the experience of hunting games where you can hit the animal, but you don't know where they escaped to.  With "theHunter: Call of the Wild", this dog can be your best friend by tracking those animals.  Sniffing the blood and looking for the animal's carcass or pointing you to the right direction.

Some say their dogs scare off quarry but you can have your dog stay while you walk and prepare for the hunt, and if you need it, whistle. 

Another fun thing is even if you travel with your ATV, your dog will follow.  Yes, travel by ATV and this dog will travel miles and grow in experience.

But treat this dog like your real dog and show it respect.  For every good deed it does, shake its paw, pet its head, give it a biscuit and this dog will make your hunting experience much more worthwhile and less frustrating.

Recommended!

https://youtu.be/cgMSHdrF74Y

Useful for newer players or those who play with clues off. Would give this a neutral review if possible. Annoying to use near water bodies as he will constantly walk into water and dry himself off. Buy on sale or as part of a better bundle.

Not sure what I'd do without this DLC! Works FANTASTIC. The dogs look great, act realistic, and provide substantial assistance. There's been countless times I've messed up a shot and solely rely on the bloodhound to find the harvest or wounded animal. They even provide great partnership and protection from aggressive animals. (If you so choose for them to have this trait.)
If the animal you're tracking is on the verge of spooking, the dog will alert you before the animal fully takes off.
Even when the wounded animal stops dropping blood, the dog will still track it for you and alert you when its nearby,

I've never been disappointed with this DLC and would highly recommend.
Highly recommended! The dog tracks only blood trails, but s/he does this very fast and efficiently. This companion makes playing much more fun and smooth. <3

name: theHunter: Call of the Wild™ - Medved-Taiga
description: You’ve hunted among the rolling hills of Hirschfelden and explored the iconic Layton Lake District. Now get ready to face nature’s most challenging conditions during a rare hunting expedition to Medved-Taiga National Park. Inspired by the harsh, frozen landscape of Siberia, you’ll traverse the unforgiving tundra, enter into the drunken forests and venture out on the frozen bay. Here, the weather can turn at an instant, leaving you swept in whirling snow and surrounded by cracking ice. THE ANIMALS OF MEDVED-TAIGA Despite its grim appearance, Medved-Taiga National Park is bustling with life. You will find prey like Musk Deer, Reindeer (also known as Caribou in North America) and Wild Boar. But larger species also roam. The majestic Moose travels these plains, as does the fierce Brown Bear. Perhaps most unique of all - hunters in this reserve will face the highly intelligent and ghost-like Lynx. MISSIONS AND MEASURES The open world of Medved-Taiga National Park measures 25 square miles (64 square kilometers), the same impressive size as Hirschfelden and Layton Lake District. It features a brand new, exciting narrative with 32 missions, 50 side missions and countless unique sights to discover. You take the role of an expert survivalist and hunter contracted to assist Alena Khasavovna and her scientific expedition. In addition to being a highly-trained climatologist, Alena hails from a reindeer herding people native to the region, offering her a unique insight into its challenges. The expedition is off to a bad start, and it's up to you to gather your group, help the locals and make sure everyone gets through the expedition in one piece. But it won’t be easy. Not everyone – and everything – wants you to succeed. SHARE YOUR EXPERIENCE As with previous paid DLCs, players that don't own the content themselves can join a multiplayer game set in Medved-Taiga National Park at no cost, by finding a player that owns it and simply joining their game. Multiplayer games allow hunters to compete or cooperate - the latter should prove useful in this challenging reserve. Lastly, the ATVs (available as a separate DLC purchase) are of course fully functional here. Driving on ice is as fun as it is nerve-wracking. DRESS UP, HEAD OUT To celebrate the release of Medved-Taiga National Park DLC, all players of theHunter: Call of the Wild will receive a free new set of clothes (purchasable with in-game currency) to enjoy in any of the game’s hunting reserves. And available exclusively for players brave enough to take on Siberia, there may just be a variation of one of the game’s weapons waiting for you as you progress through the brand new storyline… A WASTELAND FULL OF LIFE Medved-Taiga National Park features a dramatic environment unlike anything you’ve seen (let alone hunted within). The permafrost engulfing this region has created a massive sinkhole and forced clusters of trees to grow shallow roots, making them tilt and form the characteristic Drunken Forest. Not even the rock formations remain untouched by the elements. The impressive Lena Pillars have been ground down and shaped by the elements for millennia, slowly carving their unique silhouettes.
short_description: You’ve hunted among the rolling hills of Hirschfelden and explored the iconic Layton Lake District. Now get ready to face nature’s most challenging conditions during a rare hunting expedition to Medved-Taiga National Park.

the best map for mee is very very very beautiful ...big LIKE 10/10

Do NOT buy if you plan on completing this map. There's at least 3 missions which have been bugged for years that prevent you from progressing any further. Countless complaints and they're too lazy to fix a few simple bugs.

I'm thankful I got this while it was on sale and only wasted a couple bucks, because doing anything in this reserve is painful bordering on impossible. No matter what color you set the track highlights to in the interface settings, you can't see them, period - unless it's that tiny 1-hour window of time just after sunset, where it's dark out but you can still see track highlights without a flashlight. It's like having Hide All Animal Clues forced onto you permanently.

Also, I'm stuck on the mission where you have to find Gunther, because his tracks just completely cut off and I can't find the next ones anywhere, even after scouring the area for 15+ minutes. I know you can just turn off missions and play without them, but I enjoy doing the missions for each reserve. It's frustrating and disappointing to expect to be able to play the story missions, but be locked out of them because you couldn't find the next track.

Honestly, I was really looking forward to playing on this reserve. But since I can't see animal tracks or play the story missions (two things that are very important to whether or not I can enjoy playing the game at all), I won't be able to use it at all. I guess I shouldn't have gotten my hopes up.
The map is pretty but hunting is disappointing. IRL winter is the best time for hunting, but here it's the opposite. Snow can be too bright and hide both footprints and footprint hints.

This is the only map I am not enjoying so far.

Pros:
-The map is beautiful.
-Lore collectibles are placed at intuitive locations and easy to find while exploring.
-One of the story missions briefly makes a reference to Texans hunting wild hogs from helicopters with miniguns.

Cons:
-This map is like.. 1/4 innavigable rock.
-Smaller animals get stuck in the rocky terrain.
-Some areas of the map cause animals of all sizes to run around in small circles because they continuously try to path to a spot they cannot reach.
-You can barely move 10 meters on your ATV without drifting or rolling over.
-Lynx have a bug where all of their warning calls are either missing or labeled as mating calls.
-Point of interest markers are placed inside of caves at their center rather than at the cave entrances.
-Each time you find one of the cave paintings you receive the same line of dialogue from the warden which was very repetitive and annoying to me personally.
-Some areas of the map have more dense vegetation than its two predecessor reserves which is very surprising.
-Packs of gray wolves run around the map spooking all sorts of animals and will also attack you in large groups, basically you have a high chance of getting "knocked out" and sent back to your last spawn unless you have an ATV to scare them off.

Pros Summary:
To me, this map feels like an ambush style hunter's paradise. Lots of structures to build and take advantage of, potentially combined with tripods, tree stands, and the dense vegetation, you can become a ghost in this map. I am assuming this map is also the optimal choice for Great One Moose hunting. Most drink zones leave animals very expose and are also easy to navigate across because almost all the water in this map is frozen. Which makes me want to question why the animals are trying to drink there, but whatever.

Cons summary:
I would rather just play on Layton Lake, especially if casual hunting. The animals I view as most desirable in Medved are the moose and brown bear. You can already hunt moose and bears on Layton. Yes they are different types of bears, but if you aren't chasing brown bear trophies specifically, then you get a better experience on Layton because of the kinder terrain and more plentiful game. They made the gray wolves look really cool but man are they the complete opposite of fun. Even with full health, every handgun perk, and the DLC Davani 10mm pistol I can't fend off a pack of eight wolves. Maybe it's a skill issue, maybe it Maybelline. Either way, not fun.

All that being said, I did have some good experiences on this map. I am glad I bought this DLC (not the sidearms one) and I am glad I played it. I just want to fairly warn anyone who may have to pick and choose which DLCs they acquire. Within my first 10 minute of being on the map, I walked to the nearest drinking zone, spotted a moose and a few other animals, as well as a Gold male Brown Bear. Neither myself or the bear was aware that he was on a collision course with my scouting spot. I pulled out my .300 Magnum AR when he was getting dangerously close to me. Right before he tramples me, I stand up, jump to my left to dodge a bear paw swipe, and I took a double lung + liver shot that brought him down instantly. You know I taxi'd that. Later that same day, I found a Legendary Lynx that I used to complete one of the story missions. I tracked him for a long time, took a photograph, then tracked some more before harvesting him. Diamond on day 1! He's going in the lodge soon too. I tracked a troll moose that could have been diamond for over two hours one day. In a way, I harvested him by accident because I thought it was a completely different moose. His tracks were heading off to my left towards a drink zone, appropriate time too, but just over 100 meters in front of me another big bull and two females were coming out of the brush from my right. I was expecting to get this big boy, then keep on tracking his buddy, but nope. Somehow, he had gone ahead, wrapped around me like he's playing slither.io, and gave me a clean shot. With a bit of effort I overcame the weaknesses I perceived this map has and got some good trophies for it. However; I think I will only be returning for Great One moose grinding aaaand maybe a diamond musk deer if I feel up for it.

-warning-
Bugged DLC product
-warning-

I would not recommend buying this product at this time, unless it is included as part of a bundle with better DLC.

At it's core it would be an okay DLC. It shows it's age as one of the original DLC to an aging game but has some interesting themes and a unique weapon so it would still be passable.

However it suffers from an issue more and more of Hunter COTW's products are seeing over time, bugs that have now existed for years the developers don't seem interested in fixing. This includes bugs which make missions impossible to complete as well as terrain issues that can effect animals and the ATV.

If it's included with other stabler products? Sure pick it up.
Otherwise? Give it a pass and pick up something else or one of the DLC for this game that are fully functional.

name: theHunter: Call of the Wild™ - Vurhonga Savanna
description: Having explored the idyllic Layton Lake District, roamed the hills of Hirschfelden and hunted in the ice-cold forests of Medved-Taiga National Park, it’s time to head south to the Cradle of Life. Vurhonga Savanna is in need of your assistance, and it is up to you to answer the call. Traverse the scrublands, discover the riverlands and venture out into the lush savanna as you assume the role of a Warden and protect the lands from poachers and other potential misfortunes. Opportunities like this are few and far between, so pack your suitcase. Africa is calling! VURHONGA SAVANNA’S FAUNA Humming with life, Vurhonga Savanna supports a wide range of animals. On these thriving grounds you will be able to watch the Blue Wildebeest herds passing, chase after Lesser Kudus, catch a Springbok pronking, a Scrub Hare, and Warthogs grazing by the waterholes. In the late hours of the day you might catch the shrill cries of a Side-Striped Jackal. You must also watch your back for the respected and feared widowmaker - the Cape Buffalo. As Vurhonga Savanna continues to thrive, Grandfather Njabulo has decided that it is time to reintroduce the largest native carnivore and King of the Beasts - the Lion. Embodying the symbols of royalty and poise in human culture around the world, the Lion is the second-largest of the cats, surpassed only by the tiger. With a muscular and deep chest, the density of their muscles severely complicates shot placement and penetration during a hunt. The lions of Vurhonga Savanna are in their prime and will not take kindly to trespassers on their territory. As a hunter, remain steadfast in your resolve, and keep your wits about you as you venture into the wild to hunt the King of the Jungle. DIVERSE AND BEAUTIFUL FLORA Vurhonga Savanna introduces striking new environments to explore and hunt within. From the flourishing riverlands to the murky swamps, the reserve offers stunning landscapes to traverse as you, the new Warden, keeps the region safe. Discover the ancient Fever Tree Forest, or make your way to the heart of Vurhonga Savanna and discover the Tree of Life. MISSIONS AND MEASURES Much like the other reserves, Vurhonga Savanna measures a striking 25 square miles (64 square kilometers). Here you can choose to pave your own path and discover its beauty, wonders and dangers. Or follow a new intriguing story with 16 new narrative missions, 32 side missions and 14 warden missions. Your Grandfather, Njabulo Tshabangu, is the Senior Warden at the reserve. His primary responsibility is ensuring the safety and stability of this protected area, but over the last few years, Grandfather’s advanced age has slowed him down. He can’t keep up with the poachers and other forces that threaten the reserve, which has fallen into steep decline. Several threatened species have had to be moved to safer parks. Understaffed and on a tight budget, he turns to you to help him save the reserve – and your family’s reputation. THE BIGGEST CALIBER YET For in-game currency, get your hands on the KING 470DB! Used almost exclusively in single shot or double rifles, the 470 Nitro Express is currently the largest caliber available in theHunter: Call of the Wild. Regardless of the ammo you will be using this rifle has spectacular penetration and devastating damage. In addition to the default iron sight, you will be able to mount it with a Reflex Sight for improved accuracy. Premium weaponry like this rifle means premium accessories. Use it’s multileaf-leaf sight in order to change your zero point depending on the distance of your target. SHARED EXPERIENCE As with previous paid DLCs, players that don't own the content themselves can join a multiplayer game set in Vurhonga at no cost, by finding a player that owns it and simply joining their game. In Multiplayer you can choose to compete or cooperate. All players, regardless of DLC ownership, will have a whole new set of clothing available to purchase for in-game currency.
short_description: Having explored the idyllic Layton Lake District, roamed the hills of Hirschfelden and hunted in the ice-cold forests of Medved-Taiga National Park, it’s time to head south to the Cradle of Life. Vurhonga Savanna is in need of your assistance, and it is up to you to answer the call.

reviews.review_text: Open plains and a target rich environment. Vurhonga is a great place to get cash quick in game, just remember to bring a high powered rifle. Other than that, they did a great job building the map, with plenty of neat points of interest and scenic areas. The major downside to this map is that it lacks some of the most iconic large game. This includes Giraffe, Hippo, Elephant, Rhino, and more. I understand that some of those species are endangered and the developers wish to communicate that fact. However, at the end of the day this is a videogame about hunting. What does most people buying this map want to do? Hunt the biggest game on earth like the elephant, whilst not being the most dangerous force on the map. Lions and cape buffalo add danger to the map, but crocs, hippos and hyenas would really force the player to stay focused on his surroundings and add an ever looming danger to the experience. I however, would still recommend this DLC, even with my criticisms.
You haven't truly lived until you've been murdered by an angry Cape Buffalo

非洲这个地图是真的好玩，野性十足视野开阔以及天气系统都做得很好，这个图非常适合我这种新手玩家。而且地图里的蓝角马群大批量迁徙时会有地震的声音，这个细节非常完美，沉浸感超级棒。缺点就是地图有点小，而且非洲作为动物王国，里面的动物也太少了，比如斑马、大象、犀牛、猎豹、鬣狗、鸵鸟、猴子、鳄鱼这些标志性的动物都没有。我希望制造者后面可以加多一张非洲的地图把这些动物可以补充进去，价格贵点无所谓。

The map of Africa is really interesting. It is full of wildness, wide vision and the weather system is well done. This map is very suitable for novice players like me. Moreover, there will be the sound of earthquakes when the blue-horned horses on the map migrate in large quantities. This detail is very perfect and the immersion is great. The disadvantage is that the map is a little small, and Africa, as an animal kingdom, has too few animals in it, such as zebras, elephants, rhinos, cheetahs, hyenas, ostriches, monkeys, crocodiles and other iconic animals. I hope the manufacturer can add a map of Africa to supplement these animals. It doesn't matter if the price is expensive.
Really good.

Really liked the map and the lions, got taken down by a couple of them here and there, I also really like the other species of animals to be found but I do understand the community's frustration with the lack of expected species, I think the devs should understand this is a videogame rather than real life, that would give them more freedom and really open the gates to a lot of great content; not saying they have to bring a unicorn into the game (althought that would be cool as an event) just saying... you can be authentic without having realism be a constraint for your players or your creativity.

I liked the story here, it's about tradition and how values tend to change as you go through to time.

I’m honestly really disappointed by this DLC, feels like a waste of such a really good setting.

I can forgive not putting say, Rhinos in the game because Rhinos are in large part endangered, and the game takes a very clear anti-poaching stance. That’s not an issue I have with it.

But I do have an issue with how few animals there are here. Sure, all of them are unique, but where are the zebras for example? Theres so much that could have been done here that just wasn’t. Not to mention the lack of missions.

(Also, Nelson Mandela was a communist, though I’m not specifically gonna hold that against the devs here)

This is hands down the weakest DLC in the lineup. The map is disappointingly small, and the animals feel absurdly bulletproof — everything takes an excessive number of shots to bring down. I burned through more ammo here than in any other reserve, and it constantly felt like I was firing nerf darts instead of actual rounds.
Save your money and your time. This DLC just isn’t worth it.
Where are the zebras, giraffes, hyenas, crocodiles, wild dogs, elephants, rhinos, honey badgers
no
waste of money , there is no missions after story mission. I made the story mission and then no more missions.

name: theHunter: Call of the Wild™ - Silver Ridge Peaks
description: This region of the Rocky Mountains has captivated the imagination of many, who have sought to uncover its bounties. Where once people scouted the natural trails of Silver Ridge Peaks in search of silver and gold, they now come seeking a different kind of trophy - to hunt their favourite animals. Whether you are tracking turkeys through the valley or lining up to shoot a mountain goat on a cliff above the cloudline, it's hard to resist taking in the grandeur of the Colorado wilderness. SILVER RIDGE PEAKS’ FAUNA Silver Ridge Peaks prides itself in the range of animals available to visiting hunters. Whether you are stalking through the boundless grassy plains in pursuit of Pronghorn, keeping an eye out for stalking Mountain Lions or observing Rocky Mountain Elk traverse the dense forests, the reserve has something for every hunter. The ferocious Black Bear is a popular attraction, as are the Mule Deer, Plains Bison, Mountain Goat and Bighorn Sheep. But the real attraction is a little more feathery; hunters are known to flock to the reserve in search of Wild Turkeys. ROCKY MOUNTAIN FLORA Boasting a dramatic landscape that can easily take your breath away, the reserve takes great pride in its more elusive spots that can make visitors feel like they’re stepping back in time. From the mine and ghost town’s reminders of the past, to the cloud-piercing Red Mountains, the reserve is packed with marvels. As you roam the deep canyon or gaze upon the high altitude lakes, you can’t help but get lost in its beauty. TRADITIONAL STYLE ALEXANDER LONGBOW Through the use of contemporary bow-making techniques and flexible but sturdy materials, this Longbow is a stunning take on an ancient self bow, making it effective even for modern hunters. Compatible with the arrows used for the recurve bow as well as introducing two new types, the Alexander Longbow allows you to hunt animals of all sizes. A single stave of hickory and bowstring combine to form a silent but deadly weapon that epitomizes the resourcefulness of the peoples who used it to conquer prey. The longbow is available in three colour variations: Lacquered, Rough and Ash. MISSIONS AND MEASURES Silver Ridge Peaks spans an impressive 25 square miles (64 square kilometers), and it is up to you to decide if you wish to explore the landscape at your own discretion, or pursue the story with 15 new narrative missions. The history of Silver Ridge Peaks is as rich in legends of human endeavour and adventure as it is in studies of brutal expansion and mankind’s greed. Miners risked their lives to travel great distances during Colorado’s silver boom. Much of the landscape was blasted into its current shape, with only one consideration: the pursuit of money. The consequences of such single-minded profiteering have finally arrived, landing at the feet of the reserve’s latest warden: Allan Bradley. As he attempts to fill the shoes of his ailing father, Allan must repair the reserve’s reputation following a toxic spill from a century-old mine, and show the world that Silver Ridge Peaks is stepping forward into the future. Along with fellow hunter Remi Warren, it’s your job to show that there’s nowhere better to hunt than in the shadows of the Rocky Mountains. SHARED EXPERIENCE Try out Silver Ride Peaks without owning it by simply joining a multiplayer session of another player who owns the reserve. You will have access to everything but the narrative missions.
short_description: Whether you are tracking turkeys through the valley or lining up to shoot a mountain goat on a cliff above the cloudline, it's hard to resist taking in the grandeur of Silver Ridge Peaks.

reviews.review_text:
A 2026 REVIEW:

"theHunter" is a hunting simulation game developed by Expansive Worlds (originally by its parent company Avalanche Studios).

In 2015, "theHunter: Primal" was released but the game the company is known for is "theHunter: Call of the Wild" released in 2017.

A first person hunting game, the game has a plethora of maps, weapons and many other extra things that make your hunting experience much worthwhile.

Silver Ridge Peaks (2020)

When it comes to DLCs reserves, "Silver Ridge Peaks" (2020) has received a lot of positive reviews.  Thanks to its gripping storyline and the animals that can be found in this reserve, even when you finish the DLC's storyline, you just want to stay and explore the map further because there is so many things to hunt.

I'm impressed by how large this map is at 25 square miles.  So, may you walk or travel by ATV, there is so much to see, so much to hunt and it's an amazing DLC.

WHAT IS THIS DLC ABOUT?

Allan Bradley is the reserve's latest warden, taking care of the area for his ailing father.  But for some reason, the lake has become toxic.  To make things worse, someone is vandalizing locations, animals are being tortured, travelers have gone missing, and the only person that can get to the bottom of things to help Allan and others is you!

And you get some help from Remi Warren, yes the professional hunter on YouTube who lends you a few tips.

TIPS:
- Some locations are far and it helps to have an ATV [ATV SABER 4x4 DLC is needed or just walk and teleport]
- Having a dog will make things better and less lonely trekking this large map. [Bloodhound DLC]
- My favorite weapon to use on this map is the Perry .308 [Rapid Hunt Rifle Pack]
- Backpacks - The Backpack DLC will allow you to have a large backpack which will become useful.

Because come across some huge bears, ox, mountain lions, deer, and big mountain goats, if you get into taxidermy, get yourself a trophy lodge and the premium trophy mount pack.

GRAPHICS AND SOUNDS:

The environment, everything about it looks fantastic.  Playing during the day and night, this DLC looks amazing!  The amount of detail of environments, the ambiance of animals to bugs and you name it.  Great job of capturing visuals and audio for this DLC!

JUDGMENT CALL:

"Silver Ridge Peaks" is just a fun place to visit and hunt.  I can literally stay in this reserve and be happy with this single DLC because it offers so much.  

The storyline is no doubt fantastic for the majority of it but the problem is while it was building to a pretty cool ending, it seems that a true ending was never fully planned out.  It just ends.  Fortunately, right at the end, you discover another amazing place on the reserve.

For what its worth, "Silver Ridge Peaks" is a must own DLC.
The animals grat to hunt and I recommended this to my frend and he recommend it to

Easily one of the better maps in the game, however only a smaller portion is really fun to hunt in. The rest are dense mountainous areas, and the story is just grindy and long winded rather than appealing. Get on sale.
Two Fawns.
This is easily one of my Favourite maps! I'ts such a laid back map, and thanks to the very Big and Wide fields, shooting ANYTHING is all to easy! 9/10

Dear Community,
I've recently finished the storyline of this DLC and unlocked all the lookout towers/fast travel points. Therfore I came around quite a bit. My first impression of this DLC dragged itself through the entire gameplay. That playing this map feels extremely unsatisfying and blant. 
Compared to the two maps that were delivered in the original game this DLC map feels empty, uninspired and just boring. It is extremly large and gives plenty of opportunity to engage in long range/medium range and short range hunting with different species. However, it's a drag to find any animals at all. tHe map feels way too big for way too little animals. The variations of animals are nice (the game is 10 years odl, so I won't complain about the little details on the animal models), but to find any is just very difficult, as you can practuically only lure 50 % of the species and have to camp need zones or count on your luck for the rest. For me that is very unsatisfying.
Back to the map design: The developers seem to have placed the fast travel point seemingly at random. Unevenly placed, makes traveling without any quality-of-life-payed-DLC a actual timekiller. Nothing compared to the well placed travel points in the base game. Also - there is almost no variation of landscape on this incredibly large map. At few different areas are present, butr nothing copared again to the base game maps.

The story line is almost offensive. 14 or 15 missions, with a storyline that feels so boring and forced that I really had to force myself to finish it. It was no fun at all. Also - the only female character in the entire storyline is the good old fashioned 'damsel in distress' who is display as dump, idiot and not as forward thinking as any of them ale models. Perfeclt sculptured for any MAGA enthusiasts, but for people who enjoy equlal rights (to be idios and damsels and to be successful and witty) this is a large thumbs down to me. I know men are the man target group for this game, but, honestly, you designer could have done better.
No side missions. 
you get a free bow with which aiming is becomming gambling, even with a lot of practice. 

All in all, I can't reccomend this DLC, as the map is aweful big but feels super empty. An offensive story line towards women in general. But the animal species are pretty nice - if one gets to see any. as the new ones are rarely attractable.

Is anybody even reading this?

Compared to the other maps, this one just looks so videogamey. Steep smooth rounded mountains right up against the roads without much detail, very repetitive looking grass and groundcover without much differentiation... Also, they bundle this in the starter pack? What an awful way to introduce players to your game. I guess it is the only way they can convince people to purchase this. You can safely ignore this map and be completely satisfied when there are so many better alternatives.

The devs have driven the community around this game into a bad place. The the discord is just discussing exploits and throwing slurs at eachother like a call of duty game.  That's what it is now 'The Hunter: Call of Duty'

Great map, pathetic storyline.
For a game that clearly wants to spend as few resources as possible on these missions, they sure as hell don't write around it very well.

We ALL know that they're not going to model and animate an entire human npc just for this story, and yet every other mission has us tailing someone that's just out of sight, we promise. Oh, what was that noise? is there someone still here? They escaped just before you could see them, bummer! Whoops, looks like someone got lost and we need to find them before nature kills them, but we keep just barely missing them! What's that tire track over there? Wow, looks like someone left here in a hurry, juuuuuust before you arrived! Isn't that exciting?? 

I don't mind a slow story where not very much happens, but this is honestly pretty insulting. Asking me to get invested in a story that would require much more effort from you to pay it off is simply suspending too much disbelief.

Not to mention it's buggy as hell- i had to take dozens of photos from different random angles until one finally triggered the next part of the mission.

I’m beyond frustrated with this game. I spend hours playing, quit properly, then come back the next day only to find that nothing was saved. The worst part is that I actually managed to fix this once by entering multiplayer and then switching back to singleplayer, and saving finally worked again. But since the December 8 update, it’s completely broken once more. I’ve spent a ridiculous amount of money on this game, and this is what I get in return: a core feature that keeps breaking after updates. It’s unacceptable that something as basic as saving progress is still unreliable. At this point, it completely kills any motivation to keep playing.

name: theHunter: Call of the Wild™ - Emerald Coast Australia
description: Welcome to Australia! Discover breathtaking landscapes and hunt iconic animal species such as the Saltwater Crocodile and Eastern Gray Kangaroo as you explore this magical new hunting destination down under. KEY FEATURES • Explore the Australian outback, subtropical bushlands, and tropical rainforests, and experience the natural wonder of its stunning wilderness. • Hunt some of Australia's most iconic fauna, including the Magpie Goose, Banteng, Saltwater Crocodile, Eastern Gray Kangaroo, and a new Great One. • Make new friends and work together to protect the region's beautiful but fragile ecosystem through side and story missions focused on conservation, cooperation, and community. • Expand your hunting arsenal with the new Zagan Varminter .22-250 Bolt-Action Rifle. Light and comfortable to shoot, it's the perfect companion for any hunting trip.
short_description: Welcome to Australia! Discover breathtaking landscapes and hunt iconic animal species such as the Saltwater Crocodile and Eastern Gray Kangaroo as you explore this magical new hunting destination down under.

reviews.review_text: Well dam you never expect the hunting game to do it but they did it you get to hunt Kangaroos. It is weird to hunt them, first time I saw them a good 200 meters away some ran but 1 came tords me. I thought nothing of it just some weird rout the AI wanted to take then it came up to me and hit me scared me to death then I pulled out a pistol and shot it a bunch then another came and I had 2 roos at the end.
phew phew

Best map by far. Game density is high, crocs, kangaroo and banteng are a blast to hunt (and farm too, the coastline are basically money printing machine), and you get lots of variations in scenery, from the mountainous forest, plains, mangrove swamp, and beach. 

The only thing missing is emu

I bought this map for the croc money farm. It turns out to be a really good map for casual hunting too.
I definetly recommend on sale
Top 5 map. Massive, detailed, fantastic hunts.
how do i refund

An update broke the scoring on 2 species and the dev team has ignored it for 3 months now.  They should have given us a hotfix immediately but instead they just tell you to start a new game to get fresh respawns. (You loose all progress starting a new game,)  just feels like they don't care about it because only people who already bought it have the issue, new purchases won't, so it's not effecing sales.   Very scummy behavior.

I wanted to like it more, really wanted to enjoy being out back of beyond.
It's got so many negative "quality" of life choices though. The paths and roads aren't always well laid out for how large the map is. Getting to your gear is a nightmare at some outposts. Having to open multiple doors or closets or go through multiple rooms, when on most maps the gear storage is conveniently outside.
It's not a bad but at times it's more tedious than better designed and though out Reserves in the game.

Still, if you want to shoot a 'roo, it's your only option, the narrator friend pair are fun to listen to, and it does have a great rifle from an early main story line quest.

Recommend buying but only on sale or if you've played all the other Reserves up to it or really want the rifle ASAP.

*Why is the lighting of this map so dark and grey?
*The outback sub-region is very small
*"...ethical...", "...ethical...", "...ethical..." 
*Animals are rare, difficult to meet
*It's just boring honestly
It is boring for me(idk for anyone else but im tired of this one, i spent 1.5 hour on it and it wasnt worth it) returning it

name: theHunter: Call of the Wild™ - Revontuli Coast
description: Welcome to Finland, a country home to some of Northern Europe’s most beautiful hunting spots! Set in the north edge of the Baltic Sea, Revontuli Coast invites you to explore a vast and sprawling archipelago that teems with life. With peak bird season underway, you’ll hunt a wide range of waterfowl, ducks, and upland birds. Larger game also roam here, such as Brown Bear, Moose, and Whitetail Deer. Experience the thrill of stalking your prey under the magical Northern Lights, and discover exciting new hunting opportunities as you traverse this region of the “land of a thousand lakes”. Features • Explore 25 square miles (64 square kilometers) of stunningly varied nature, spanning across rocky archipelagos, wild fields, thick spruce forests, and vibrant fell hills. • Use the Water Layout Blind to conceal yourself from waterfowl, ducks, and upland birds while in water. • Hunt 19 different species - Revontuli Coast has the largest number of animal species ever featured in a reserve, and includes new and returning favorites such as the Western Capercaillie, Black Grouse, Brown Bear, Whitetail Deer, Raccoon Dog, and many more. • Gaze at the magical Northern Lights that illuminates the night sky. • Discover memorable geographical landmarks such as a large glacial pothole, an impressive balancing rock, and a wondrous gorge. • Hike along color-marked routes and trails during the day, and rest by cozy campfires as night falls.
short_description: Explore a sprawling archipelago that teems with life. Hunt waterfowl, ducks, and upland birds, in addition to larger game such as Brown Bear and Whitetail Deer. Gaze at the magical Northern Lights, and discover exciting new hunting opportunities in this region of the “land of a thousand lakes”.

reviews.review_text: Decent map with short but decent storyline. Recommended for bird hunters, otherwise skip. Neutral rating if possible, get on sale.
Keeps Crashing my game
As a Finn, I'm biased but it's still amazing. It has lots of different birds, but also bigger animals like moose and brown bear.
Ikavalko makes the best musick ever
Good map if you plan to go hunting on small game like ducks and goose

Used to be a time when they would fix issues in a timely manner. Especially when an entire species was broken.  There are 2 species now that have been broken for months.   And their solution is to tell you to loose all your progress and start a new game.     Just spit in my eye why do t you?
worst dlc out there

This is a torturous "No" to give but in a game known for bugs, up to game breaking bugs, and a company known for not going back and fixing old products (DLC), this map stands out for quest and game interrupting or breaking issues.

Which is a shame! The map itself was clearly some level of labor of love. The voice acting was fun to listen to. The main questline very relaxing and the hunting left to open ended and side missions. The boat travel mechanic was useful. The DLC item, a water blind, is useful if you like that sort of thing.

It's in the worst five DLC's for the game though in terms of risk of serious bugs. Unless you really want that water layout blind, unless they ever take the time to resolve it's issues (3+ years and counting, doubtful), consider giving this reserve a pass in favor of less glitchy ones.

First mission is to unlock the first safehouse. I already did that in multiplayer. Now the game wont let me unlock the first safehouse in single player mode to complete the mission as I've already unlocked it in multiplayer. Therefore I cannot do any missions at all. 

This is typical of this developer. Just the most basic things they cant do right. Ive been playing this game since 2018 and they'd rather scum you out of money with DLCs that spend time fixing bugs and making a competent product. 

Screw Expansive Worlds

i play over 100 hr no problem at all but when i play this map it make my game to freeze on start up. (I play on linux mint.)
if anyone have a problem like me, try to disable this DLC.

name: theHunter: Call of the Wild™ - Sundarpatan Nepal Hunting Reserve
description: Welcome to a unique hunting experience. The spectacular Sundarpatan Nepal Hunting Reserve awaits you. Here, beauty and exploration go hand in hand. But if you're also excited by a challenge, then this is the hunting ground for you. Elevate your hunting game Discover a remarkable array of wildlife, some exclusive only to these lands. Don't miss the likes of the rare and impressive Barasingha, the Snow leopard, and particularly the large Tahr – known in these parts as the “Great One” – that are among the 13 species roaming these disparate lands. When in the lowlands, being on high alert is critical. Home to the Bengal tiger, its solitary and elusive character will push your survival skill set to its absolute limit. Often seeking to stalk and ambush anyone that enters its territory, the challenge is simple: hunt or be hunted… Go beyond unique boundaries Grab your opportunity to hunt where life exists at the edge. Hunt at altitude on snow-covered, rough open ridges in the mountain region, explore the iconic Rhododendron forests of the mid-hills or brave the overgrown sal forests that bask in the tropical climate of the lowlands. Diversity and variation are omnipresent throughout Sundarpatan. The sheer scale of this location is guaranteed to challenge the most experienced of hunters. Come on a journey of cultural exploration and education Sundarpatan provides the perfect opportunity for you to uncover the richness of the natural Nepali environment. Together with local experts and wardens, you'll showcase your hunting expertise. Non-hunting mission objectives including agriculture, photography and repairs will also be part of supporting and developing the reserve. Hunt with the historical Gandhare Rifle Originally a breech-loading firearm in the late 19th Century, the Gandhare Rifle is a testament to Nepali innovation, both within the military and hunting realms. Call upon this handcrafted, high-caliber, single-shot rifle that symbolizes a major chapter in Nepal's history when it's most needed.
short_description: Brave the beauty at the stunning Sundarpatan Nepal Hunting Reserve. Hunt among three distinct climate regions and go in pursuit of 10 new animal species.

reviews.review_text: This map is absolutely beautiful, and the tigers and the snow leopards are the coolest animals to hunt in the entire game. The only negative is the fact that you will get regularly attacked by random aggressive water buffalo or wild yak, which can be super disruptive and annoying. Nevertheless, this is still the best DLC map in the whole game and definitely worth picking up.
An absolutely stunning map. Getting outside the bounds and trying to scale the mountains is more fun than hunting on it. I could care less about the animals, I just want to make my way up north.
This map gets me excited to play the game like no other

Hunting bengal tiger here is the most fun I've had in this game. Yes, the game can feel more scarce because the hilly and grassy terrain means you can barely see any, and they'll already run away from you. However the animal here is simply top notch, and the views are great.

Sundarpatan is amazing for casual hunting. It has three different biomes, mountains, woodlands and a more swampy area. A diverse selection of species is also amazing. My personal favorite for casual hunting. My opinion is kind of biased though, since multiple of my favorite species in this game are in this map, like tahr. It doesn't have any good grinds though, so only for casual hunters.

Spoiler for 15 minutes into the map:


Its a fine map, its fairly unique. Theres some bushes that your dog gets caught on which are annoying, but other wise its fine.

The story is horrific, which is saying a lot for this game.

There's a part of a story where a tiger starts killing farm animals, then it attacks a person and is "shot" off screen. 
You run around the map to find its body, only to be told that poachers SHOT ITS CANINE TEETH OUT and its "our fault" that the tiger became a man eater.

This is perhaps the dumbest story I have ever seen included in a game. I am a staunch advocate for ethical hunting and wildlife conservation, but this is so ham fisted it makes me want to drive every living thing to extinction.

All they had to do was make the tiger old, with worn out teeth. This happens to real animals and it can lead them to attack humans. But NO, we have to shove in MUH HUMAN ENVIROMENTALLY DAMAGE with all the grace and subtlety of a drunken elephant in a window factory.

The Napalese "culture" segments are really, really over done, which is a shame because a lot of their culture is really cool. The culture segments in this feel completely out of place, like youre watching a little kids show that has to hit factoid beats to be considered "educational". 
"Oh man we gotta find this tiger before it starts killing people, this is SUPER important and serious. ALSO DID YOU KNOW THAT THE KHUKURI CURVED BLADE IS VERY IMPORTANT TO PEOPLE IN NEPAL, ALSO WE LOVE FOOD."

Its a genuine disservice to the culture and its agonizing to sit and listen to. Farcry 4, of all games, displayed Nepali culture in an infinitely superior manner to this.

The map is fine, its pretty different. Its another heavily forested map and most of the animals are clones from another map, but its fine. Not for $12, but if you can pick it up in a sale and you REALLY want to hunt tigers, give it a shot.

There is a bug in the Game for months now, they have fixed this exact bug before with hotfixes. I don't understand why they have not given us that fix this time. They are aware, they just stopped caring.

I have played cotw for almost 900hrs over the years and on almost every reserve.
Have always loved the exploration and the thrill of the hunt but I have to say this reserve should never have been released in the state it is.
It is a walking sim at best and even then getting stuck on terrain and having to hop not walk is frustrating. To find need zones let alone animals is ridiculous. Save your money and put it into other DLC. Not worth it even on sale. EW should be ashamed of this against all of it's other reserves, can't spend any more energy on the negatives when I spent enough walking around the map with no hunting.
Bugged missions. You have to scare tigers away from a trail, you'll make them flee, and it doesn't count.

Spoiler Alert

I have not played COTW in a long time (I still played it 190+ hours). It was since Way of the Hunter(WOTH) came out, and I moved there, as I found that game to be more realistic. Still, one thing I should admit as a strong pro of COTW is the vast range of options across different hunting reserves and the consistent support that has been ongoing for years. Hence, when I learned that there is a reserve featuring the Himalaya, I decided to come back to the game. 

However, after finishing the main story, this has been an enormous opportunity wasted, and I cannot emphasise enough how huge a let-down it was. What would anybody think of upon hearing tiger hunting? Jim Corbett, of course. The legendary hunter who was born in the Himalayas during the colonial period hunted down the infamous Champawat and Powalgarh  tigers. I initially anticipated that the devs would take many references from his own memoir in recreating the reserve featuring tiger hunting, which turned out to be quite wrong. Yes, Corbett himself was a conservationist, and the game never stops preaching the importance of preserving wildlife and ethical hunting. (I am quite fed up with it at this point, not because I am against it, but the idea has been recycled in literally every hunting game and main stories.) Nevertheless, the game never gives you any opportunity to hunt down man-eaters in the main story. There is a moment in the game where a player is informed that a man-eating tiger has appeared in a village, but it is hunted by someone else in the game you cannot physically interact with, and all you find is the dead body of a tiger that you have been tracking down for hours, following the footstep marks on the ground. After that, the story continues by giving you endless new locations miles away to visit, turning the game into a literal walking simulator. I remember that other reserves in the game had missions where you hunted down predatory animals that were threatening or injured. Still, I have no idea why this was not done for this particular reserve. 

Besides the story, I am also quite disappointed by the weapon you can unlock from the reserve, as well. Upon buying the reserve, a player can obtain a Gandare rifle, originally a Nepalese Gacendra rifle copied from the British Martini-Henry. I must say, this wasn’t a bad gun to add to the game, and I am happy to acquire a unique rifle that I believe was never featured in other games (Althought I am pretty bitter that the gun does not have the real name. This was one of the main reasons why I moved to WOTH which actually licence real gun companies). However, Jim Corbett’s preferred gun was a .275 Rigby, and it is the one known to have hunted down the Champawat tiger. I seriously wish this were available in the game when purchasing the reserve, even if that meant paying double the price. 

I understand that many of my complaints about the game don’t necessarily matter, and if you are a COTW player who just wants a new reserve and new animals to hunt, it won’t matter at all. Yet, I still think the reserve could have been done much better, and the main story could have been far more interesting than a walking simulator. As this is the only hunting game that features the Himalaya that I have been dreaming of for a long time, I really wish it had been just better.

name: theHunter: Call of the Wild™ - Hunters' Choice: Bolt-Action Rifle Pack
description: Get ready to take your shooting skills to new levels and become an accomplished hunter with the Hunters' Choice: Bolt-Action Rifle Pack. This is the first pack to be made in collaboration with our incredible and supportive community. Selected by theHunter community, each of the three bolt-action rifles brings its own nuanced characteristics, offering owners the opportunity to engage a variety of small and large game with impressive accuracy. Each rifle is compatible with a variety of scopes. Vallgarda .375 Your ultimate companion while hunting large and dangerous game. With the Vallgarda .375 bolt-action rifle's accuracy and power, it's an ideal weapon to bring with you to your hunts. Johansson .450 Designed for hunting medium to large game, the rugged construction and smooth operation of Johansson .450 enables it to deliver a consistent and reliable performance while offering the perfect balance between modern innovation and traditional styling. Fors Elite .300 Take on the biggest game with the Fors Elite .300. This bolt-action rifle thrives in fast-paced scenarios. Make sure to take full advantage of its rapid reloading mechanism and superior hipshot capability. Its large magazine size means extended shooting sessions are a must.

reviews.review_text: The weapons are top notch  for me and I recommend using the Agus scop for the weapons and an tree stand on selver rig pecs
you can basicalaly kill anything with the vallgarda

Often I just use one gun from a weapon pack, but I use all three of these in my loadouts. 
1. The .375 is the most powerful class 7-9 gun to date. Enough said.
2. The .450 is a great alternative to a class 4-8 weapon in tight loadouts, weighing only 3 rather than 3.5 or more.
3. The .300 is a great upgrade from a class 2-6 weapon when you don't need the class 2 coverage, e.g. on Silver Ridge Peaks.

currently the best weapon DLC
Valgadra is currently the hardest hitting weapon in its class (7 to 9)
Taking down moose with double lung shots from 300m+
An upgrade from the Arzyna .300 for hunting big game. 
While Arzyna offers softer recoil, Valgadra makes up for it in stopping power.
carry the 450, the 375 and like a 12 gauge with all 3 ammo types, you can basically kill anything in this game.
DLC won't load to the game

rifles are pretty cool, but honestly, there's no real reason to buy this pack. many other weapons from other packs (namely the modern rifles pack and the high caliber pack) and several base game weapons are able to do the same thing these rifles do.
if you're looking for ways to spice up your hunting with new rifles that have some unique properties then i'd say this is worth it on sale.
however, if you don't really care much about weapon diversity then i wouldn't get this pack, there's other packs with guns that essentially do the same things these guns do, but just a bit better (and a bit cheaper in the case of modern rifles pack)

Vallgarda .375 has a slightly higher penetration than the .300 canning mag, almost negligible, which does not always guarantee to have enough penetration to do a heart shot thru the shoulder muscle of a moose at a slight angle from 300 meters. Also there is a noticeable increase in bullet drop compare to the .300 canning mag and the Rangemaster 338. In fact, it has more bullet drop than the popular class 4-8 rifles, the Ecker .30-06, the .303, the m1 and the .308. I am not sure how the physics work in this game, but I assume more bullet drop also means slower muzzle velocity.

Here is what I got from shooting at the bullseye of a target from 400 meters at the shooting range, zero at 300 meters, on March 13, 2025:

.300 Canning mag scores 7.3/10
Eckers .30-06 scores 6.5/10
F.L. Sporter .303 scores 6/10
M1 Iwaniec scores 5.5/10
Rangemaster 338 scores 5.5/10
ZARZA-10 .308 scores 5/10
Vallgarda .375 scores 3.5/10

This pack is the most unrealistic thing to have ever come into the Hunter universe. 

I'll sum this up quickly. 

In-Game: 300 Blackout out-performs the 7.62x54R.
Real world: 7.62x54R has almost triple the muzzle energy of 300 Blackout. 54R typically carries the same energy at 400 yards that 300 blackout delivers at 200 yards. 

In-Game: .450 Bushmaster is almost identical to the .270. 
Real World: .450 Bushmaster is designed for AR platform rifles and for use in brush hunting, NOT long-range shooting. The .270 Win outperforms the 450 Bushmaster past 100 yards in every category. 

More "I've never actually shot a gun or studied a ballistics table" content from Expansive Worlds. At this point the game is a lost cause.

The best way I could describe this pack is it just exists. I'm being serious, that's the best way I can describe it. I'll start with the good parts about it 

 Vallgarda .375
The .375 is by far the best part of this pack. It hits like a tank and absolutely slams large animals like Moose and Buffalo. Unfortunately, that's about where it ends. The .375 truly only shines in areas that the 300 & 338 struggle in (double lunging Moose for example). Animals like Black Bear and Elk you really won't notice any difference between this and the aforementioned rounds.

Johansson .450
Another class 4-7 rifle yay.... It's a solid Deer rifle and that's about it. 7mm & 308 does everything the .450 can but better.

Fors Elite .300
I'm genuinely not sure why this even exists. It gets completely overshadowed by the base game 243 due to being classes 3-6 and not 2-6 like the 243. 

I really wanted to like this pack, but when 66% of it what it includes is virtually useless, it's really hard to. Only way I could truly justify a purchase is if you exclusively hunt big game animals like Moose and Buffalo. Otherwise, you're not missing out on much

name: theHunter: Call of the Wild™ - German Shorthaired Pointer
description: Meet your newest hunting companion. Just as its name suggests, the German Shorthaired Pointer is designed to point you in the right direction of your desired prey. On your command, it will leap and bound its way towards all but the largest of predators, making them much easier to target. Find animals faster Capable of pointing towards animals of all classes with the exception of harmful predators, the German Shorthaired Pointer helps you encounter more animals and find their whereabouts quicker, delivering a more efficient hunting session. Some additional traits also bring extra utility, such as searching for a particular species after you've located and interacted with a clue associated with it. Benefit from pointing behaviors Throughout your session, your choice of command will dictate the dog's search and point behavior. But one thing's for sure: you'll know immediately when it has identified a target animal through the stance it displays. Here's a breakdown of the German Shorthaired Pointer's different behaviors: • Point S (small game): The dog searches within a close proximity to you. Upon finding a target, it will ‘point' in that direction and await your command before pouncing to flush out the animal. • Point M (medium game): Keeping its distance from you yet staying close enough so that its ‘point' can still be seen, the dog searches for animals within a wider radius. Game of this size will eventually flee if the dog ‘points' at them for too long, so make sure to always keep a good eye on your companion. • Point L (large game): For larger animals that won't be intimidated by the dog's presence, it will roam within a very large radius. It will signal to you when a scent has been found, guiding you in its direction. • Spot Point: The dog will advance towards and ‘point' at an animal which has already been ‘spotted' by you. • Point Species (trait): Shortly after encountering animal tracks or droppings, you can direct your dog to search for the particular species that left the clue. Train your expert hunting dog Players who already own the Bloodhound or the Labrador Retriever are in for a real treat. Through a free new update available for all dog owners, you can customize your very own expert hunting dog by enhancing one dog with the skills of two. For example, by combining the skills of a Pointer and a Retriever, you can train your dog to skillfully flush out birds and efficiently retrieve them after you've taken your shot.
short_description: Meet the German Shorthaired Pointer! This skillful dog will point you in the direction of your desired targets and help you find their whereabouts quicker.

reviews.review_text: This one is my favorite of the dogs since I play the game as a hiking simulator rather than camp -> respawn -> repeat. If you play that meta I don't recommend this one. But if you wander around like a drunk skunk with its head stuck in a peanut butter jar, this dog is good for that. I wish there was a "neutral" option instead of recommend or don't recommend, because this one is not at all useful for the trophy meta stuff. Aside from the mechanics (takes some getting used to, a lot of trial and error on this one for me) being a bit wonky, it does a solid job with spot point or just trying to get an animal alert for a better shot. Which if you're hanging out at drink zones you wouldn't really need anyways. So I'll just say "tentative yes" for me but not for most players. I also like this dog's model more than the lab and bloodhound and ability to add the bloodhound tracking after the level 15.

good game

Let's go back over what the page says the dog should do:
 Point S (small game): The dog searches within a close proximity to you. Upon finding a target, it will ‘point' in that direction and await your command before pouncing to flush out the animal.

• Point M (medium game): Keeping its distance from you yet staying close enough so that its ‘point' can still be seen, the dog searches for animals within a wider radius. Game of this size will eventually flee if the dog ‘points' at them for too long, so make sure to always keep a good eye on your companion.

• Point L (large game): For larger animals that won't be intimidated by the dog's presence, it will roam within a very large radius. It will signal to you when a scent has been found, guiding you in its direction.


Takeaway after using the dog for several hours: The above is what a game director/designer told someone to impliment, it was harder than expected and they took what they could. The dog simply does not do these things. It has some merits, sure, if you study the creature long enough you can find that it goes up to literally anything and barks in its face. 25% of the time it will even go the direction you yourself intend to go and give you a shot you would have missed. 75% of the time it is out of sight doing who knows what and it's a huge struggle to call it back to you even to play or pet. Really half baked mechanic here.

name: theHunter: Call of the Wild™ - Salzwiesen Park
description: A COMPACT MAP FOR SMALL GAME HUNTING Located in Schleswig-Holstein, which sits near the border of northern Germany and Denmark, Salzwiesen Park offers a more contained biome packed with the same animal density and species diversity as a standard reserve. Equipped with a shotgun or your favorite weapon of choice, enjoy the thrill of hunting within these smaller-scale wetlands, making your way through reed beds and summer polder to seek out the park’s large bird population as well as the mammals that have adapted well to this flooded environment. KEY FEATURES: Explore a beautiful compact park designed specifically for memorable small game hunting experiences. New Clay Shooting Range – Train your aim and test your skills on your own or against friends in clay- and skeet-shooting. Hunt 15 small game and waterfowl species, including two new birds—the Ferruginous Duck and Gadwall. The GOPI 10G GRAND Shotgun – Perfect for targeting small game and waterfowl, the new over-under shotgun is a versatile addition to your arsenal and also great for the shooting range. FULL SPECIES LIST: Birds: Gadwall Ferruginous Duck Black Grouse Ring-Necked Pheasant Mallard Eurasian Teal Tufted Duck Golden Eye Eurasian Wigeon Bean Goose Greylag Goose Small Game: Red Fox European Rabbit Common Raccoon Raccoon Dog
short_description: Explore the salt marsh habitat at Salzwiesen Park and hunt a wide range of waterfowl and small game. Plus, sharpen your shooting skills solo or compete against friends at the new Clay Shooting Range.

reviews.review_text: It's an amazing map for casual hunting and the pheasant grind. Only for small game hunters though, since it doesn't have anything bigger than class 2
Worth it alone for the the clay shooting range imo.
Fun small map for small game. Really good for money and xp grind.
Great map for bird hunting

People complaining about this map looking like hirschfelden, not having enough big game or it being bland probably bought this DLC blindly. It's literally advertised as a waterfowl hunting park and for that purpose it's amazing. So maybe don't buy it if you don't enjoy hunting birds??

Probably the absolute worst DLC so far.

- Flocks of birds bug out and get stuck mid-air, and don't count as kills when shot down. 
- Most flocks of birds can't even be identified or spotted with the binoculars, as if the flock doesn't actually exist.
- The flocks that CAN be identified, flee away from your position within 200m to 250m, without spotting you or hearing you. They just permanently know EXACTLY where you are. ESPECIALLY in Multiplayer.
- Blinds/Hides don't work. (see previous point)
- Lures/callers don't work.
- Scent cover doesn't work.
- Decoys don't work.
- Literally the only thing from this DLC that actually works is the Skeet Shooting range, which isn't without its own issues.

And before anyone says "BuT Did YoU HidE foR LonG eNoUgH anD MakE SuRe yOu Are dOwN WiNd?"...

...YES AND YES. For literal HOURS.

DO NOT BUY THIS DLC unless you have a ravenous thirst for Skeet Shooting only.


Edit : Even the ingame hours for the DLC is glitched and not appearing on my profile/review, instead showing as 0 hours. Literal garbage.
THIS IS BUNS

I’m only disliking this map for 2 reasons. 
- the cost is unreasonable for such a small map
- no hunting structures

I’ll be honest. I was expecting a larger map with more to explore. The map itself is really pretty and I do find it designed well ~ minus its size. It’s a fine map for bird hunting except the fact that there are no hunting structures. 
You will need many tents and ground blinds or other hunting structures just to get close enough to the birds so you don’t scare them away. 

Definitely has the most annoying voice acting out of all the maps. (I apologise to whoever voiced the narrative in this map.)

This map is also NOT worth the cost. Even on sale it’s not worth it. I almost want a refund for it but I won’t ask for one just to support the developers.  I do hope they take in to consideration some of the ideas to make the map better.  

- add at least 3 hunting towers around the map.
- add a few more story missions
- why are the birds more skittish in this map than the others…? I could be moving crouched 150yd away and they would still flee.
pretty boring map, by far the most limited in scope and content.

Most DLC are worth it for COTW but this falls short for me. 

There are a few pro's and con's which I'll list below.

Con's
Small reserve
Ugly outposts, and few of them, they literally feel like an afterthought on this reserve.
Zero lookout towers
Only class 1 & 2 ammo needed throughout 
Hardly any animals to hunt (mostly birds catered for)
Practically no missions, almost no story line
Very few Points Of Interest
Price, definitely not worth it, should be at most 40% of a normal reserve cost considering how much this reserve lacks in comparison to others. Of 15 DLC reserves 13 cost £6.69, 1 costs £9.99 and this one costs £5.89. (with no discounts/sales) Honestly it should just be lumped into the base game given how little it brings to the table.

Pro's
The clay pigeon shooting range
Plenty of birds to train your dogs with

Hopefully future reserves will be better, like the previous 13 resonalbly priced ones.

name: theHunter: Call of the Wild™ - Rapid Hunt Rifle Pack
description: Gear up for a new level of hunting with the Rapid Hunt Rifle Pack, featuring three semi automatic rifles known for their quick action and power. Be ready for anything the wild throws your way. The Rapid Hunt Rifle Pack Includes: PERRY .308 The PERRY.308 is a rugged and reliable semi-automatic rifle. With its gas-operated long-stroke piston action, tough build and powerful recoil management, every shot will be just as effective as the last making it a trusted companion. Chambered in .308, the rifle is a perfect fit for medium to big game hunting. HANSSON .30-06 The HANSSON .30-06 is a semi-automatic rifle designed for quick action and hip fire in the harshest environments. Built with exceptional attention to detail, it offers an extremely smooth action making sure you get fast follow up shots with ease. Equipped with .30-06 ammo, it’s the ideal rifle for hunters targeting medium to big game. QUIST REAPER 7.62X39 Built to handle virtually any environment and utilizing an all new ammo type, the QUIST REAPER 7.62X39 stands as a timeless choice for hunters. Known for its ease of use, it’s the perfect rifle for small to medium game. Utilizing the new 7.62x39mm ammo beloved by precision hunters worldwide, it offers a completely new challenge for even the most seasoned hunters.
short_description: Gear up for a new level of hunting with the Rapid Hunt Rifle Pack, featuring three semi automatic rifles known for their quick action and power. Be ready for anything the wild throws your way.
