you get a sks ♥♥♥♥ yeah

So, you're probably sitting here, looking at an SKS, a sporterized AK with an interesting rivet pattern and what looks to be an AK with a longer bolt carrier and different bolt, fitted for ,30-06

So what do we have here?
So what I called a sporterized AK, is more actually closer to a .308 Vepr, the poster child of a very messy relationship between the AK platform, and an M14 when they both realized they were single and ready to mingle. So what can we say about this? well, it's got the Receiver of an RPK, a thumbhole stock to remember you that makin holes is what this thing was built for, a barrel that isn't super long, but it's enough to burn most of the powder, a gas system that unapologetically russian, or Canadian if you see it like a flipped m1 garand, a dust cover only an ak lover could find like home, and an optics mount that looks very very similar to what you'd see the guards from the kremlin use.

So, if you're feeling cold, and you want to bring the war to boars, this one will have you sit right at home. 

The second one is an "Ak in ,30-06" to most untrained eyes? and to most trained eyes? might as well be, I mean sometimes these things look so alike you wonder if the only thing stopping them from being interchangeable is the lack of a dremel and a spot welder. Listen, i won't do you no favour my dear reader, i would've loved to see a Romanian PSL, in 7.62x54mmR but hey, you can't have all the cake and eat it.
So you get given this jelly filled donut that's what seems to be a cross breed between a saiga and a vepr and maybe a sprinkle of zastava m76 in 30-06. I'm not much of an ak connoisseur, but if you ever wondered if this is what you'd find on an eastern block, then, the answer from me is a "who cares, it's an AK like platform in 30-06"
So, long story short, this thing's definitely something, and i'd say it's fun enough to shoot.

Last but not least, the SKS, truly the old guard.
This one only holds 5 rounds, i guess you have to thank canada or those damned scalpers for cutting in half the stripper clips AND limiting the magazines. But you know what? there's actually something admirable to that, we've always seen the best of man come from where it got limited most, so if anything, this gun is a socratic lesson on learning to be your best with the worst. So, this one of a kind hand me down piece of history from the 'nam war (probably a bringback from a rat tunnel expedition) fires oddly straight, might smell of cosmoline, and might have more rust than your 240D, i'm looking at you, guy who can't be bothered to weld the car's floor.
Anyhow, as far as guns go, this one, shoots too, loads a 5 round stripper clip, and just like in tarkov, it's good enough just as it comes, and if you want to bubba it, maybe, you don't deserve it.

The bayonet isn't included with the weapon, i suppose the person buying it left it at home on the charcuterie board

This just about leaves us with the rounds
.308 / 7.62x51
It flies straight, and it's often racing to see if it pokes more animals than roadside signs. It has a very appreciable trajectory, this thing's just like the discount coffee, it'll get you there but it won't surprise you. The key to a strong marriage is consistency, and this has plenty

30-06:
If you felt like getting a .308 would've yelled to the whole world you have a Midlife crisis, and to you it's too modern of a round, i have good news for you, break out that three piece suit and your bowler hat because this thing's so old it probably would like AI posts on facebook if it could.
It's a straight shooter, it's heavy, drops slightly less than the .308, or at least, it does IRL, but, just like a pissing competition, it's negligible if the end goal is watering the floor.

7,62x39:
If the two are having a head to head competition on who's more worthy to sit there, this fellow's in his own corner of the sandbox. Hell i wouldn't bother pitting it against the other two rounds to begin with. This falls more in the intermediate round cartridge. 
It's got pep, it takes down medium game, it's one hell of a jack of all trades, for what it is, i'd say it's worth the price tag, I found myself to enjoy this caliber quite a lot in game. I'd probably trust this round enough to reccomend it to a blind date, or to even have it hired where i work if it were a person. It's got the charming do it all style that comes with something like a Lada or a UAZ

My personal thought on this DLC Weapon pack?
Overwatch reels players back with new heroes and more questionable development choices. This game here brings you back by putting three guns under a cardboard box and a stick. And maybe i'm as dumb as a quail but I fall for it anytime. These weapons are masterfully crafted and well modeled. Sometimes i wish i could just reload them all the time just to admire the animations, textures, and models the devs worked hard to make. If the sistine chapel was Michelangelo's greatest work. I'm sure we can call this a solid hitter. The devs of this game have potential, this hits like a birdie, Timeless and classic. Keep it up

I rate it 5/5, i guess magazine limitations also hit the reviews.

Hansson 30-06 is a stronger variant of the Zarza .308 moderately. Offering higher penetration on the field with similar handling and recoil. Hansson makes more double lung shots than the zarza 308 (due to the stronger penetration on the 30-06 ammo) consistently. 

If you are deciding between modern rifles vs this pack, this pack is the goat for mid-big game

Though the Quist reaper is outclassed by Frost 257 marginally, you will likely use Hansson in your perma loadouts for grind and for trophy hunts.

name: theHunter: Call of the Wild™ – Pump, Flip and Fire Pack
description: Pump, flip, unfold, and fire with 3 tactile hunting weapons. Featuring new takes on classic favorites like the lever-action rifle, fast-loading pump-action rifle, and the lightweight portable folding shotgun, hunters will appreciate the versatility this curated firearm collection brings to their loadouts. ANANTHA ACTION .22 LEVER-ACTION RIFLE The Anantha Action, with its sleek and modern finish and tubular magazine, is a tactile new take on the popular classic lever-action rifle. Designed with .22 magnum chambering, its speedy and reliable reload capability will have you leveling up your small game hunting in no time. FROST .257 PUMP-ACTION RIFLE The reliably accurate Frost pump-action rifle is the perfect example of high-powered, smooth functionality. With its rapid reload magazine chambering in .257 ammunition, it presents a unique loadout option for hunters looking to switch things up. It’s also a complementary choice when hunting a wide variety of medium-sized game. GIDDINGS SSC .410 COYOTE FOLDING SHOTGUN From folded to fireable in seconds, the backpack-friendly Giddings is a versatile and lightweight addition to your loadout, ensuring you’re ready to react when the hunting opportunity strikes. As well as birdshot, the .410 chambering now includes buckshot and slugs, designed for efficient takedown of small to medium game.
short_description: Pump, flip, unfold, and fire with 3 curated tactile hunting weapons: a modern lever-action rifle, a fast-loading pump-action rifle, and a lightweight portable folding shotgun.

reviews.review_text: Overall, I gives this one a thumbs up. I find the .257 to be a good upgrade to the base .243 for classes 2 to 6. The .22 mag is a nice addition for classes 1 to 2. Not particularly necessary IMO but fun. The Chiappa Big Badger clone is my personal favorite, to supplement a super light type of loadout. Though to be fair, the .45 Judge clone is arguably better when looking for something to fill that type of slot, with its 6 shots, and covering classes 1 via the .410 shells and the 2 to 5 stuff with the .45 cartridges (and longer ranges than .410 buck or slugs). Still, for me it's a fun DLC with more interesting options compared to other weapon DLCs that were recently put out.
if your a new player there are more important gun packs to get, but this Pump, Flip and Fire Pack is a fun nice to have and helps to support the game too.

The Frost .257 is one of the best class 2-6 rifles in the game (better than the .243 Ranger but not as good as the Quist Reaper). It's also the first pump-action rifle in the game and I hope more will come. The rifle also weighs only 3 units compared to 3.5 for the .243 and 4 for the Quist Reaper, so it fits nice in most loadouts.

The Anantha .22 mag is a great alternative to the .22 LR and .22H rifles, but it weighs 0.5 units more than both. It has a very long reloading time compared to the others even with the perk that speeds up reloading. The rifle is for class 1-2 like the .22H but it has similar recoil as the .22 LR. Therefore, I think that the .22 mag is better than the .22H but not as good as the .22 LR. It's definitely better at taking birds out of the air compared to both. Another lever action rifle is nice, too.

A .410 shotgun is something I've been wanting to be added for a while and I'm glad it's here. It's a single shot shotgun that weighs only 2 units, so it's amazing if you want to squeeze a shotgun into your heavy loadout. It's not the best at taking out birds from afar, so you'll want to hit them close if you want them to die. The buckshot and slugs are really nice additions, although I probably won't use them much other than for special trophies. 

Overall it's a really nice weapon pack; I think all the guns are really nice. I would say it's worth the money just for the .257, though.

Frost .257
This is one of the best class 2-6 rifles in the game at the moment. It has a massive amount of power for a simple deer and pig rifle. The pump action of the rifle also allows for quick follow up shots, with 4 in the mag which is great for heard management. I love this rifle and it's definitely the star of the show for this weapon pack.
Anantha Action .22 Mag
Also a great 1-2 class rifle, with a ton of ammo in the reserves (10 in the tube). This rifle is great for turkeys, rabbits, and water fowl. I still like the 10/22 better then this rifle, but it makes load outs more creative so to speak. Nothing really more to say, it's just a good rabbit gun.
Giddings SSC .410 Coyote
I'm gonna have a hot take on this gun... It kind of sucks. I know people like this pack for the folding .410, but it's extremely under powered. I know it's a .410, so i'm not expecting 20 gauge or 12 gauge performance, but if I shoot a fleeing turkey from 15 yards and it doesn't even drop then I don't use the gun. It's cool for sure, but i'm not gonna use it that much if at all.
Overall
I really like this pack, and highly recommend it for the Frost .257 and the Anantha .22 Mag. The Folding .410 is hit or miss for me, but it's cool for sure. For 5 bucks I think it's worth it for  a better 2-6 rifle, nothing against the Ranger .243.
The .410 patterns nice and tight! Now we need a "Nordin SA-410" please!
mediocre as ♥♥♥♥

Kind of overrated if you ask me. .22 Mag is nowhere near as good as the .22H, .410 is still extremely weak and the new .257 is good and all, but I still find the SKS to be much more effective, without counting the bullet drop.

This DLC is nothing special: there is only one gun, the .22 Lever Action Rifle, that may truly be worth having, given its uniqueness which the other weapons in the package lack.  The .257 Roberts Pump Action Rifle is just a clone of the Quist Reaper 7.62x39, featured in another DLC.  Similarly the .410 carries a 2-5 cartridge that a good hangun can easily replicate.  That sums up this DLC, and it really doesn't warrant purchase at full-price.  You can get it on sale or even pass on it.

The developers continue to ignore the most basic of needs for accessibility users. They've also cut things we loved, like the streams by Jaxy. Adding a simple pause feature would take this game from a good game to an amazing game. I enjoy it, but I cannot recommend it because I can't play it long enough to truly love it without having to quit because of some incredibly urgent interruption, sometimes due to life or death circumstances. The game is based upon the engine that Just Cause 3 is built atop, and that game allows pausing. Expansive Worlds is ignoring and has been ignoring this extremely deal-breaking plea from its community for years.

can we please just stop releasing dlcs every other month? Their has not been a free weapon released since 2022, and even then, it was one revolver. Before that was 2017, with the bearclaw (unless i'm mistaken), not to even mention their has been 0 free maps released. Would it hurt to make even a few of the older weapon packs free?

name: theHunter: Call of the Wild™ - Alberta Hunting Preserve
description: It may be bitterly cold, but the frigid landscape of Alberta, Canada, is teeming with life - if you know where to look. Discover and track 19 species through the snow, tackle 13 engaging missions, and explore from the pine-covered peaks down to the frost-swept grasslands to find the perfect hunting spot - but watch out for falling trees, a sign that the preserve’s most industrious inhabitant is active! DEVELOP YOUR HUNTING MASTERY Discover Askiy Ridge's diverse wildlife, some exclusive to the region, but all adapted to survive in sub-zero conditions. The deep snow reveals tracks * , but where do they lead? Spot hardy species such as the Manitoban elk roaming the frozen landscape, and for the lucky few, the majestic Great One mule deer. Stay alert for the sound of falling trees; this is the territory of the industrious North American beaver. EXPLORE THE FRIGID CANADIAN WILDERNESS If you’re bold enough to brave the snow, the wildlife in the Alberta Preserve is plentiful. Start your hunt at altitude in the dense boreal forests before heading down into the rugged foothills. Moving onwards, the contrasting sightlines of the grasslands afford easy spotting for snow geese and dusky grouse - an advantage even the most experienced hunters will appreciate. JOURNEY INTO ALBERTAN CULTURE Experience the diversity of the Albertan ecosystem, home of the First Nations, with 13 location-exclusive missions to complete. Help the warden and local experts to maintain the preserve on the trail of the secretive Sasquatch, and showcase your expertise with challenges designed to test even the most skilled hunters. To top it off, enjoy an epic soundtrack inspired by the wilderness and native cultures of Alberta. EXPAND YOUR FIREARM COLLECTION The new Laperriere Outrider .30-30 is a lever-action rifle with a side-loading and side-ejecting design, suited for small to medium game, like the whitetail deer or the gray wolf. Compatible with a wide range of rifle scopes, it is a versatile and powerful choice for hunting at any time of day or night. *Please note, the deep snow tracks feature can be turned on/off in the graphics settings menu.
short_description: It’s bitterly cold, but the Albertan wilderness is teeming with life. Discover and hunt 19 local species, tackle 13 exclusive missions, and explore this stunning snowy preserve.

reviews.review_text: The atmospher is amazing, the snow, the mountians, the few geese quacking around. But then you get the objective find some Beavers and its just weird but also funny at the same time.

By far this is one of the best maps in the whole entire game. The variety of animals in this map is A LOT, ranging from Ducks, Deer, Bison, or even Beavers. The Landscape is PHENOMENAL, mountains all around and lakes and rivers, including frozen rivers. Overall this map is a 9/10 totally recommend buying.
Stunningly beautiful

honestly if ur starting the game with 1 dlc get this its abt all (i) could want deer moose elk even wolves and as a swedish person im more biased cuz it has snow but still anyways 8.5/10 if ur starting out 9.5/10 if ur pretty gud already
(oh and the free 30-30 u get from ts is my fav gun)
It's pretty good. Not my favorite. It's also overpriced af

I was on the fence about how to review this- I wish there was a star level instead of just 'yes or no'. But the story is a core reason to consider a map purchase and is a large enough part of COTW that it's fully justified to NOT recommend this map, which is coming from a fairly lenient person.

Emotions I want to experience when I play a game: Joy, wonder, curiosity, excitement, achievement, splendor.
Emotions this story evokes: Disgust, stress, more stress, anxiety, regret (of choosing this map to play.) To a point it overwhelms the joy of the map design's actual quality and animal selection.

The bad guy had a very appealing voice actor and is likeable. The main story teller 'good guy' is hard to listen to (sorry whoever voiced it, I'm sure it was the writing and direction and not you, the takes and or generation was clean.)  But the story itself is SO BAD. I thought silver ridge was bad but this is somehow even worse. Like silver ridge had some redeeming moments and that wonder/curiosity and surprise at parts. I literally had to stop playing because it was too hard to enjoy the map with overbearing cringe level negativity. Woah.

Anyway if you take out the story and forget it exists, pretend it never happened and focus on THE MAP and level design, the experience, this is a great buy! The beavers work on crocodile logic of disappearing and the views are cleaner than most maps available- you actually have really great hunting odds with a lot of large game options. The game has hella dated graphics but there was real work put into this one to make it prettier than most of the other maps, and the lookouts are combined with outposts and have fast travel to the top so it might be the only time I'd ever use them outside of a mission which is nice. I feel like the premade hunting structure locations are quite nice and there are so many 'favorite hunting spots' to find on this map. Elk do tend to graze in valleys for some reason, and my map happened to have a lot of that. :)

As a long time player (who has spent way over $300 across many years and owns everything available to purchase except the new feeder packs and the cosmetics) and a lover of this game, I must say that these maps are all starting to feel the same and the game really needs something new and exciting. We have tons of maps to enjoy and relax within. Alberta is just another of the same with new skins! Its pretty but nothing new.
I'm not investing anything else into this game until we get something exciting, we really need new aggressive species like elephants, rhinos, dinosaurs...lol, how about an Africa 2 or a Jurassic Map please. Ill speak with my wallet until I see something that looks fun and new, invest the time please Dev's, the game, community, and players are worth it! Thanks for listening!

Got lots of animals, breaks several core mechanics of the game. Animal behavior is just completely random, they don't return to their need zones or will go to need zones that aren't their own. Tracks will randomly appear and disappear, making trails impossible to follow. That was enough for me to write this map off, 20 minutes tops. J U N K.

There is a bug that only effects current players, not new players, so they won't fix it. Because why would they do anything for the people that already gave them money.  They only fix bugs that could effect new sales.

The map looks beautiful in some areas (especially in the south) but mostly it is boring, generic and makes me feel like they didn't had much time and budget for it. Medved Taiga, on of the oldest maps and it looks 1000% better than the newest one. Scotland will probably be the same...

name: theHunter: Call of the Wild™ - Game Feeder Pack
description: Introducing Game Feeders - a new way to influence how animals behave. Decide on the ideal location, choose from three new Game Feeders, and select the right bait for a hunting experience like never before. Leverage your knowledge to attract animals, but stay alert for bears who can disrupt your carefully laid plans. Success comes to hunters who have the patience to wait for the perfect shot. THE BOX FEEDER - THE SELECTIVE HUNTER'S CHOICE Target deer species with precision. Four gravity-fed chutes attract your intended prey while keeping broader-snouted animals away. Elevated design protects bait from pests, but bears can destroy it—choose placement carefully for high-reward, high-risk hunting. Supported bait: Fortified Pellet Feed, Multigrain Flake Mix THE POST FEEDER - THE ELEVATED ADVANTAGE The raised structure of the Post Feeder has solar-powered technology that automatically dispenses bait over time. Its easy raise-and-lower refilling mechanism and robust metal construction withstands bear attacks. The Post Feeder is a reliable, long-term animal attraction without the risk. Supported bait: Fortified Pellet Feed, Multigrain Flake Mix THE BAIT BARREL FEEDER - SIMPLY UNBREAKABLE Thankfully, there's the Bait Barrel Feeder, which will keep the bears and boars busy and let the other animals eat from one of your other feeders. This rugged steel drum withstands aggressive wildlife without damage. Multiple drilled holes provide steady bait distribution while the blue barrel stays visible in dense terrain. It’s a prime choice when durability matters most. Supported bait: Sour Fermented Mash, Meat Scraps
short_description: Take your hunt to the next level with the new Game Feeder Pack! Choose from three feeders, study your prey, and learn to master placement, bait setting, and timing.

reviews.review_text: Great addition to the game that pairs well with the electronic caller for an essential kill pit.

I picked this DLC up the day before Scotland dropped, and now that I'm getting into my Great One Wild Boar grind, I have to say, the barrel feeder is a game-changer! Instead of hunting at night with night vision on the drink zones, you can set up these barrels and cull the boars in the daylight hours! Just makes things so much easier!

If you have your heart set on getting a Great One Boar on Scotland, seriously consider picking up this DLC if you haven't already.

I haven't played with the other types of feeders yet, but I imagine they would be useful in certain situations.

If you're not sure, wait for a good sale.
Glad we have bait feeders now with this DLC pack,will be useful with the treestands DLC and the hunting towers around each map.

The Game Feeder package is really good, and in my opinion it lets you set up some really nice hiking routes that you can follow. It also allows you to grind for rare animals and so on very effectively, without having to deal with herd management
Unbelievably strong.

they make you buy this then they made you buy this ingame.

sounds stupid? yes it is stupid

These were useful for three months until the devs decided they worked too well for a $6 DLC and nerfed them into uselessness.  If you bought this when it came out, you basically paid a $2/month subscription for them.  Now they are worthless and do not work as they were advertised, which I guess is legal in Sweden.

Customers were the only ones successfully baited by this DLC.  Sure as heck wasn't the hogs.
Pay to win
how do i refund this sh1t

Paywalling all of this and still not being able to lure coyotes with it is just too much for me to bear.

It's fun and allows for more options, but the majority of species that are lured by these are not so hard to hunt anyway.

https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3605306573
You're welcome.

If you're gonna take my money and make the feeders non-usable for certain species (e.g. Coyote, Eurasian Lynx, Bengal Tiger, etc.), then don't ask me for a thumb up.

name: theHunter: Call of the Wild™ - Premium Trophy Mount Pack
description: Now you can really show off your hard-earned trophies. The Premium Trophy Mount Pack comes with amazing-looking mounts, platforms, and plaques, and makes your trophies stand out as true centerpieces. Customize your lodge layout, create unique displays, and showcase your greatest achievements. What better way to remember those magic moments and give your trophies the attention they deserve? THE PREMIUM TROPHY MOUNT PACK - EVERYTHING YOU NEED TO CUSTOMIZE YOUR COLLECTION THIS PACK CONTAINS One Special Floor Mount - Supports Red Deer, Black Bear, Wild Boar, Roe Deer and European Bison One Special Wall Mount - Supports Red Fox, Ring-Necked Pheasant, Merriam Turkey, Coyote and Canada Goose Four Premium Platforms per size (40 total) - Authentic display to admire your achievements Four Premium Plaques per size (16 total) - Museum-quality presentation of your trophies
short_description: Put your hard-earned hunting achievements on display with the new, ornate trophy mounts. Great memories - yes! Good looking lodge - absolutely!

reviews.review_text:
2026 REVIEW:

When it comes to "theHunter: Call of the Wild" DLC, the Premium Trophy Mount Pack is a DLC that is more of a vanity purchase for those who enjoy the game and those who love to have to showcase the taxerdemy of their harvest (ie. kill).

While I am familiar with people do mounts, there are wealthy hunters who do showcase their lifesize mounts and while there those who can afford to travel worldwide and showcase their hunts, for many of us, we can experience that, maybe not in IRL but through this video game.

I will say that it's one thing to get a huge black bear or ox or even a mule deer but seeing the mountain goat mounted, that was way too freaky for me.

But it does look good and there are so many options for wall, floor mounts, trophy mounts, etc.  Definitely worth it if you get it cheap!

Nevertheless, with a plethora of DLC available for this game and so many things that one can hunt, it's like Pokemon (aside from the killing part) and you want to catch them all and showcase your harvest.  

Some mounts require you to have numerous animals, so yes, you will need to own DLC for that.  Fortunately, there are many DLC sales so you can get this DLC and maps for a great price.

DO YOU NEED THIS?

No, you don't need it.  But do you want it?  If you want to showcase what you got, then understandable.  Go for it!  

JUDGMENT CALL:

I think because "theHunter" sales happen, you can get this DLC affordably.  I would prioritize purchasing maps and weapons and certain accessories but if you have the money (real money to purchase, and in game money to purchase - Hint: Wand is your friend) and want to showcase that huge class 9 behemoth in your lodge (yes, another DLC to purchase), then go for it.

It's very clear about what you get in this pack., Some pedestals that you can change color on and shape of.

Is it mandatory? Absolutely not. 

However i cant understate how happy i was when the pedestal actually changed its secondary color to represent the harvest score of the animal you put on display, I.e. Bronze, Silver, gold and Diamond

I now have as a main display an Albino Bison with a Marble white pedestal with gold inlays which looks really nice and it really stands out when i can role-play as a rich posh duke-of-snob land safari hunter. 

That stated i think it lives up to the price if you enjoy the Trophy houses, otherwise just bag it in when it goes on sale!
Excellent
A nice upgrade from the standard trophy stands and plaques. I don't personally use the lodge all that much but i have many hours in the game so worth it overall.

I like this! For only four bucks at regular price, it makes all the trophies in my lodge a little bit shinier, and gives me more customizability. I like visiting museums when I have the chance, and looking around my lodge with these nicer plaques and platforms is like visiting a nicer museum rather than a low-budget one!

This DLC is not a huge game-changer, but if, like me, you've spent countless hours wandering around your lodges trying to get things to look just right and admiring your trophies, you might enjoy this DLC as I have!

If you're intrigued but put off by the price, just wait for a sale!

I got it as a bundle with the Scotland map, which made it only $1 or $2. I don't believe I would be able to justify paying anymore than that for it. The rock mount doesn't look that good imo, and the animals that can go on it as well as the branch are extremely limited. Of course, it says that in the description of the DLC, so it's not a surprise, but still disappointing. Why only one type of goose, turkey, fox and pheasant? As well as no lynx or bobcat? I feel as though those would make much more sense than the coyote so for them not to be included is a very odd choice. I have only seen the fox and coyote on the branch mount so far. They are not my favourite, but they do add a nice variety to the lodge and allows a lot more spaces for those species to be placed (I do not like head mounts for canids or felids) which is quite appreciated.

I do however quite like the plaques that come with it. My lodge looks much nicer now and I really like it for my rares lodge. At a quick glance you can see what the animal scored at without pulling up the information. The trim is rather pretty. Between the premium plaques and the free ones, I much prefer the premium version. 

I would not spend anymore $2, but if you can catch it on sale and enjoy your lodge I would say it is worth it. I would encourage it is not purchased at full price. It is a bit steep for what is included, especially considering for $5 or so more you can get an entire new map to play.

Thank you for attending my TED talk, I hope this review has helped. 🙏
legit the only DLC for cotw that felt useless and completely un-needed. it barely adds anything new and is overpriced. skip this.
Disappointing.

Was super pumped to get this DLC but you can only put like 4 animals on the special mounts which is just so stupid. so many other animals can fit on them they just dont let you for whatever reason. SUPER SUPER disapointed

Bit of false/untruthful advertising, if you get this and only own Spring Manor, you will not be able to use Red Deer or European Bison on the special mount as it is limited to only circular mounts of which it only has 3, all are large or smaller. Not stating it to be only be able to be used on circular mounts is a huge disservice to paying customers.

name: theHunter: Call of the Wild™ - Scotland Hunting Reserve
description: Breathtaking! Torr nan Sithean - our Scotland Hunting Reserve - offers legendary hunting among towering Highlands mountains, green valleys, glittering lochs and Celtic woodlands. This reserve comes with 17 iconic animals, 8 main missions and 10 side missions. This is a hunting experience enshrouded in legends! A STUNNING SCENERY - IF A PICTURE SAYS A THOUSAND WORDS, A VIEW SAYS A MILLION Close your eyes and imagine Scotland. Do you see legendary landmarks like old castles and ruins, vast landscapes, deep blue lochs, and picturesque fishing villages? This is what you will see from one of the many lookouts in our Scottish reserve. Take out your binoculars because besides unique and iconic animals, you might also see standing stones doubling as time indicators and - wait - whispering stones..? THE MYTH OF SCOTLAND - THE OLD IS NEW ONCE MORE Hunt in a unique reserve - a vast landscape where you'll feel like it is only you and the wild animals roaming through misty Highland air. Take a deep breath and be transported back in time. Old legends make the atmosphere feel mythical. Turn sound on for original Gaelic-inspired music. Everything is old, yet new! ICONIC SCOTTISH WILDLIFE - A WIDE ROSTER OF BOTH FAMILIAR AND UNIQUE SPECIES In this reserve, you will find a roster of 18 animals with diverse classifications - some remastered classics, others incredibly fast, and some where you might have to look twice to make sure you saw what you think you saw. These animals will challenge your hunting skills, so if you have a hunting dog, it will be invaluable as a companion.
short_description: We are going to Scotland! The highlands are home to Celtic legends, old castles, and vast landscapes. Hunt iconic Scottish animals in a reserve where every path winds through history.

reviews.review_text:
It's alright. Not as big as some other maps but the rolling hills + massive lakes makes for some good hunting spots. My review is based on my 20~ hours on PS5 version, I just also happen to play PC lol. But the badgers and other small wildlife additions are cool, the "sit at the top of a hill and spot 300m in all directions" is worth it for me on this one. Like with all other DLC on this game, I recommend waiting for a sale to get it if the animal list interests you. I'm also slightly biased because I got an albino diamond sika deer on this one. I also play a bit differently than the meta, tend to just wander like I'm hiking and get incidental kills, and it's really good for that playstyle because of all the open ground instead of annoying densely wooded areas. Still, I'd pick the Nepal map over this for variety of biomes and size. I can recommend it, but not as strongly as bigger maps.

(Side note PLEASE UPDATE THE AFRICA MAP I'm BEGGING THE DEVS....IT COULD'VE BEEN AMAZING PLEEAAASE) okay I'm done, good hunting everyone.

Lots of small critters - largest game on the reserve is red deer. Leave the .300 Win Mag at camp.

The main story missions are a bit hit-or-miss on Tòrr nan Sithean, but the warden is hilarious and has great commentary.

The scenery is beautiful - definitely my favorite reserve in terms of aesthetics. The reserve consists of rolling hills and plenty of meadows. There are also some wooded areas here and there to keep everything from feeling too samey.

If you have to choose only one DLC reserve, get this one.

I loved this map. :) It's a bit of a mix of Te Awora and Silver ridge- you can actually hunt on it with decent views, work put into the various discoverables around the map, some myths and legends tastes. When it comes to story "How does this affect the player?" and "Why is the player interested?" should always be asked and I'm not sure the writers for these really ask it enough so my take is simply 'Was the story bad? Did I turn it off?' And in this case it was entertaining and enjoyable. Really great map.

over all a really beautiful like who does not love scotland right?
would be very cool if they have the babour jacket outfit.
A relaxing and nostalgic experience, though the minks are too elusive and the goats too annoying!

Come back after years of not playing to find all the same bugs are there like miraclous healing despite being 0-25% on blood loss. Nothing has changed in the bugs of this game same core problems. EW seems to have got rid of their programmers and only kept on designers and even that is looking thin now. This game has been milked of all the DLC it could have.
i bought this and it reset my game to to 11 months before and i cant get it back. i've uninstalled the dlc and i've tried restarted my game nothing seems to work

Great looking map, but small. After having played a day, I'd completed most achievements already.
It's not worth 11-12 euros. Better wait untill it drops significantly.
the second quest is bugged out for me with the haggis in the tree. Won't let me spot the animal and I can't progress not a bad map tho. just stupid bugs that shouldn't be there
Gamwe is loaded with bugs. cant claim killed wildlife, couldnt claim the first location. highly disappointing

name: theHunter: Call of the Wild™ - Game Feeder Pack 2
description: Introducing Game Feeder Pack 2! This solid and indestructible trio of feeders - the Mineral Lick, Scent Tube, and Carcass Feeder - are specifically designed to attract a diverse array of species, including elusive carnivores and predators. Study your prey, find the perfect location and bait, and beware or you might just become the bait yourself. THE MINERAL LICK FEEDER - THE ESSENTIAL HERBIVORE MAGNET Target previously unbaitable herbivore groups like sheep, goats, and bovines. The Mineral Lick Feeder draws in hooved wildlife craving vital sodium and essential minerals, offering a unique opportunity to attract and hunt these elusive species. Strategic placement is key to make the most of its powerful lure. Supported bait: Salt Block THE SCENT TUBE FEEDER - THE CARNIVORE'S CALL Project the primal scent of prey with the Scent Tube Feeder, made to attract carnivores and scavengers such as foxes, wolves, and a variety of small predators like mustelids. Its durable design and perforated structure let the scented meat aroma to spread, attracting predators while limiting direct access to the bait. Master its placement to draw out the most elusive hunters. Supported bait: Scented Meat Scraps THE CARCASS FEEDER - THE ULTIMATE PREDATOR LURE The biggest predators will come to the imposing Carcass Feeder, designed exclusively to attract the largest and most dominant game. These animals rarely share, meaning only one group will feed at a time. By elevating the bait, it keeps smaller scavengers away, offering a perfect big game hunting opportunity. Pursue the ultimate thrill, but beware when the predators come, or you’ll become the bait! Supported bait: Carcass
short_description: Expand your hunt with Game Feeder Pack 2. Three new specialized feeders attract herbivores, omnivores, and even predators, so be prepared!

reviews.review_text: A really good bundle for tiger hunting or predator hunting

Yes!
Feeder Pack 2 (and Feeder pack 1) is gold for me.

I am 99% an ambusher, and prefer to observe and wait (in safety).

Now, with "Game Feeder pack 2", I am finally able to enjoy Parque Fernando (and other maps with Buffalo/Bison/Yaks etc...) my way. Put down a mineral lick and they will likely come to it (if the mineral lick is in their 'neighbourhood' and it is eating time). I can sit in a tripod/hunting stand and wait.

The Feeder Packs (1 and 2), adds many possibilities and playtime for me, who likes waiting, safely, in tripods, hunting stands and so on...
Especially the bigger and more dangerous animals, like Wolves, Tigers, Gators... , Buffalo/Bison... that I'm not comfortable hunting on foot, can be comfortably lured, while observing in your tripod/stand.

But also Sheep/Goats, and the Mustelids, that were introduced with the Scotland map, can now be lured in.

And added too with this pack, is a MAP SPECIFIC, PER FEEDER listing of animals interested in your (fx.) mineral lick. This listing is shown when you hit "E" to fill the feeder you just placed. This map specific animal listing has also been introduced to the feeders from the first Feeder pack.

Great additions EW, thank you.

Just remember, you do NOT need ANY DLCs to play, or complete the base game at all!!! That being said.

Depending on which map you are on...

[h2]HANGING CARCASS FEEDER[/h2]
Possible species: 
Eurasian Lynx, Mountain Lion, Puma, Bengal Tiger, Lion, Saltwater Crocodile, and American Alligator.

[h2]SCENT TUBE FEEDER[/h2]
Possible species: 
Gray Wolf, Iberian Wolf, Tibetan Fox, Red Fox, Gray Fox, American Mink, Eurasian Pine Marten, European Badger, Coyote, Side-Striped Jackal, Bobcat, Mexican Bobcat, Eurasian Lynx, Snow Leopard, Common Raccoon, Raccoon Dog, Collared Peccary, Warthog, Feral Pig, Wild Boar, Wild Hog, Black Bear, Brown Bear, and Grizzly Bear.

[h2]MINERAL LICK FEEDER[/h2]
Possible species: 
Blue Sheep, Desert Bighorn Sheep, Iberian Mouflon, Chamois, Rocky Mountain Bighorn Sheep, Feral Goat, Pronghorn, Beceite Ibex, Gredos Ibex, Southeastern Spanish Ibex, Ronda Ibex, Himalayan Tahr, Mountain Goat, Siberian Musk Deer, Axis Deer, Hog Deer, Javan Rusa Deer, Sika Deer, Mule Deer, Roe Deer, Blacktail Deer, Whitetail Deer, Red Deer, Fallow Deer, Sambar, Mountain Reindeer, Barasingha, Lesser Kudu, Northern Red Muntjac, Blackbuck, Springbok, Gemsbok, Grant Caribou, Woodland Caribou, Blue Wildebeest, Nilgai, Manitoban Elk, Rocky Mountain Elk, Roosevelt Elk, Moose, Banteng, Cape Buffalo, Water Buffalo, European Bison, Wood Bison, Plains Bison, and Wild Yak.


--------------------------------------------------------

I got this when it came out right away. Love the feeder pack 1. 
First thing I did was slap down the croc feeder in the middle of the swampy area to the south on the Emerald Coast map. Went back to my text and turned it to night time, then skipped ahead for a second night. Went back to the feeder, scoped around and found a piebald croc (rare skin) just sitting there, waiting to be shot!

10/10 would shoot again.

The new salt lick is also crazy nice. Cheaper feeder when you need to buy more in game, seems to feed just about everything on the Emerald Coast map as well. Missing kangaroo sadly.
As someone who hates running around lakes for the Gray Wolves, this DLC really helps. Maybe slightly overpriced but if this is an issue wait for a sale.

Now it is time to update storage user interface.

Ps. add new Scents and lures
the buy button on the feeders to refil them doesn't do anything? Is this broken? The feeders are useless if you can't fill them. Starting to wonder if you can get a refund on a dlc.
Better get your money's worth out of them before the next patch- Once their beloved streamers get their GOs off of these feeders they'll nerf them for us plebs just like the last set I'm sure.

name: theHunter: Call of the Wild™ - Multi-Class Weapon Pack
description: The new Multi-Class Weapon Pack comes with 4 new weapons: 3 bolt-action rifles and a unique combination gun. From small game precision to large game power, this pack gears you up for every hunt. Make every shot count with new ammo types and experience a powerful gun feel, from weighty recoil to dynamic adaptability. RICHARDSSON .500: UNMATCHED STOPPING POWER The Richardsson .500 is a powerful straight-pull rifle for the biggest game, offering a distinct, weighty feel. Its de-cocking safety is chambered in the .500 Jeffery, making it your ultimate choice for large game (classes 8-9) at long ranges. Ammunition: .500 Jeffery for solid rounds for extreme penetration or Soft-Point for exceptional expansion. ALIS PRECISION SHOT .25-06: VERSATILE MID-RANGE PERFORMANCE The ALIS Precision Shot .25-06 is an iconic, reliable choice for medium game, offering a flat trajectory and versatile ammo for diverse animal classes. This iconic bolt-action rifle, around for nearly a century, provides considerable force and comfortable recoil, perfecting your medium game hunts. Ammunition: .25-06 features Polymer-Tip for precise penetration on classes 3-7, and noticeably lighter Soft-Point for controlled expansion on classes 2-6, both effective up to 200m. JONEROS .17HMR: SMALL GAME SPECIALIST WITH EXTENDED RANGE The Joneros .17HMR is a highly accurate small game and bird specialist, offering an exceptionally flat trajectory for easier long-range aiming. This miniature rifle has quick second shots and 10-round magazine, making it ideal for varmints and small predators (classes 1-2), giving you extended zeroing modes up to 300m. Ammunition: .17HMR for Full Metal Jacket for a flat trajectory and high penetration or Polymer-Tip for increased expansion, both great for classes 1-2 with a 125m effective range. ​​CASSIDY COMBINATION SHOTGUN: ADAPTABLE AND SEAMLESS SWITCH The Cassidy Combination Gun lets you switch between rifle and shotgun, giving you unmatched adaptability for varying situations. This classic break-action weapon is essentially two guns in one, covering small game at long distances with its .22 Magnum rifled barrel, and a wide range of animals at close quarters with its 12-gauge shotgun barrel. Hold reload to switch ammo type and adapt to any hunting scenario. Ammunition: Uses existing calibers: .22 Mag Jacketed Hollow Point or Hollow Point for classes 1-2. For 12 GA, choose Birdshot (class 1), Buckshot (classes 2-5), or Slug (classes 4-8).
short_description: From small to big game, the Multi-Class Weapon Pack gears you up for every hunt. Carefully balanced for all levels of mastery, it’s perfect for building your personal go-to loadout.

reviews.review_text:
A nice selection of new guns if you prefer variety over chasing the "meta" of a hunting game. 

The .25-06 covering classes 2-7 lets you hit class 2 species while still being able to take class 6s more reliably than the 2-6 guns. Ideal for a map like Scotland where red deer is the largest species.

I love combo guns so the .22 WMR/12 GA is right up my alley. It'd make a sleek but challenging way to single-gun Salzwiesen. Also perfect for new players to hunt small game if they haven't unlocked birdshot or bought a DLC that has .22LR.

The .17 HMR and .500 Jeffery I don't plan on tying to specific maps, but instead I'll bring them into any map when I'm focusing on species at the lowest or highest end of the class scale (buffalo missions, etc.).

A good mix of guns and nice to get four of them.

Frustrated with rifles firing blanks? Tired of crocs hiding underwater after a perfect shot? Looking to future-proof for the elusive Africa 2.0? Wanna obliterate those pesky rabbits into molecules? 

Well my friend, look no further! No matter which direction the animals are in - the new .500 rifle will help you remove that direction. One shot from this thing is gonna set you back an annual budget of a small nation, and it boasts such a recoil it's gonna burst out of your display and kick you in the teeth. Fortunately, you won't need to shoot more than once.

For all you red deer enthusiasts out there - this DLC got you covered as well. The Alis Perfection rifle hits hard, handles smoothly and the iron sights are chef's kiss. It's time to turn over your Garands to a local museum.

And for my fellow ADHD who just can't decide what you wanna hunt and get anxious whenever the game makes you feel like you're missing out - the new shotgun lets you hunt bears while you're hunting birds... it also works the other way around.

This DLC is a true return to form for the Expensive Worlds - here's hoping they will continue expanding our arsenal with much-needed additions like suppressors, bigger magazines, thermal vision, anti-tank rifles, helicopters, artillery, nuclear missiles... okay, maybe not that last one.
i just started playing on askiy ridge. This gun pack works well with the new map. 3 new guns, class 1 to 9, no fuss! Great weapon pack!

Genuinely fun so far, like HSR or Etheria Restart meets Umamusume (with a dash of GoV:Nikke)

Have to keep in mind a lot of Steam reviews for Gacha games aren't always as nice as you'd find elsewhere.

Animations and character lines are well done, the character designs are fun and entertaining. If you enjoy turn based combat, its got it. If you enjoy the kind of deck building, almost rogue-lite Umamusume offers, it has that too. At the same time, when it comes to leveling characters up, the limit breaks and sync'ing etc.. a lot is very reminiscent of GoV:Nikke.

The Gacha rates could be better, but at the same time, I prefer this more drawn out 200-pull requirement up front, compared to the weird flow-chart you have to follow for some other recent releases (Arknights Endfield.. factory building was great, combat was lame, sorry). I prefer the ol' 50/50 80-90 pull banner system just because I'm used to it I guess?

Overall though, the story, the character lines when you play through the story and journey modes, animations, all come together to really help you enjoy the experience. This one will be replacing a couple I was briefly trying and one I had actually started playing. I personally prefer the gachas more like the traditional 80-90 pulls for the 50/50, but that's completely subjective from playing others.

If you enjoy HSR, Etheria, and/or Umamusume, you should definitely give this a shot, with as strange as that combo might sound.

name: RACCOIN: Coin Pusher Roguelike
description: RACCOIN: Coin Pusher Roguelike blends the chaotic fun of classic arcade coin pushers with the element of roguelike deckbuilding. Combine special coins with powerful items to trigger satisfying synergies. It’s a nonstop dopamine rush packed into one unpredictable, coin dropping ride. Discover all kinds of special coins during your runs! Combine Seed Coins with Water Coins to grow a money tree right inside your machine. Drop a Cat Coin to hunt down every last Rat Coin and rack up extra tickets. Need more points? Use a MultiCoin to boost your score across the board. Too slow? Blast a TNT Coin to trigger a massive explosion and blast every coin forward at once! Uncover 150 unique items that can dramatically shift the course of your run. Stack multipliers, spawn coins with extra points, or simply shake the machine when you're running dry. Apply special plating to your favorite coins to enhance their effects and unlock even wilder combos. Play as one of several unique characters, each with their own set of themed coins and playstyle. Take on the role of a greedy raccoon manager who excels at math-based combos, or a quirky biologist who specializes in animal-based coins. Every character offers a fresh and wildly different way to play. 6 playable characters, each with their own unique coins 150 distinct coins with special effects 150 power-up items with unique abilities Endless run mode for infinite replayability 8 difficulty levels to master
short_description: A coin-pushing dopamine machine. RACCOIN is a roguelike deckbuilder where you combine special coins with powerful items to trigger wildly satisfying combos. Stack shiny towers, spin the lucky wheel, and shake the machine until all the coins drop.

Not a particularly good intersection of roguelites and physical-toys-turned-videogame, in my opinion. Looks good but the game feel is just not there for me--the game often lacks the explosiveness that other games it's clearly referencing (Balatro, for instance) has. I am well aware that the choices I'm making are having some sort of effect, but it often feels so piddling or pointless I can't bring myself to care. Moments of "Woah, nice!!!" are so few and far between, and if I'm not playing this game for moments like that, I'm kind of unsure why I am in the first place. That and the fact that the game is built around a steady stream of coin pushing that extremely punishes you near the end of the run if it ever stops for any reason (which you can have so so little control over), overall just doesn't feel great. Might be able to be something I could recommend in the future, but at the moment it feels as much a screen saver as cookie clicker, except it looks a bit nicer and you're paying 12 dollars for that.

Honestly, it's ok. Not amazing or ground breaking like games such as Balatro were. It's fun for a while but then gets a bit boring, at least in my experience. For what I paid it gave me a bit of fun but probably could be priced a bit lower, in my opinion. 

Hard to tell which items are going to be really good and which aren't, but I am rather dumb so that's to be expected. 

If you've got money to burn and a few hours to kill or you enjoy partaking in "gardening" while mindlessly firing coins in to a machine then go for it. Otherwise I'd say skip.
They managed to make this game very boring, it's not fun if your never let me win. The scores you need to achieve are outrageous.

I really wish that I could give this game a "Meh" score. There is a lot I like about it in terms of style, gimmick, mechanics, etc., but honestly I can't in good faith recommend this game because it is so unbelievably unbalanced that you will not get your money's worth out of it before you've already beaten everything the game has to throw at you.

I'm someone who actually likes being broken in video games, Risk of Rain 2 is one of my favorite games and it lets you get CRAZY powerful. BUT, even in RoR2, you still always have the opportunity to fail, and you need to still think on your feet and plan out your build.

In Raccoin... you just get the Percentoin, and you win. I unlocked every single piece of metaprogression in the game in my first 3 runs, and it only took me that long because I hadn't bothered to pick up a Percentoin until run #3. I got to round 286, and only stopped because I was bored out of my skull of watching the game win for me with barely any effort. I could not lose even if I wanted to, and I mean that literally. The actions I would need to take to get the game to pop up the "Bankrupt" prompt would guarantee I beat the boss before I could even reach that point. No strategy, no clever tricks, not even a second coin needed. Just the Percentoin.

So yeah, if you can't tell that pretty much soured my experience of the game. If you do pick it up, don't use the Percentoin (at least until the balance it or remove it from the game)

TLDR; Fun game, but the balance is so off that I unlocked everything in the game before my 4th run

name: Hearts of Iron IV
description: Expansion Pass 2 About the Game Take charge of history’s greatest war machines in Hearts of Iron IV , a grand strategy wargame that challenges your strategic abilities and political insight. Refight World War II at the head of nation from the time period, building up an industrial powerhouse and a diplomatic alliance that will effectively wage war on ideological opponents. Hearts of Iron IV is more than a historical simulation. Alternate historical paths open up with almost every decision you make, from small ones such as where to launch your attack to crack open Fortress Europe, to large ones, such as whether to re-establish democracies or long forgotten monarchies. In Hearts of Iron IV , you can plan the military strategy almost down to the rivets, as you design division templates for your armies, research new modes of production and means of destruction, set out factory instructions and pursue political objectives. Almost no part of the war goes untouched as you guide a nation of your choice through humanity’s darkest hour. This purchase comes with the Hearts of Iron IV base game as well as four expansion packs: Death or Dishonor: focuses on eastern and central European middle powers Together for Victory: focuses on British Commonwealth nations Waking the Tiger: focuses on China and Japan Man the Guns: focuses on naval features and alternate history paths for the United States
short_description: Victory is at your fingertips! Your ability to lead your nation is your supreme weapon, the strategy game Hearts of Iron IV lets you take command of any nation in World War II; the most engaging conflict in world history.

[h1]Age of Empires II: Definitive Edition[/h1]
[h3]Dishonest and Untrue to the Classic Version[/h3]
[h3]Really Hard to Recommend[/h3]

Over the years, Age of Empires II has gone through so many changes, updates, and both good and bad DLCs that it now looks like an unrecognizable cultural crucible far removed from its original concept. At this point, it feels like an endless title that keeps expanding in such a disproportionate and careless way that the game no longer feels cohesive, but instead feels like an overloaded collection of unbalanced campaigns and civilizations. 

The fact that there are so many balance changes immediately after new civilizations are released shows an incredible lack of proper planning and testing in the DLCs, and it makes it clear the game isn’t being expanded in a healthy way. The same thing happens with the new campaigns and historical battles, many of them are poorly designed, with objectives that don’t trigger properly, unbalanced missions, and campaign stories that are terribly built.

Another issue is the monthly and seasonal events system. It’s a strange mechanic that feels out of place in a strategy game, especially since it has little to no relevance in this title. I think that for many years now they’ve been pushing an awkward reward system to retain players and encourage them to come back after a certain period of time, but it feels completely unnecessary and forced for this type of game.

Overall, for me this game is still very enjoyable and nostalgic, and it has clearly evolved a lot over time. However, since most of these changes have been for the worse and the game doesn’t seem to be changing for a better direction anytime soon, I can’t really recommend this title, despite it being one of the games I grew up with and a classic among strategy games.

[h3]Gameplay[/h3]
Age of Empires II has always been a very fun RTS since its release more than two decades ago. However, from the very start to this day most civilizations feel almost exactly the same, with only some minor changes for available technologies, unique units and bonuses that barely makes them feel different from one another. Of course, this is actually a good thing from a competitive standpoint, since it keeps the game balanced and ensures that matches depend more on player skill and strategy rather than on the selected civilization. But as a result, you end up with players relying on low-resource exploits like wall trapping and other cheesy strategies, which can feel frustrating and unfun to deal with, especially for casual players.

Another issue this game had since its early days was the lack of proper characterization for each civilization, since many of them shared the same architectural style and the same visual appearance for their shared units. Fortunately, part of this issue has been addressed through many updates that have finally made each civilization visually unique and more personalized after so many years. 

Lastly, its gameplay is one of the most standardized in the RTS industry, which is both its biggest strength and its biggest weakness. On one hand, Age of Empires II has basic resource-gathering and unit management mechanics that are very easy to understand and learn, which is great for new players, but on the other hand, the lack of a more complex system makes the game very repetitive and dull over time. 

[h3]Art and World Design[/h3]
This version has nice graphics that feel refreshing and vibrant. However, this new visual style sometimes can also feel oversaturated. For example, the fog effect is very distracting and completely unnecessary for the game’s atmosphere, colors are excessively flashy, the texture blurring is extremely annoying and significantly reduces map visibility. Personally, I prefer playing without any of these options because they only make the visuals more distracting and reduce the overall match readability, which negatively affects gameplay.

The game also received a complete visual redesign for its main menu, cutscenes and the campaign selection screen, which now look much more polished compared to the original version. Personally, this is one of the aspects I liked the most about this version.

[h3]Music and Sound effects[/h3]
The soundtrack is another aspect that received many improvements. The tracks now sound much cleaner due to their better audio quality and also more refined with the introduction of new short arrangements. As for the sound effects, there isn’t much to say. The game keeps the same audio cues and unit voice lines but with slightly improved sound quality.

name: Theater Pack - Hearts of Iron IV: Thunder at our Gates
description: Step into the heart of command in Thunder at Our Gates , a Theater Pack for Hearts of Iron IV . Expand on the art of organizing a campaign with new features such as military headquarters and navy captains, as well as unique new content for nations caught between rival empires; Australia, Siam and Indonesia. Hearts of Iron IV: Thunder at Our Gates includes: Australia Focus Tree: Support your Allies across the world and hold fast as the last bastion against Japan in the Pacific, or make use of your unstable political climate to change your course entirely. Siam Focus Tree: Turn rival nations into pawns, exploit the great powers and carve out your own empire in Southeast Asia. Indonesia Focus Tree: Steer the course of Indonesia’s future; remain under Dutch rule, or seize the moment to fight for independence alone, or under a new patron. Military HQs : Deploy your generals where they matter. Wield local command and support your troops through synergies with updated command abilities, proximity, and new division design features. Ship Captains : Named captains lead your major ships, where they earn skills and can be promoted to admirals to command your fleets
short_description: Step into the heart of command in Thunder at Our Gates, a Theater Pack for Hearts of Iron IV; featuring new content for Australia, Siam, Indonesia and more.

name: Total War: WARHAMMER - Chaos Warriors
description: Immortal Empires The Chaos Warriors DLC Pack is playable in The Old World (Total War: WARHAMMER), Mortal Empires (Total War: WARHAMMER II), and Immortal Empires (Total War: WARHAMMER III). About the Game Total War: WARHAMMER - Chaos Warriors The Chaos Warriors brings a new race with its own new Legendary Lords, units, mounts, items, quest chains and mechanics to Total War™: WARHAMMER®. Three new playable Legendary Lords. New Chaos Warrior mounts, units and monsters. New quests, magic items and abilities. New horde campaign playstyle. Lay waste to the Old World as the dreaded Chaos Warriors. This Pack includes a full roster of bloodthirsty units, terrifying mounts and monstrous creatures. Lead the corrupted Chaos hordes as one of three new playable Legendary Lords. Embrace the Ruinous Powers as you embark on brand new quest chains, unlocking powerful items and gaining glory in the name of the dark Chaos Gods. Beyond Kislev, beyond Troll Country, beyond even the barren Norsca Peninsula lies a land shrouded in darkness, frozen in an unnatural, never-ending winter. Here, the mortal realm and the immaterial realm of Chaos overlap, creating an uncontrollable gateway to that infernal dimension from which all manner of nightmarish creatures spew forth. It is a place where few mortals have dared to venture, and even fewer have returned. The wild people that live in the lands to the south are devout in their worship of the Chaos Gods and many seek to journey north and offer themselves up to become tools of their will. Those that do survive this unholy pilgrimage and live to tell the tale are irreversibly altered. Clad in dark armour bearing the dread marks of ruinous favour, possessed of an unwavering resolve to carry out the will of their malevolent deities, with the indomitable Archaon the Everchosen as their leader, these are the mighty Chaos Warriors! This pack makes the entire Chaos Warrior race from the main game playable in the Grand Campaign and multiplayer matches; as well as adding entirely new Legendary Lords, Lords, Heroes, Units, Magic Items and more. Three new Legendary Lords. Lead the Chaos Warriors as one of three new Legendary Lords, each with their own unique bonuses: Sigvald the Magnificent: Beautiful from birth, Sigvald’s fondness for hedonistic excess saw him exiled from his tribe but also gained him a powerful patron: Slaanesh. Now, he heads an army devoted to himself. Slaying anything he deems ugly or unsavoury, he fights as much for his perverse ego as he does for Slaanesh. Kholek Suneater: Kholek Suneater is a formidable figure to behold. A gigantic Dragon Ogre, believed by many to be the second eldest of all Dragon Ogres, he towers over the battlefield swinging his gigantic hammer with ease. He is considered by some to be a God. Archaon the Everchosen: A former Sigmarite Templar, Archaon stumbled upon an ancient doctrine that contained within the secrets of his faith. Renouncing his allegiance to Sigmar he journeyed north to the dreaded Chaos Wastes to offer his service to the Chaos Gods. Here, the Ruinous Powers gave him their blessing to set out on his quest to become the Everchosen; the Lord of the End Times. New Horde playstyle Introduces Horde-style gameplay to Total War: WARHAMMER, allowing the Chaos Warriors to raze enemy settlements and set up encampments anywhere, taking their war spoils with them. The armies of Chaos rest only when exhaustion halts their marching, leaving nothing but corruption and ash in their wake. New units, buildings, items and technologies. Unlock new units, buildings and technologies. Seek out the powerful Artefacts of Chaos and become the true Herald of the Apocalypse. Spread corruption in the name of Chaos! The foul energies that drive the Chaos Warriors leave ripples that permeate any place they pass through, influencing the populace. Use this to spread dissent amongst the enemies of Chaos, creating cultist uprisings through the proliferation of dark worship. Key Units: Chaos Warriors. No longer fully human, the Chaos Warriors have been altered to become living weapons, honed perfectly to do their dark masters’ work. Imbued with the strength of chaos, enrobed in dark hell-forged armour, they can best several mortal warriors easily. They have no need for food, drink, sleep or other mortal comforts, subsisting on carnage alone. Chaos Dragon (Mount). Once, these great beasts sailed the skies as their brethren still do. Now, corrupted by change, these two-headed dragons are malevolent predators in their own right. One head spews forth dark flame, the other corrosive gas. Only the most powerful can ride such a monster, and even then it is only a temporary alliance, Chaos Dragons can never be tamed. Forsaken. Once proud men, their long-standing service to the Chaos Gods has left them altered. Where once they killed in the name of ambition and glory, their mutations have made them something less than human. Now they fight, whirlwinds of flailing flesh, hair and claw, only to satisfy their unending, unnatural hunger. Dragon Ogres. These magnificent, terrible beasts are amongst the most ancient and impressive of all living creatures. Their immortality is a gift from the Chaos Gods, given in exchange for an eternity of dark servitude. Seeking out lightning, they swing their gigantic weapons with ease, cleaving all but the most powerful warriors to ribbons. Gorebeast Chariot. A massive construction of hell-forged metal, mutant beast and jagged blade, the Gorebeast Chariot is ridden by some of the most powerful Warriors of Chaos and serves a single purpose: to shatter enemy lines with unstoppable force. Hellcannon. The Hellcannon is a massive artifice of metal and daemonic power that hurls bolts of raw arcane energy across the battlefield, vaporising anything in range. Part war machine, part Daemon, this infernal construct is powered by the liquefied remains of fallen enemies and operated by a sturdy crew of Chaos Dwarfs. Full Unit Roster: Legendary Lords: Archaon the Everchosen – Melee/Caster (Lore of Fire) (Race Leader) Prince Sigvald – Melee Kholek Suneater – Melee Lords: Chaos Sorcerer Lord – Caster (Lore of Death, Metal or Fire) Chaos Lord – Melee Heroes: Chaos Sorcerer – Caster (Lore of Death, Metal or Fire) Exalted Hero – Melee Mounts: Dorghar, Archaon’s War Horse (Archaon only) Manticore Chaos Dragon Chaos Steed Barded Chaos Steed Melee Infanty: Chaos Marauders Chaos Marauders (Great Weapons) Chaos Warriors Chaos Warriors (Great Weapons) Chaos Warriors (Halberds) Chosen Chosen (Great Weapons) Chosen (Halberds) Forsaken Melee Monstrous Infantry: Dragon Ogres Chaos Spawn Chaos Trolls Chaos Trolls (Armoured) Melee War Beasts: Chaos Warhounds Chaos Warhounds (Poison) Melee Monsters: Dragon Ogre Shaggoth Chaos Giant Melee Cavalry: Chaos Knights Chaos Knights (Lances) Missile Cavalry: Marauder Horsemen Marauder Horsemen (Throwing Axes) Vehicles: Chaos Chariot Gorebeast Chariot Artillery: Hellcannon These units are exclusive to the Chaos Warriors and will not appear in the base game if you do not have the DLC.
short_description: The Chaos Warriors Race Pack brings a new race with its own new Legendary Lords, units, mounts, items, quest chains and mechanics to Total War™: WARHAMMER®.

reviews.review_text:
[This is review for how this DLC works in Warhammer 3]
I'm really happy with what they did to Warriors of Chaos in Warhammer 3. This DLC started off as a really meh experience. Not only was it cut out content from the first game, locked behind preorder "bonus", faction itself was not very fun to play or interact with. 
Now, with big rework in game 3, I think they are really fun faction to play as. Very easy to play, since Warriors infantry is just attack move to win, but still, very fun. They also are always fun to fight against, at least for me. 
Overall, very good DLC.
10/10 DLC in Immortal Empires, if you like to destroy the world, than this DLC is for you :)
On sale, very much bang for your buck type dlc. Lords are okay/good, but nothing crazy.
With all dlc its best pokemon exp
Basically you just spawn and go kill everything. I thought it was better looking than actually playing it as I got it on day 1 because Chaos - Sexy Thing.
CA GREED
CA cant make a working game thematic warhammer game without modders.

I find playing this faction to be both disappointing and annoying. The horde mechanic is interesting, but ultimately it heavily constrains the number of armies you can control. This makes it so that while you are destroying and razing the cities of the Empire, another faction will just move in around and behind you to resettle those cities. I does not seem to matter how much Chaos corruption you leave behind in a province, or how far away the razed settlements are. Someone will always come and claim them, meaning you need to take your one army, or one of two armies if you can afford it, to go back and wipe them out. Then the process repeats.

Your Norscan allies are absolutely worthless, and will often fall into fighting each other, rather than getting with the program and going down South to raid and fight the enemy. Perhaps if they actually did what they were supposed to, other factions wouldn't have such a clear and easy time colonizing the wreckage you leave behind.

As an added note. This faction was clearly all there. The horde mechanics are from Attila, and all of the units are present in the game for the end of the world mechanics built into the main campaign. Meaning that they are making us pay for this to make us pay for this. This should have been free with the first game, but their greed knows no bounds. I would not recommend this at all.

I have over 3k hours in this game, it is without a doubt my most played game. That being said, my reviews for all products in this franchise are being changed to reflect the lack of support and continued negligence of the publisher. The Lizardman and Tomb Kings AI are bugged, the legendary lords do not move and idly wait to be killed. Furthermore the gate bug has still not been addressed. And lastly they have delayed and delayed DLC with no communication, outright going against the last public statement showing they do not value their customers.
