I don't really like most of the mechanics they added here. I don't think this represents what playing as Nuln and Wissenland should be. It is, for the most part, simply a rich province with especially good artillery and handgunners. There should have been a Lord focusing on this alone, and not an amethyst witch with magic crap imbuing her troops.
The final tier of the armory is bugged and unreachable

name: Total War: WARHAMMER III - Skulltaker – Omens of Destruction
description: The Skulltaker DLC pack for Total War: WARHAMMER III introduces Khorne’s Immortal Champion and the greatest of all Bloodletters, Skulltaker, as a playable Legendary Lord alongside a deadly host of new battle units, gameplay features and objectives to be experienced in the immense Immortal Empires campaign or Custom Battles. Skulltaker relentlessly seeks worthy skulls for his master. Constantly scanning the battlefield for the strongest opponents, he issues a thunderous challenge before swiftly ending their lives. Embodying Khorne’s unyielding fury, he takes immense pride in his martial abilities and views himself as an unstoppable force. Campaign Feature: ● Cloak of Skulls: As the champion of Khorne, Skulltaker must seek-out and defeat powerful enemy lords across the heroic pantheon of the Warhammer World and add their skulls to his infamous cloak. With each new skull he earns ‘Champions Essence’ allowing him to unlock new bonuses or upgrade existing ones, granting powerful new abilities and traits that increase his proficiency in combat. Battle Playstyle: ● Few daemons ascend to the exalted position of Khorne’s Champion and those who do are assured to be lethal in almost every regard. This is true for Skulltaker, a force of daemonic destruction, he excels at hunting down and duelling enemy lords before cleaving through ranks of troops with his two-handed Slayer Sword. His Cloak of Skulls passive ability grants increasing spell resistance and ward saves the more he kills. New Units: ● Nine new units from the Blood God’s domain enter the fray, including two Legendary Heroes; Skarr Bloodwrath ‘the bloodthirsty butcher’ and Scyla Anfingrimm ‘the rage of Khorne’, followed by a host of acolytes including the Bloodspeaker, Slaughterbrute, Bloodbeast, Wrathmongers, Skullreapers, and Khorngors. Content Overview: ● 1 Legendary Lord ● 2 Legendary Heroes ● 1 Lord ● 5 Battle Units ● 3 Regiments of Renown ● New Gameplay Mechanics and Features ● Playable in Immortal Empires and Custom Battles Only
short_description: The Skulltaker DLC pack for Total War: WARHAMMER III introduces Khorne’s Immortal Champion and the greatest of all Bloodletters, Skulltaker, as a playable Legendary Lord.

reviews.review_text:
Although I have mixed feelings about this DLC, I settled on a thumb up because it suits the Khorne gameplay. Veteran players looking for a challenge, this campaign is not for you.

Skulltaker's mechanic allows him to gain insane buffs by defeating enemy Lords. While it's a simple design, it works for a race like Khorne whose theme revolves around mindless carnage and thus makes Skulltaker one of the most powerful Legendary Lords in the game.

The ability to teleport to enemy Lords adds another layer of insanity to the power trip that is his campaign. Fun for one time, but not something that I see myself replaying a whole lot (which is not really a problem in itself).

The unit roster is a mixed bag, some units work other don't. The Khorngors and the Wrathmongers are fantastic new entries to Khorne, but the Skullreapers are disappointing and the Slaughterbrute is yet another center-piece monster with buggy animations that prevent it from reliably landing hits.

The DLC is okay overall, far from the quality of content that is provided by something like Thrones of Decay however.

name: Total War: WARHAMMER III - Golgfag – Omens of Destruction
description: The Golgfag Maneater DLC pack for Total War: WARHAMMER III introduces one of the most successful Ogre Mercenaries, Golgfag Maneater, as a playable Legendary Lord alongside a deadly host of new battle units, gameplay features and objectives to be experienced in the immense Immortal Empires campaign or Custom Battles. A weathered veteran, there are few foes in the world Golgfag hasn’t faced, having fought across continents and cultures the world over. He’ll take any job, kill anyone, and steal anything as long as the price is right, and his loyalty is strictly for hire, lasting only as long as the deal does. Boisterous and larger-than-life, Golgfag is also a top guzzler of Bugman’s ale. Campaign Feature: ● Mercenary Contracts: As an internationally renowned mercenary, Golgfag helps client factions throughout the WARHAMMER world settle their warring disputes by taking on all their wars. When a contract is signed, he must earn points by making acts of aggression against the enemy within a specified turn-limit and after reaching his target the contract is complete, granting a payout and rewards such as Meat, Maneater Banners and culture unique Ancillaries. Playstyle: ● A fearsome reputation such as Golgfag’s isn’t forged through the showing of mercy, and on the battlefield, he ensures that any fools who stand in his way regret it. A proficient anti-infantry killer, he cuts through foes like a sharp knife through stale Old-World butter, but can also call upon some deadly abilities such as Dead Eye in in which he fires a powerful single shot that deals significant ranged and armour piercing damage. New Units: ● New units joining Golgfag’s mercenary brigade include the Legendary Hero Bragg the Gutsman, the self-proclaimed executioner of the Ogre Kingdoms, as well as the Ogre Paymaster, Bruiser, Golgfag’s Maneaters, Pigback Riders, Blood Vultures, Yhetees and Thundertusk. Content Overview: ● 1 Legendary Lord ● 1 Legendary Hero ● 1 Lord and 1 Hero ● 5 Battle Units ● 3 Regiments of Renown ● New Gameplay Mechanics and Features ● Playable in Immortal Empires and Custom Battles Only
short_description: The Golgfag Maneater DLC pack for Total War: WARHAMMER III introduces veteran mercenary Golgfag Maneater as a playable Legendary Lord alongside a deadly host of new battle units, gameplay features and objectives to be experienced in the immense Immortal Empires campaign or Custom Battles.

Hundreds of hours played, but for hundreds of dollars spent. All for a game still in a broken state, running on an aged engine.

Buying it at discount is a trap for you to spend more on the endless stream of DLC lords and factions that are deliberately far more interesting than the base game's offerings.

The game remains a lukewarm stew of bugs within bugs. The developers are well aware of this, but are too focused on pushing out their next power-creep slop DLC to bother fixing it. 

I wish this wasn't the case, but it is.. and so shall it remain.

name: Total War: WARHAMMER III - Gorbad – Omens of Destruction
description: The Gorbad Ironclaw DLC pack for Total War: WARHAMMER III introduces one of the most infamous of all Orc Warbosses, Gorbad Ironclaw, as a playable Legendary Lord alongside a deadly host of new battle units, gameplay features and objectives to be experienced in the immense Immortal Empires campaign or Custom Battles. One of the most infamous of all Orc Warbosses, Gorbad knows but one language and that is war. Unique amongst his race due to his rare mix of brain and brawn, he has become an inspiration amongst his kind for "invasions done propa". Campaign Feature: ● Da’ Plan: A tenacious tactician, Gorbad must experiment with unique battle strategies such as matching different units in his army roster to unlock powerful synergistic bonuses such as abilities, attributes, and campaign perks. Each tactic can progress in level by completing challenges, encouraging creative army compositions and rewarding strategic thinking. Battle Playstyle: ● Riding into battle atop his fierce Warboar, Gnarla, Gorbad penetrates the enemy lines with devastating charges before engaging in close combat with his mighty axe, Morglor ‘the Mangler’. His ‘Da Great Leader’ ability which activates when troops are in his vicinity, granting increased melee attack and defence. New Units: ● New units joining Gorbad’s Waaagh! include the legendary hero Snagla Grobspit accompanied by a motley crew of warriors such as the Savage Orc Great Shaman, Night Goblin Big Boss, Black Orcs (Axe & Shield), mangler squigs, Colossal Squig, Arachnarok (Flinger), and Bolt Throwa. Content Overview: ● 1 Legendary Lord ● 1 Legendary Hero ● 1 Lord and 1 Hero ● 5 Battle Units ● 3 Regiments of Renown ● New Gameplay Mechanics and Features ● Playable in Immortal Empires and Custom Battles Only
short_description: The Gorbad Ironclaw DLC pack for Total War: WARHAMMER III introduces infamous Orc Warboss Gorbad Ironclaw as a playable Legendary Lord alongside a deadly host of new battle units, gameplay features and objectives to be experienced in the immense Immortal Empires campaign or Custom Battles.

[h1][b]I don’t want balance.
I want Creative Assembly charging interest on my Waaagh.[/b][/h1]

Total War: WARHAMMER III – Gorbad: Omens of Destruction is not just an Orc DLC.
It is Creative Assembly finally revealing their true form.

They are not developers.
They are Goblin merchants in human skin, and Gorbad is the distraction while they empty your pockets.

[spoiler][b]THIS AIN’T CONTENT.
THIS IS A LOAN AGREEMENT WITH AXES.[/b][/spoiler]

Gorbad the Unstoppable does what Orcs do best:
charge forward, smash everything, never stop.

Creative Assembly does what Goblins do best:
stand behind the Orc, rubbing their hands together, calculating interest.

[h3]GORBAD SMASHES.
CA COLLECTS.[/h3]

Mechanically, Gorbad is built for aggression.
Constant fighting.
Constant pressure.
No downtime.

This is perfect.

Because while you’re too busy charging the next army, Creative Assembly is setting up the next DLC pitch.

You don’t get a breather.
You get momentum.

And momentum is how Goblins sell you things.

Every successful battle makes you want:
• more units
• more upgrades
• more Lords
• more DLC

That’s not accidental.
That’s merchant design.

[h3]CREATIVE ASSEMBLY IS THE GOBLIN FACTION[/h3]

Let’s stop pretending.

They didn’t make Gorbad because Orcs needed depth.
They made Gorbad because Orc players don’t hesitate.

You see a bigger Orc.
You buy the bigger Orc.

Simple.
Efficient.
Profitable.

Creative Assembly understands Orc psychology better than anyone:

You don’t read fine print

You don’t want restraint

You want more, now

So they give you Gorbad — loud, direct, uncomplicated — while quietly attaching a recurring payment mindset.

This DLC doesn’t trick you.
It leans in.

[h3]THIS DLC CHARGES INTEREST IN FUN[/h3]

You buy Gorbad.
You feel satisfied.

Briefly.

Then you realize:
“This would feel even better with more stuff.”

That’s the interest kicking in.

Not punitive.
Not hostile.

Just enough to keep you spending.

Creative Assembly doesn’t drain you all at once.
They space it out.

Like Goblins.

Gorbad’s campaign reinforces this perfectly:
keep moving, keep fighting, keep expanding.

Stopping feels wrong.
Thinking feels slow.

Buying feels natural.

[h3]THIS IS HONEST GREED[/h3]

When someone asks “[i]is Gorbad worth it?[/i]”
the honest answer isn’t about stats.

It’s this:

[b][i]If you like Orcs, this DLC is Creative Assembly meeting you at your level — loud, greedy, and not pretending otherwise.[/i][/b]

This pack doesn’t hide the business model.
It embodies it.

Gorbad is the Orc that makes you shout “MORE.”
Creative Assembly is the Goblin that replies “OF COURSE.”

And somehow, it works.

Because you don’t feel cheated.
You feel understood.

You wanted an Orc who never stops.
They wanted a DLC that never stops selling.

GORBAD: OMENS OF DESTRUCTION.

[hr]

[h3][b]CREATIVE ASSEMBLY GOBLIN DEV DIARY #27[/b][/h3]
[b]“WHY DLC COSTZ (ALOTA) MONEY”[/b]

Oi.

Bigger.
Louder.

[h3]STEP 2: WE ADD VALUE (DAT YOU DON’T READ)[/h3]

[/list]

[h3]STEP 3: WE SET PRICE[/h3]

Goblin ask:

“Can Orc afford?”

Answer:
“Orc already buyin’.”

So price is correct.

If Orc buy fast, price good.
If Orc buy slow, next DLC bigger.

Dis is called market feedback.

[h3]STEP 4: INTEREST (VERY IMPORTANT)[/h3]

We don’t take all money at once.

Dat make Orc sad.

Instead:
[list]

[*] You feel strong

[/list]

Dat last part is interest.

Interest is when fun makes you want next DLC.

Goblin love interest.

[h3]WHY WE NO MAKE FREE DLC[/h3]

Free DLC bad.

Free DLC teach Orc patience.

Patience bad for Orc.

So DLC cost money to:

[*] Keep Orc buyin’
[/list]

Balance not goal.
Completion not goal.

[b]Momentum is goal.[/b]

[h3]FINAL WORDS FROM GOBLIN[/h3]

You like Gorbad because Gorbad never stop.

We like Gorbad because Orc never stop buyin’.

Dis is harmony.

(Goblin Dev Division)
Eh. ended up playing Skarsnik and confederating Gorbad instead, hero is great. SOOOPA SQUIG!

This DLC is what ruined the greenskins for me, they added 2 unit variants of already existing units, all the while releasing unit variants for free in updates for other races. 2 artillery pieces for a melee faction that already has great artillery pieces if you want to go that way. i mean the gawd damn spear launcher is just inferior in everyway, it does nearly no damage and cant hit the broad side of a barn. 1 arrow boyz unit can out dps that ♥♥♥♥ in less than the time it takes for the target to close in on the arrow boyz. and dont get me started on the two unit variants we pay for in this DLC... black orcs with shields does comes with a shield and an ability that you will never use because it makes the unit in to a wet noodle you can just ignore, i dont even think the AI uses the ability- the same goes for the ability they gave to the "updated" ordinary black orcs. They dont attack nearly fast enough for the ability to not result in them getting killed before being able to do damage... and there is absolutely no reason to pick the damn spider with the catapult in the first place, it has a 20 second reload time and only 300 in range. the damn enemy can get into melee before it gets to shoot twice and the damn spider it self is not even slow so there is no reason to wait for the enemy... they should have just given the ranged ability to the generic damn rogue idol... it atleast needs it because the damn battle is over before it even gets into melee with how slow it moves... dont buy this DLC it wont add anything to greenskins that you did not already have and what it adds is an insult to the faction itself..

A completely unnecessary DLC for a race that was in an already great spot prior to its release. Gorbad adds nothing interesting to the Greenskins, neither does his campaign or the new units.

Gorbad's campaign mechanic revolves around completing very specific conditions to unlock sets of bonuses, called Tactics, that boost your armies. There are 3 different tiers, each unlockable by completing a number of Tactics. While it may sounds interesting at first, this campaign mechanic is wholly unpractical in the context of a Total War game and forces you to play a certain way to unlock rewards which limits player agency.

Building an army full of Nasty Skulkers to unlock a 3-turn bonus that, in reality, only requires you to have 2 Nasty Skulkers in said army to use it is nonsensical game design. Not to mention that Nasty Skulkers are a low-tier unit that you end up disbanding in the mid-late game, at which point this Tactic becomes entirely useless. This among other Tactics that expect you to shuffle your armies around every 3 turns to fit very specific conditions for mediocre bonuses just doesn't work in terms of campaign pacing.

The roster additions are uninspired. The Colossal Squig takes the spotlight of this DLC, leaving everything else in the dirt. Selling unit variants as DLC is insulting to the player, and is part of the reason why I wouldn't recommend anyone buying this.

Try [url=https://store.steampowered.com/app/1209120/Total_War_WARHAMMER_II__The_Warden__The_Paunch]The Warden & The Paunch[/url] instead, it's of vastly superior quality.
The price for  a single lord DLC is a rip off now. They simple aren't worth the price tag. Most of these lords should be in the base game anyway. Its lazy and scummy

Easily wiped by dwarves early on even on normal difficulty. Lost a long campaign because the WAAHG glitched and despite being ready to launch just wouldn't activate despite everything I tried. If you can't waaahg, what is the point of playing the Orcs...

[h1]Beware: no story whatsoever[/h1]
If you like the idea of the Ogre Kingdoms, go ahead and buy the Ogre Kingdoms DLC instead. Why, you ask, if you get all the core roster from Ogre Kingdoms PLUS the units from this DLC if you only buy this? Because the ogres are thematically mercenaries, and Gorbad is "the" mercenary of the faction, meaning he has no ties whatsoever to anything. Meaning you start a campaign, play 10 turns, go "big chungus man goes pow-pow haha" and then it hits you: what the hell am I supposed to do? And if you have no answer to that, this campaign is dead right there, forever. If you are that type of player, this DLC is a complete waste of money. The same applies to the entire Omens of Destruction DLC trilogy by the way, CA just thought "these guys kill stuff" (oh gee, really? in Warhammer?) and just cut the costs and didn't make any story for them.

With Greaseus the Shockingly Obese, at least you have some narrative: rule the Mountains of Mourn, be on good terms with Cathay. At least the theme gives him a vague direction what should he do. This alone will get you plenty enemies and carry your campaign for a 100 turns. 

With Skrag the Slaughterer, it's admittedly a bit harder because he is all about "go kill, get meat, offer it to the great maw", but you know, he comes in the same package as Greaseus and that makes both cheaper, and if you like Greaseus you might want to give Skrag a try for free.

Units, Lords, Heroes.
Honestly, there is not much to talk about. You get Yetis, but... you already had Gorgers, filling the same animalistic primitive ogre-like role in your roster. You get some ice mammoths who can magically conjure a really weird giant snowball and shoot it, but you already had stonehorns for big melee monsters that function as a mount for it's rider to shoot. And you get a goblin, riding another goblin in a big jacket, with a spear, because it's a thing on the tabletop but in the game, post-rework of the Ogres, they so filthy rich you won't ever recruit any goblin units. Honestly, I don't even remember the other 2. The Paymaster is a fun thematic lord if you prefer one that stays behind and buffs allies, but honestly it does nothing your legendary lords can't already do, and you are better off with a melee tank and a mage hero combo no matter which race you play.

[h1]Summary[/h1]
I would not buy this if I were you. I only own it because I bought Omens of Destruction as a package. The new units feel mostly duplicates for roles already filled but thematically less working for the ogres, the new legendary lord / faction is easily the most aimless in the entire game with nothing to do.

name: Total War: WARHAMMER III - Mother Ostankya – Shadows of Change
description: This pack introduces Mother Ostankya, a new Legendary Lord for Kislev, usable in both Realm of Chaos and Immortal Empires campaigns, as well as new units, characters, and regiments of renown. • Conjure powerful hexes and incantations whilst engaging in witchcraft, flipping campaigns on their head as Mother Ostankya of Kislev. • Fight for Kislev with The Golden Knight, the finest warrior from among the ranks of the Druzhina. ​​• ​Expand your roster with 5 additional units, and enhance your troops with 3 new Regiments of Renown, including the Things in the Woods, Hag Witches, and the giant Frost Wyrm. For years we thought she was a child's tale to frighten us into behaving. Then one day, in my twentieth winter, I got too close to the woods. And when I was told Mother Ostankya would get you... I knew if I took one step closer to the trees it would be true. Tales of monsters and bogeymen have haunted many lands for centuries, but the tale hanging above the Kislevite children, a reminder of the consequences for displaying disobedience, was not just a tale. Mother Ostankya is real. A guardian, faithful to the land, holding anyone who seeks to harm it accountable, she draws her power from the very woods, fens and oblasts that she protects. When war comes to her door, she’ll be on the front lines, protecting the warriors of Kislev, and inflicting her terror upon any who oppose them. And if you cross her path, make no mistake. Mother Ostankya will punish you. As a powerful sorceress with a bespoke melee component, Mother Ostankya utilises her cauldron on and off the battlefield, able to rain spirits down upon her foes from afar or sprout the legs of a spider in melee combat as an artillery-chariot hybrid. The Daughters of the Forest must infuse Mother Ostankya’s Witchcraft at the Witch’s Hut. With a new rare resource, Spirit Essence, you can take advantage of ingredients gathered during your playthrough to create potent Incantations and utilise Hexes to vastly change the course of your entire campaign. Force enemy armies from your lands in an instant, purge entire provinces of corruption in a single move, buff your units far beyond their given capability, and unleash the full might of the witch as you search for the Malediction of Ruin; Mother Ostankya’s final Hex to secure the sanctity of Kislevite land. New Kislevite units lurk in these woods. A terrible and frightening creature, known to many as The Thing in the Woods, brings its enormous size and infantry sundering capability to the frontlines of Kislev. Left unchecked, this beast will tear through enemy troops like paper, before rushing into the woods for cover and emerging to do it all over again. CONTENT OVERVIEW • 1 Legendary Lord • 1 Legendary Hero • 1 Lord • 1 Hero • 5 Battle Units • 3 Regiments of Renown • New gameplay mechanics and features • Playable in Immortal Empires and Realms of Chaos
short_description: This pack introduces Mother Ostankya, a new Legendary Lord for Kislev, usable in both Realm of Chaos and Immortal Empires campaigns, as well as new units, characters, and regiments of renown.

name: Total War: WARHAMMER III - Yuan Bo – Shadows of Change
description: This pack introduces Yuan Bo, a new Legendary Lord for Grand Cathay, usable in both Realm of Chaos and Immortal Empires campaigns, as well as new units, characters, and regiments of renown. • Establish and execute grand stratagems on a global scale as Yuan Bo of Grand Cathay. • Defending Cathay is the Legendary Hero Saytang the Watcher, the great sentinel from the Mountains of Heaven. ​​• ​Expand your roster with 6 additional units, and enhance your troops with 3 new Regiments of Renown, including Onyx Crowmen, Celestial Lions, and the Great Moon Bird. Those in other nations may deny it, but all mortals desire control. To be controlled, that is. Ruling over the Central Provinces of Cathay, Yuan Bo, the Jade Dragon leads the Court of Celestial Sorcerers. A bureaucrat of the highest order on the surface, yes, but a talented spymaster at heart, the Jade Dragon runs the city of Wei-jin and, by extension, is an administrator for the Cathay empire. Liked by his siblings, the good of Grand Cathay is in capable hands. Serving two roles between his human and dragon forms, the Jade Dragon can be a devastating single target destroyer, and a potent spellcaster with the Light, Yin, and Heaven lores of magic. Soar above the battlefield as a dragon, burning down even the strongest of enemy units, or take the fight on the ground with game-changing spells at your side. Cathay stands, all the same. Engage in a chess match unlike any other with the Matters of State. As a master statesman, a right hand to the emperor and a spymaster, Yuan Bo’s influence over the kingdom runs deep. With this power, enact overt and covert policies across settlements, armies and even characters to best suit your empire’s needs. Crack down on settlement corruption, rush construction for your entire empire in a single move, create diplomatic deadlocks to move freely through unfriendly territories, and much, much more. Go beyond mere balance and embrace disharmony, leveraging it as an excuse to serve more severe and extreme actions to fit your agenda, and plan chains of actions to ensure the balance of harmony is pulled back in your favour when all is said and done. Yuan Bo doesn’t follow the rules. He writes them. With a plan centuries in the making under the request of Yuan Bo’s father, the Jade Dragon is tasked with travelling to critical locations and constructing relays to empower the Jade Compass, before defending them from the Lizardmen incursions trying to dismantle Cathay. Destroy them, and plot a course to victory. It won’t take a compass to find the new additions to the Cathayan roster. Crafted by the wizards of the Celestial Court, Guardian Lions are living statues made from Jet or Jade. For those seeking a magical touch, the Jade Lion is a conduit of sorcery, ​​able to use spells bound to its body and launch flaming attacks. The Jet Lion, however, is the opposite, countering magical spells to diminish any sorcery that may harm themselves or those nearby. CONTENT OVERVIEW • 1 Legendary Lord • 1 Legendary Hero • 1 Lord • 1 Hero • 6 Battle Units • 3 Regiments of Renown • New gameplay mechanics and features • Playable in Immortal Empires and Realms of Chaos
short_description: This pack introduces Yuan Bo, a new Legendary Lord for Grand Cathay, usable in both Realm of Chaos and Immortal Empires campaigns, as well as new units, characters, and regiments of renown.

reviews.review_text:
A good change of pace from the two base game Legendary Lords with a dual start position that provides more challenge in the early game if you decide to expand your empire in two locations at once.

Matters of State is an interesting mechanic that plays on the idea of Yin and Yang with two unique resources that deplete each other out depending on which one you decide to use. Steel focuses on reinforcing your armies while Stone boosts your economy.

Yuan Bo gets access to 4 new options for the Wu Xing Compass, unlocked by completing his campaign objectives. A design choice that I wish was applied to more Legendary Lords in the game, as to limit the power gap between base game factions and DLC factions, and to provide a reward for completing campaign objectives.

The unit additions are all great and they synergize well with the Cathay unit roster. The Great Moon Bird is potentially the weakest addition (not by a large margin), being less useful than a Celestial Lion. Onyx Crowmen provide an excellent anti-backline option for most Cathay army compositions in the early-mid game. The Celestial General is also an excellent melee Lord option.

My only gripe with this DLC is Yuan Bo himself. His dragon transformation is worse than Miao Ying and Zhao Ming who are already weak themselves. His attack animations are overly complicated and flashy, making him unable to land a hit in a lot of situations. Cathay Dragons are worshipped as demi-gods in Warhammer, and sadly, the game doesn't do a good job at representing that.

Yuan Bo's execute ability, which allows him to finish off enemy characters under a certain HP threshold, is wholly unnecessary considering he's an already incredible fighter and spellcaster.

Too easy and a map painter campaign, but well its relaxed and fun. If you enjoy challenges, then this might not be for you. Due to multiple things, which makes the campaign part quite easy and eco booms super easily and quickly.
fun DLC. play with SFO.

[h2]4/10[/h2]

Yuan Bo has one of the most interesting characters in the Shadows of Change pack... and somehow CA still managed to make playing him feel like a chore.

This is not a bad DLC in the sense that it breaks anything, and the lore surrounding Yuan Bo is genuinely compelling. The problem is that the mechanic attached to him in the Realm of Chaos campaign is a mess of ideas that never cohered into something fun.

You're sent globetrotting across the entire map just to activate mine nodes and crack open Chaos Realm gateways. On paper: flavourful. In practice: the Chaos Realm outcomes feel utterly random, the payoff is unclear, and after all that effort you're left wondering why you didn't just pick Miao Ying for a cleaner experience.

The saving grace? [b]Immortal Empires.[/b] Dump Yuan Bo in there and he becomes a genuinely refreshing alternative to the standard Cathay block-and-push formula.

[hr][/hr]

Bottom line: if you care about roleplaying the LL and want to feel the lore, Realm of Chaos has the flavour, even if the gameplay jank will test your patience. If you want to actually enjoy the mechanics, go Immortal Empires. Either way, this DLC does absolutely nothing that makes Cathay more [i]necessary[/i] to own.

[hr][/hr]

[b]Verdict:[/b] Skip unless you're a diehard Cathay fan. If you do buy, load Immortal Empires and never look back.

name: Total War: WARHAMMER III - The Changeling – Shadows of Change
description: This pack introduces the Changeling, a new Legendary Lord for Tzeentch, usable in both Realm of Chaos and Immortal Empires campaigns, as well as new units, characters, and regiments of renown. • Enact malign schemes and trickery with the Changeling of Tzeentch. • Scatter magical spells far and wide with the Blue Scribes of Tzeentch; a new Legendary Hero. ​​• ​Expand your roster with 5 additional nits, and enhance your troops with 3 new Regiments of Renown, including the Chaos Lord of Tzeentch, Tzaangors, Changebringers, and the Mutalith Vortex Beast. I make my home in the darkest pits of your soul. In the shadows, I bide my time. As the shape-shifting agent of misrule, the Changeling embodies the Changer of Ways’ most deceiving and manipulative tendencies. Able to take the form of any legendary character from the noble to the fearsome, none, save perhaps Tzeentch himself, know the Changeling's true form. His trickery may even deceive the Dark Gods themselves, able to mirror foes, create false allies, and disrupt empires from within, with only one truth becoming clear long after he’s enacted his handiwork. The perplexing prankster of the known world, the Changeling of Tzeentch, was here. As a spellcasting shapeshifter bolstering the Lore of Tzeentch, the Changeling can adopt the form of any lord he faces, making his battle efficiency grow stronger as your enemy tries to do the same. When not fighting directly, he lingers in the shadows, stalking his prey, and waiting for the optimal time to strike. Trust no one. The Changeling’s inscrutable and deceiving nature is the perfect recipe for schemes and trickery. For the Changeling, the world is divided into Theatres; segmented regions that contain Schemes, both minor and pivotal. Fuelled by an unending legion of tricksy cultists, to achieve his goals he must first break a Theatre down through smaller, cheekier Schemes by constructing buildings, stealing identities, and instigating tensions amongst otherwise civilized populations, culminating in a Grand Scheme and an epic battle for supremacy, rife with bountiful rewards along the way. Gold, Grimoires, and Rifts aplenty. With enough Grand Schemes carried out, the Changeling’s final purpose is within reach: conducting the Ultimate Scheme for campaign victory. This troublesome Legendary Hero, the Blue Scribes, ride atop their Disc of Tzeentch, scribbling on parchment as they go, and are capable of spewing all manner of spells across the battlefield by verbalising their scrolls. If only they knew what those words meant... CONTENT OVERVIEW • 1 Legendary Lord • 1 Legendary Hero • 1 Lord • 1 Hero • 5 Battle Units • 3 Regiments of Renown • New gameplay mechanics and features • Playable in Immortal Empires and Realms of Chaos
short_description: This pack introduces the Changeling, a new Legendary Lord for Tzeentch, usable in both Realm of Chaos and Immortal Empires campaigns, as well as new units, characters, and regiments of renown.

reviews.review_text: Very fun and unique DLC. If you plan well, other factions might never even know where you are the whole campaign.

But if you enjoy messing around and just annoying everyone around the whole world in a multiplayer campaign, then get this, otherwise read-up on the changeling mechanics before buying.

[h1][b]I don’t want transparency. I want The Changeling.[/b][/h1]

This DLC does not have a form.
It changes when you look at it.
It changes when you complain.
It changes when you buy it.

Total War: WARHAMMER III – The Changeling is not a Legendary Lord.
It is a corporate mirror.

[spoiler][b]This is not content.
This is behavior.[/b][/spoiler]

The Changeling is deception as a mechanic.
Misdirection as design.
Promises that move when approached.

He is perfect.

Because he is honest in the only way that matters.

[h3]Creative Assembly is the Changeling[/h3]

The Changeling doesn’t fix problems.
He reframes them.

A bug isn’t a bug.
It’s emergent behavior.

A broken mechanic isn’t broken.
It’s being “iterated on.”

A missing fix isn’t missing.
It’s coming later.

Paid.

This DLC understands something fundamental:

[b]You don’t stabilize a live service.
You keep it moving.[/b]

Fixing things would mean stopping.
Stopping would mean certainty.

Certainty kills monetization.

[h3]The lies aren’t the problem
they’re the feature[/h3]

The Changeling teaches you how to play Warhammer III correctly.

You stop expecting fixes.
You stop reading patch notes for solutions.
You read them for vibes.

Will this break something else?
Yes.

Will it matter?
No.

Because there’s another DLC coming.

And you’re going to buy it.

You already know that.

[h3]This DLC doesn’t respect you
and that’s why it works[/h3]

The Changeling doesn’t pretend to care about balance.
He doesn’t promise stability.

He performs sincerity while rearranging the furniture.

That’s not incompetence.
That’s branding.

Every new DLC arrives wearing a new face.
Every apology has a different silhouette.
Every roadmap is flexible enough to escape accountability.

And you nod.

Because the alternative would be not playing.

[h3]Bugs are proof of life[/h3]

A stable game is a dead game.

A game that never breaks has stopped selling.

The Changeling spreads bugs the way Creative Assembly spreads features:
[list]
[*]inconsistently
[*]unapologetically
[*]profitably
[*]Each bug confirms momentum.
[/list]
Each unresolved issue confirms the next DLC is more important.

And it is.

[h3]You know they don’t care
and you buy it anyway[/h3]

This is the most honest part.

There is no illusion left.

You know fixes won’t come.
You know promises will shift.
You know the Changeling will change again.

And still, you click “Add to Cart.”

Not because you believe.

Because you’ve accepted the cycle.

When someone asks “[i]is The Changeling worth it?[/i]”
you don’t defend it.

You say:

[b][i]This DLC explains the entire business model.[/i][/b]

And that’s enough.

This pack doesn’t add a lord.
It adds context.

It doesn’t deepen gameplay.
It clarifies expectations.

It doesn’t lie to you.

It shows you how lying works
and charges you for the demonstration.

The Changeling isn’t broken.
He’s accurate.

He’s not unbalanced.
He’s flexible.

He doesn’t fix the game.
He ensures the game never has to stop changing.

And neither does the company.

You don’t hate this.

You’ve moved past hate.

You understand now.

This is not betrayal.
This is consistency.

This is Warhammer III at its most honest:

[b]unfinished, unstable, expensive, and still irresistible.[/b]

The Changeling smiles.

You already bought it.

You’ll buy the next one too.

Because you don’t want truth.

You want motion.

And motion costs money.

name: Total War: WARHAMMER III - Dechala – Tides of Torment
description: The Dechala the Denied One DLC pack introduces the Slaaneshi champion as a playable Legendary Lord for Immortal Empires and Custom Battles. Starting in the Hidden Landing of the Cathayan Hinterlands, Dechala leads The Tormentors with a cruelly decadent and aggressively mobile playstyle. For centuries, Dechala has waged an unending war of pain and pleasure. More Daemonic serpent than human, her sinuous body rises to a six-armed torso, each hand wielding a sword in a blur of killing strokes. Combining beauty and horror, she embodies Slaanesh’s essence, inspiring her followers to enforce her will with zeal and cruelty. Unique mechanic: Pillars of Cruelty Captured settlements convert into: • Thrall Camp: generates Thralls and resources, though productivity wanes as they deplete. • Tormentor’s Hold: enables recruitment, infrastructure, and defence. • Pleasure Palace: Major Settlement option, requiring Thralls but granting advanced recruitment and economy buildings. Unique mechanic: Decadence Dechala’s actions raise Decadence, expanding Pleasure Palaces and unlocking powers. Benefits: Slaanesh boons (temporary buffs), Corruption of enemy units before battle, and Daemonic Attraction for instantly recruitable Daemons. She may also sacrifice Thralls for In Praise of Slaanesh, an area-damage ability summoning an enhanced Spawn of Slaanesh. Unique mechanic: Tormentor’s Tributes Through Decadence, Dechala selects powerful rewards: • Marks of Vindictiveness: temporary battle buffs • Opulent Gifts: unique ancillaries • Exquisite Spoils: rituals with campaign benefits New units In addition to the standard Slaaneshi roster, Dechala commands: • Legendary Lord: Dechala • Lord: Chaos Sorcerer Lord of Slaanesh • Legendary Hero: Strykaar of the Sortsvinaer • Heroes: Druchii Anointed, Exalted Hero of Slaanesh • Melee Cavalry: Pleasureseekers • Melee Infantry: Devotees of Slaanesh • Ranged Infantry: Devotees of Slaanesh (Crossbows) • Monstrous Infantry: Champions of Slaanesh, Slaangors • Monster: Preyton • 3x Regiments of Renown
short_description: The Dechala the Denied One DLC pack introduces the deadly Chaos Champion Dechala as a playable Legendary Lord. Leading The Tormentors, she wages campaigns of brutal exploitation worldwide.

reviews.review_text:
There are still significant bugs in this campaign (boons of slaanesh not working), it's far too easy to steamroll and get an obscene number of pleasure palaces if you play in a specific way and the quest battle is terrible since the total war game isn't built for fighting armies and not killing them, plus it doesn't communicate it well. The units are good though and it is pretty fun. 

If it's on discount it's worth it.
It’s a slanesh faction and having two stance changing characters in the update is a bit questionable but I actually like slanesh so I kind of like this dlc.

I enjoyed this campaign. I liked the unique building system and I liked the new units. Normally I strongly dislike this part of the map but it didint bother me as much for some reason on this playtrough.
Bought this DLC on sale, and I would suggest you do the same.
snek
Bad faction. Boring roster additions. This DLC adds very little engaging content to the campaign.

Do not buy any DLC for this game. It is beyond unoptimized. Drop of FPS, slow campaign, eternal end turns... Wait until they decided to actually improve optimization... or at least make smaller campaign maps...

Slaanesh corruption Massively decreases FPS
Slaanesh corruption drops fps from 100+ to 40 and tanks gpu performance massively.
Whenever i hover over some slannesh province i get massive fps drops and my GPU temperatures spikes up to 90C
The map with Slaneesh  corruption kills fps. Cant recommend till they fix it.

This comes with 2 useful units; snake riders and some low-tier range. The rest is trash you will NEVER use because you already have much better options. If you bought the WoC dlc that is. Remember this is CA; they love money and hate you.
Dechala's campaign buffs these new garbage units and the old ones too in a very underwhelming way, and comes with a convoluted economy with several new types of currency, some THAT HAVE THE SAME NAME AS EACH OTHER AND PAY FOR DIFFRENT THINGS, INCLUDING ONE ANOTHER. That's some next level bad design.  
Not worth buying even if it's on sale.

name: Total War: WARHAMMER III - Aislinn – Tides of Torment
description: The Sea Lord Aislinn DLC pack introduces the High Elf naval tactician Aislinn as a playable Legendary Lord for Immortal Empires and Custom Battles. Commander of the Sea Patrol, Aislinn is Ulthuan’s spearhead against Dark Elves, Norscans, and all who threaten the Asur. Though many nobles find his ruthless methods distasteful, none deny his unmatched naval expertise. His mastery of the legendary Dragonships and the forces they command is Ulthuan’s best hope of survival. Unique mechanic: Dragonship Fleets The Sea Patrol does not develop settlements traditionally. Instead, they command Dragonships—vast, self-sufficient vessels with infrastructure, economy, and military buildings. They support nearby friendly fleets and launch major assaults. Aislinn begins with one Dragonship but can add more as his campaign expands. Unique mechanic: Asur Occupation Aislinn transfers most conquered settlements to other High Elf factions but retains limited building rights. He can develop Sea Patrol Outposts to strengthen naval forces, or establish Elven Colonies at key locations, both generating resources. Unique mechanic: Asur Domination Outposts grant knowledge that fuels upgrades for Outposts, Colonies, and Dragonships. Dedicating Outposts to major Elven factions unlocks aid from their leaders, including elite units and buffs. New units Aislinn’s roster emphasizes coastal strikes, alongside the standard High Elf lineup: • Legendary Lord: Sea Lord Aislinn • Lord: Sea Helm • Legendary Hero: Caradryan • Hero: Mist Mage • Melee Infantry: Ship’s Company • Monstrous Infantry: Sea Elemental, Oceanids • Monster: Merwyrm • Flying Chariot (Ranged): Lothern Skycutter (Bows), Lothern Skycutter (Bolt Thrower) • 3x new Regiments of Renown
short_description: The Sea Lord Aislinn DLC pack introduces master High Elf naval tactician Aislinn as a playable Legendary Lord. Commanding the Sea Patrol, he spearheads Ulthuan’s defence and expands its sea-borders.

reviews.review_text: Most unique HE faction. New units are cool and thematic.
liked it.
Had quite a bit of fun with Aislinn.
This lord is pretty fun, but you should buy at a sale
Interesting campaign that seems well thought out for the scale of Immortal Empires, and the new HE units are awesome.

High Elves are my favorite faction in WH2 and 3. This DLC is an uninspired cash grab that ruins established mechanics and brings nothing new of value. If CA can't make a fun and engaging DLC even for HE, this game is truly done.

Do not buy any DLC for this game. It is beyond unoptimized. Drop of FPS, slow campaign, eternal end turns... Wait until they decided to actually improve optimization... or at least make smaller campaign maps...

I had fun time playing this faction. It is very unique and juggling the various resources is fun. BUT:
Confederations, while being the reward for Ulthuan politics, are absolutelly detremental to your progress.
Unlike the other HE factions you control very few settlements (you have something akin to the herdstone shard mechanic from beastmen where you have a limited ammount of tokens you can buy real settlements with). The other settlements you gift to one of the other legendary HE factions in exchange for a Changeling-esque outpost in that city.

You have a resource that is generated by outposts which is then being consumed in your main settlements for profit. Overall fun concept that works very well.

But since you are not expected to hold many settlements, when you confederate, the faction you confederated is replaced by a completely passive faction and gifting you all their armies.
But since you don't get any cities and therefore no new buildings, you have to usually disband most of the gained armies.

I could see that working if at least you recieved an outpost in those confederated settlements, but for a baffling reason you don't.

The most agreagous a about it is that the tokens you need for more cities are not given back unless you upgrade the settlement to tier 3 AND its an ancient elf collony (a settlement with a special port type). Problem is that somewhat unsuprisingly elven factions often hold those settlements - Teclis starts in one and others are pretty close. So... when you confederate an elven faction... you are permanently locked from getting an elven collony making it almost optimal to attack the elven factions you are supposed to be uniting.

Also there is a bug that when there are no more elven factions left and there is nobody to gift your settlements to - you are given an option to gift the settlement to the passive subfaction. But that just does not work. And so you have only the option to sack or raze it (or build a settlement, but that is very limited).
Whats also rather frustrating is that there is a mechanic that allows you to make some dillemas that get better the more settlements you gifted to this or that legendary HE faction. But these
a) share cooldown between each HE faction
b) have a obnoxiously long cooldown
c) they share this cooldown even with one off rewards that you get for maxing off the bar.

So imagine this: each 10 turns you get an option to
a) get a mediocre buff
b) pay for a couple of items (meh items, cheap price)
c) pay "a lot" of one currency for a permanent buff
d) get a temp buff and a instant boost(reset movement, full heal etc.)
for one of !6! factions.
I don't understand why the cooldown is shared and why the items and permanent buffs are not in a separate menu. I mean you already have to spend a valuable resource on the permanent buffs AND have to wait 10 turns to do it AND have to give up all the other buffs to do it.

I still had a lot of fun with it and there are some cool ideas, but for some reason it looks like nobody really playtested it.

I will give it a negative one to point out these bugs, but I must say that I enjoyed the campain even in this state.

Gimmick, one time play only. Stoped playing after less than 50 turns on 2 attempts.

The resource managment feels more restrictive than rewarding. It fails to capture the balance and fun of the resource management of the Chorf DLC.

Most players, myself included, like to confederate the other LL's of their race.
To my surprise in this one, when you do, you get the LL, but all of the settlements go to an npc faction.
Another NPC to babysit? Really? With this still braindead AI?
I don't get any say in what I want to keep? What about landmarks, another player favorite?
Finally a hero for increased movement for the high elves!
Whats that? Another paywall, idiotique extra mage class?
Ugh...

Extremely disappointing. I have played the high elves for over  5 thousand hours across Warhammer 2 and 3, and this DLC and Update totally ruins them. The patrons of the realms is so poorly designed that it takes all seven possible campaign starts and forces them all together into one campaign that plays the exact same every single time. The high elves must control all of the cities of ulthuan, or else you cannot confederate the other legendary lords or interact with the system in general. So that means that instead of playing  the varied fun start positions that the different legendary lords have access to, they all have to hot foot it back to ulthuan and play there instead. Booorrriinnnggg. 

the new campaign for sealord aislinn is also terrible. The dragon ships mechanic works great and is amazing but they are locked to the worst generic lords of the faction which wastes their potential completely. the high elves have too many really good legendary lords for them not to be assignable court positions.  

other minor notes are that he is forced to go strait back to ulthuan to discover high elf factions in order to have full access to his campaign mechanics, even though the whole point of his campaign is to be everywhere else. he also needs to be able to vassalize other factions. the lothern sky cutters unfortunately underperform and the ai high elf factions can obtain the new legendary hero, locking you out of him until you can confederate later.

Over all I would pay the asking price of the DLC to Undo the DLC, and I will not be spending any more money on this game untill this travesty is addressed.

name: Total War: WARHAMMER III - Sayl – Tides of Torment
description: The Sayl the Faithless DLC pack introduces the treacherous sorcerer as a playable Legendary Lord for Immortal Empires and Custom Battles. Sayl leads the Dolgans from the new four-region province of Mountain’s End, rising to power through lies and betrayal. Sayl’s ascendancy began when he manipulated his tribe into a Daemonic ritual—only to sabotage it, fusing his peers into the Chaos Spawn Nightmaw, his monstrous and mindlessly loyal companion. Unlike his peers’ brute-force tactics, Sayl thrives on cunning and deception. Unique mechanic: Manipulations A master schemer, Sayl uses an array of Manipulations to weaken his enemies by disrupting the logistics of their armies, throwing their settlements in disarray and even directly whispering lies that warp and sever their diplomatic ties with friends and neighbours. Even his own forces are subject to the machinations of the Twisted Seer of the Waste in their march to victory and doom. Growing in power unlocks additional Manipulations, ever more potent and treacherous but relying on them is equally as treacherous. Too many Manipulations in a short time span will inevitably attract dark, malevolent attention that Sayl needs to navigate around or else he will suffer for his transgressions. Unique Mechanic: Dark Ritual Ever-thirsty to magnify his sorcerous strength, Sayl strives to enact a great ritual. To do this, he must construct Chaos Altars, allowing him to channel great arcane power. Through enacting rituals, he will face many battles, events and dilemmas in his quest for supreme power, including his greatest challenge, if he is to claim the ultimate prize. New units Sayl’s army blends sorcery, monstrosities, and betrayal-driven creations. His unique roster includes: • Legendary Lord: Sayl the Faithless • Lord: Great Shaman Sorcerer • Legendary Hero: Beorg Bearstruck • Hero: Fimir Noble • Melee Infantry: Marauder Bearmen (Axe & Shield, Great Weapons) • Shock Cavalry: Kurgan Horsemen (Dual Weapons, Great Weapons) • Monsters: Curs’d Ettin, Curs’d Ettin (Runecaller), Chimera, Dread Maw • 4x Regiments of Renown
short_description: The Sayl the Faithless DLC pack introduces the deceitful sorcerer Sayl as a playable Legendary Lord. Leading the Dolgan Tribe, Sayl is ruthless and self-serving when executing his manipulation of others.

reviews.review_text: Monke people finally getting some BIG love.
Good

Sayl is probably the best out of 3 (which honestly, isn't saying much, since one is the unlosable Lothern Sea Guard spam campaing, like every HElves ever and the other is marauder spam). 

So in this DLC, you'll get:
1. Shaman Lord. Which is pretty cool, with very volatile magics vibes. Very fitting. 
2. Dread Maw. One of the most unique units in game. Versatile artillery piece, that can be used actively to do damage in melee with it's movement abilities. Lot's of fun.
3. Reskined skinwolf Legendary Hero. Meh. 
4. Fimir Noble. Hero with pretty okay-ish skills.
5. Marauder Bearman. They serve as a really good mid point between your basic marauder units and elite infantry.
6. Kurgan Horseman. A pretty unique way to add cavalery to Norsca. Instead of having heavy cav, they have heavy hitting light cav. Pretty rare and fitting. 
7. Ettins and Cursed Ettins are both decent giant variants. The dual-personality passive is really interesting. The bound spells are useful. Another good unit. 
8. Chimera, which is just a manticore that spits fire. Still fun. 
9. Sayl- a boring Legendary Lord with very poorly implemented mechanics. Same old Norsca, but you don't get different colored chaos spawns from altars. Honestly, biggest disappointment of the DLC. 

Honestly, pretty good DLC. Not the best campaign, but you can feel the effort put into the units and the whole Norsca.
Like Tzeentch but danish themed. Pretty unique.  Not half bad.

If you love deep strategy, faction diversity, and narrative flavour, Tides of Torment is a worthwhile expansion that expands the sandbox of Total War: WARHAMMER III in satisfying ways—especially for players who enjoy pushing the limits of campaign and combat tactics.

Do not buy any DLC for this game. It is beyond unoptimized. Drop of FPS, slow campaign, eternal end turns... Wait until they decided to actually improve optimization... or at least make smaller campaign maps...

Its just not enjoyable to play a game where the ai gets all the benefits and you have to play by all the restrictions what a weird developer its like they just hate the people that play their game and it leaks into it, have not been ABLE to enjoy this DLC just dont understand CA now and this compared to 2 is night/day, there no individual AI its just all them vs you demons ignoring elves and dwarves to attack your chaos faction its just so ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ annoying and weird, why have you clearly set the ai to treat me wwaaay differently to the rest of the ai.

It's just a bit of rinse and repeat of the past few dlc's.  Somewhat quirky new "system" to use (but won't really get used much) followed by a "campaign" with almost no story or exciting battles.
Remember when a TW Warhammer dlc used to be a big and exciting spectacle?  Amazing voice actor and exciting campaigns, bringing the lore to life in gripping ways.  I do.  What on earth happened in Warhammer 3 to fall SO far?
Hopefully this upcoming stuff with Nagash will be better.  A big hope

I refuse to play the Sayl campaign till Nightmaw is not at least a summon like Krell.
Very disappointed.

The best thing about Sayl should have been Nightmaw :'(

name: Total War: WARHAMMER III UPGRADE PACK
description: EXPAND YOUR ARMY If you already own Total War: WARHAMMER I or II, expand your experience with the Total War: WARHAMMER 3 Upgrade Pack. This content includes: • 8 factions with 10 legendary lords • The Realm of Chaos narrative campaign • Multiplayer campaigns: The Realm of Chaos, Something Rotten in Kislev and Darkness & Disharmony • Mirrors of Madness game mode" CHOOSE YOUR HERO Play as 10 Legendary Lords! Will you side with the Chaos Gods and take one of their Daemonic Champions, play as an ancient transforming Dragon from Grand Cathay, defend the frozen nation of Kislev with the Ice Queen or play the ultimate villain as the Daemon Prince? REALMS OF CHAOS The last roar of a dying god ruptures the boundary between worlds, opening a portal to the Realm of Chaos. From this maelstrom, the four Ruinous Powers – Khorne, Nurgle, Tzeentch and Slaanesh – emerge, spreading darkness and despair. The stern warriors of Kislev and the vast empire of Grand Cathay stand at the threshold, as a vengeful Daemon Prince vows to destroy those who corrupted him. The coming conflict will engulf all. Will you conquer your daemons? Or command them? UNLEASH YOUR INNER DAEMON Customise the Daemon Prince to your own unique design with a huge suite of body parts and powers, creating billions of potential combinations to suit your own playstyle. MULTIPLAYER CHAOS Multiplayer is now bigger than ever. With the vast 8-player Realm of Chaos multiplayer campaign, the intense 1 v 1 Domination mode, the story-driven multiplayer campaigns built around Kislev and Grand Cathay and ranked & custom battles, there’s no shortage of ways to cause chaos with friends. MIRROR OF MADNESS Take on the Trials of Fate and fight through four increasingly chaotic battles before breaking through into the Realm of Tzeentch and taking the fight to Kairos Fateweaver, or enter the Infinite Portal and bend reality to your will by creating insane custom battles. This Upgrade Pack requires Total War: WARHAMMER I or Total War: WARHAMMER II.
short_description: Upgrade your Total War: WARHAMMER experience with Total War: WARHAMMER III’s original factions and Legendary Lords or wage total war to decide the fate of the Warhammer world across the Realms of Chaos campaign mode.

name: Hearts of Iron IV: Content Creator Pack - Soviet Union 2D Art
description: Paradox Interactive has teamed up with talented modders from the Hearts of Iron IV community to release a content pack with new art assets for the Soviet Union. Ethan “156” Mayer and Aqeel “Uncharted” Mughal , Head Developers on the popular World Ablaze mod for the game, have created new icons and weapons art that reflect the historical designs and art of one of the key players in World War II. A portion of the revenue generated by this Content Creator Pack goes directly to the creators - by buying it you are supporting HOI modders. Soviet Union Community Content Pack includes over 300 new 2D assets: 61 new tank icons 35 new plane icons 32 new ship icons 4 new pieces of infantry art 11 new artillery icons 3 new motorised infantry icons 5 new mechanised infantry icons 3 new armoured car icons 1 new support equipment icon 3 new train icons 23 new icons for the Soviet industry focus tree 54 new icons for the Soviet military focus tree 93 new icons for the Soviet political focus tree 38 new icons for Soviet National Spirit Note: To ensure maximum benefit to the modder creators behind this DLC, this community creator pack is not included in the Hearts of Iron IV subscription package.
short_description: Paradox Interactive has teamed up with talented modders from the Hearts of Iron IV community to release a content pack with new art assets for the Soviet Union.

name: Hearts of Iron IV - DLC Subscription
description: In order to enjoy the content included in the DLC Subscription you will need to own the Hearts of Iron IV base game. Hearts of Iron IV is Paradox Interactive’s grand strategy wargame. Command the mightiest war machines in history, rewriting the events of World War II as the fires of conflict swirl around you. If you are missing some or all of the Hearts of Iron IV expansions or other additional content for the game, this subscription plan makes much of that available for you so you can experience the titanic struggle of 20th century ideologies to its fullest extent for a convenient monthly price. With this subscription, you will have access to all expansion and country packs released to this point, as well as all art and music packs, but not the Community Content Packs - these must be purchased separately. The precise contents of subscription change with every new Hearts of Iron IV release. This subscription also does not include access to any pre-order bonuses that may have been offered or advertised.
short_description: If you are missing some or all of the Hearts of Iron IV expansions or other additional content for the game, this subscription plan makes all of that available for you so you can experience the titanic struggle of 20th century ideologies to its fullest extent.

name: Music - Hearts of Iron IV: Sabaton Soundtrack Vol. 2
description: New Hearts of Iron IV Music Pack Featuring Sabaton Paradox Development Studio and Hearts of Iron IV continue their musical synergy with Swedish heavy metal band Sabaton. Best known for their songs about real historical events and the human side of war, Sabaton has licensed ten new songs for Hearts of Iron IV, each illustrating a moment of World War II with passion and power chords. The 10 songs included in Sabaton Soundtrack Vol. 2 are: Ghost Division: Tells the story of the elusive 7th Panzer Division. Resist and Bite: Recounts the experiences of a Belgian division facing German invasion. Coat of Arms: Deals with the difficult Italian invasion of Greece. Uprising: Celebrates the courageous but doomed Warsaw Uprising of 1944. Screaming Eagles: A song about 101st Airborne’s battles in Bastogne 40:1: Honors a small Polish brigade’s stalling defence of Wizna against a much larger Wehrmacht force. Panzerkampf: Song about the Battle of Kursk, one of the largest tank battles in history. Smoking Snakes: Honors three Brazilian soldiers who refused surrender against overwhelming odds. To Hell and Back: A song about Audie Murphy, the most decorated soldier in American history, and his struggle with PTSD. The Last Battle: Tells of how a motley group of French prisoners, German defectors, and American soldiers defended Castle Itter against a SS division.
short_description: Paradox and Hearts of Iron IV continue their musical synergy with Swedish heavy metal band Sabaton. Best known for their songs about real historical events and the human side of war, Sabaton has licensed ten new songs each illustrating a moment of World War II with passion and power chords.

name: Music - Hearts of Iron IV: Sabaton Soundtrack
description: Paradox Interactive is happy to continue its musical partnership with Swedish power metal band, Sabaton, a group best known for how it uses its music to illustrate great episodes of history. This time, Paradox adds five of Sabatons World War II themed songs for Hearts of Iron IV, the newest Paradox game about humanity’s greatest conflict. The songs included in this content pack are: 1. Hearts of Iron : dedicated to German 9th and 12th armies that covered the escape of citizens fleeing the Soviets to safety with the Western armies. 2. Primo Victoria : dedicated to Operation Overlord and the opening of the Western Front. 3. Stalingrad : dedicated to the Soviet soldiers who died fighting to defend Stalingrad. 4. Wolfpack : dedicated to victims and survivors of the submarine attack on convoy ON-92 in 1942. 5. Night Witche s: dedicated to a deadly Soviet bomber crew composed entirely of women.
short_description: Paradox Interactive continues its musical partnership with Swedish power metal band, Sabaton, a group best known for how it uses its music to illustrate great episodes of history. Paradox adds five of Sabaton's World War II themed songs for Hearts of Iron IV.

name: Hearts of Iron IV: Bonus Songs Pack
description: Hearts of Iron IV: Bonus Songs Pack Round out the musical landscape of your war plans and industrial buildup with this pack of six songs originally only available through pre-orders. This collection of music inspired by the World War II setting includes period anthems and original arrangements of classic tunes. The Bonus Songs Pack includes: March of the Eagles : A pre-order bonus for the espionage focused expansion La Resistance , this stirring march has elements of French classical compositions and European chorale. Katyusha: A pre-order bonus for the Soviet Union themed expansion No Step Back , this is an original arrangement of a Soviet wartime hymn to the patriotic sacrifices made on the eastern front. Bella Ciao : A pre-order bonus for the Italian focused expansion By Blood Alone , “Bella Ciao” became a popular Italian anti-Fascist anthem during the war years. Säkkijärven Polkka: A pre-order bonus for the Nordic themed expansion Arms Against Tyranny , this Finnish folk song became popular on the frontlines of the Winter War, with its chords used as a means of disrupting Soviet radio mines. Carnaval Do Brasil: A pre-order bonus for the South American themed expansion Trial of Allegiance, this is a pastiche of military brass and traditional Brazilian party melodies. Ride of the Valkyries: A pre-order bonus for the German and Central European themed expansion Götterdämmerung, this familiar excerpt from Wagner’s Ring Cycle opera Die Walküre has become synonymous with the threat of war.
short_description: This collection of music inspired by the World War II setting includes period anthems and original arrangements of classic tunes.

The Hearts of Iron Radio Pack gives access to: Faction Radio Channels: A total of 35 new songs spread across three faction specific radio channels, each with their own unique look and design. Free Access to Older Hearts of Iron Tracks: The scores for Hearts of Iron II and Hearts of Iron III will be freely available to all Hearts of Iron IV players, letting you experience the soundscapes of veterans who have gone before. COMRADES, citizens, brothers and sisters, men of our Army and Navy!

My words are addressed to you, dear friends!

The perfidious military attack by Hitlerite Germany on our Fatherland, begun on June 22, is continuing. In spite of the heroic resistance of the Red Army, and although the enemy’s finest divisions and finest air force units have already been smashed and have met their doom on the field of battle, the enemy continues to push forward, hurling fresh forces to the front. Hitler’s troops have succeeded in capturing Lithuania, a considerable part of Latvia, the western part of Byelorussia and part of Western Ukraine. The fascist aircraft are extending the range of their operations, bombing Murmansk, Orsha, Moghilev, Smolensk, Kiev, Odessa, Sevastopol. Grave danger overhangs our country.

How could it have happened that our glorious Red Army surrendered a number of our cities and districts to the fascist armies? Is it really true that the German-fascist troops are invincible, as the braggart fascist propagandists are ceaselessly blaring forth?

Of course not! History shows that there are no invincible armies and never have been. Napoleon’s army was considered invincible, but it was beaten successively by the armies of Russia, England and Germany. Kaiser Wilhelm’s German army in the period of the First Imperialist War was also considered invincible, but it was beaten several times by Russian and Anglo-French troops, and was finally smashed by the Anglo-French forces. The same must be said of Hitler’s German-fascist army of to-day. This army had not yet met with serious resistance on the continent of Europe. Only on our territory has it met with serious resistance. And if as a result of this resistance the finest divisions of Hitler’s German-fascist army have been defeated by our Red Army, this means that it too can be smashed and will be smashed, as were the armies of Napoleon and Wilhelm.

As to part of our territory having nevertheless been seized by the German-fascist troops, this is chiefly due to the fact that the war of fascist Germany against the U.S.S.R. began under conditions that were favorable for the German forces and unfavorable for the Soviet forces. The fact of the matter is that the troops of Germany, a country at war, were already fully mobilized, and the 170 divisions brought up to the Soviet frontiers and hurled by Germany against the U.S.S.R. were in a state of complete readiness, only awaiting the signal to move into action, whereas the Soviet troops had still to effect mobilization and move up to the frontiers. Of no little importance in this respect was the fact that fascist Germany suddenly and treacherously violated the non-aggression pact which she had concluded in 1939 with the U.S.S.R., regardless of the circumstance that she would be regarded as the aggressor by the whole world. Naturally, our peace-loving country, not wishing to take the initiative in breaking the pact, could not resort to perfidy.

It may be asked, how could the Soviet Government have consented to conclude a non-aggression pact with such perfidious people, such fiends as Hitler and Ribbentrop? Was this not an error on the part of the Soviet Government? Of course not! Non-aggression pacts are pacts of peace between two states. It was such a pact that Germany proposed to us in 1939. Could the Soviet Government have declined such a proposal? I think that not a single peace-loving state could decline a peace treaty with a neighbouring state even though the latter were headed by such monsters and cannibals as Hitler and Ribbentrop. But that, of course, only on the one indispensable condition-that this peace treaty did not jeopardize, either directly or indirectly, the territorial integrity, independence and honour of the peace-loving state. As is well known, the non-aggression pact between Germany and the U.S.S.R. was precisely such a pact.

What did we gain by concluding the non-aggression pact with Germany? We secured our country peace for a year and a half and the opportunity of preparing our forces to repulse fascist Germany should she risk an attack on our country despite the pact. This was a definite advantage for us and a disadvantage for fascist Germany. What has fascist Germany gained and what has she lost by perfidiously tearing up the pact and attacking the U.S.S.R.? She has gained a certain advantageous position for her troops for a short period of time, but she has lost politically by exposing herself in the eyes of the entire world as a bloodthirsty aggressor. There can be no doubt that this short-lived military gain for Germany is only an episode, while the tremendous political gain of the U.S.S.R. is a weighty and lasting factor that is bound to forth the basis for the development of outstanding military successes of the Red Army in the war with fascist Germany.

That is why the whole of our valiant Red Army, the whole of our valiant Navy, all the falcons of our Air Force, all the peoples of our country, all the finest men and women of Europe, America and Asia, and, finally, all the finest men and women of Germany—denounce the treacherous acts of the German-fascists, sympathize with the Soviet Government, approve its conduct, and see that ours is a just cause, that the enemy will be defeated, and that we are bound to win.

In consequence of this war which has been forced upon us, our country has come to death grips with its bitterest and most cunning enemy—German fascism. Our troops are fighting heroically against an enemy armed to the teeth with tanks and aircraft. Overcoming numerous difficulties, the Red Army and Red Navy are self-sacrificingly fighting for every inch of Soviet soil. The main forces of the Red Army are coming into action equipped with thousands of tanks and planes. The soldiers of the Red Army are displaying unexampled valour. Our resistance to the enemy is growing in strength and power. Side by side with the Red Army, the entire Soviet people is rising in defence of our native land.

What is required to put an end to the danger imperilling our country and what measures must be taken to smash the enemy?

Above all it is essential that our people, the Soviet people, should appreciate the full immensity of the danger that threatens our country and give up all complacency, casualness and the mentality of peaceful constructive work that was so natural before the war, but which is fatal to-day, when war has radically changed the whole situation. The enemy is cruel and implacable. He is out to seize our lands watered by the sweat of our brows, to seize our grain and oil secured by the labour of our hands. He is out to restore the rule of the landlords, to restore tsarism, to destroy the national culture and the national existence as states of the Russians, Ukrainians, Byelorussians, Lithuanians, Latvians, Esthonians, Uzbeks, Tatars, Moldavians, Georgians, Armenians, Azerbaijanians and the other free peoples of the Soviet Union, to Germanize them, to turn them into the slaves of German princes and barons. Thus the issue is one of life and death for the Soviet State, of life and death for the peoples of the U.S.S.R.; the issue is whether the peoples of the Soviet Union shall be free or fall into slavery. The Soviet people must realize this and abandon all complacency; they must mobilize themselves and reorganize all their work on a new, war-time footing, where there can be no mercy to the enemy.

But yeah, I would say this DLC is a 10/10

These speeches get outright annoying after a couple playthroughs. If a good song is playing, well, too bad, because you're about to hear a 4 minute monologue from Neville Chamberlain for the 99999th time. It's also annoying how they included speeches that aren't even in English. It's also an ironic oversight by the developers that a motivating speech about the defense of Moscow by Joseph Stalin can play even when you're playing as the attacking Germans. Overall, this DLC is pointless, and paying money for this was such a waste.

You'll have to turn all other volumes down to 5% and have voice volume set to full, then crank your speakers right up to just about hear the Churchills mumbling. Released 5 years ago and you can't even be bothered to master the volume and you charge for it. 

Shame on you!

Every DLC in the game adds something positive to it. Not this. If you feel your experience is incomplete without this $5 waste of hard drive space, let me be the first to assure you that you can safely avoid this purchase.

This is more of a song pack, really, with historical speeches from political and military leaders playing over them. The music is not particularly inspired, and the speeches run way too long. Not to mention they will play at absolutely mind-boggling times, such as a communist Britain attacking Germany, but Churchill gives the speech.

The absolute worst part about this though? I play the game with music OFF. Yet, when a war in-game starts, it will trigger these speeches to begin playing, regardless if you turned off the radio or not.

I disabled this DLC in the launcher. Not only is it a complete ripoff, I wouldn't even want it if it was free. Avoid this "content" completely.

name: Country Pack - Hearts of Iron IV: Battle for the Bosporus
description: This expansion for Hearts of Iron IV adds dedicated national focus trees to the minor powers around the Black Sea and the Aegean. Bulgaria, Greece and Turkey can each chart unique historical and alternate history paths as they resist efforts by the major powers to dominate the strategic sealanes of the Bosporus and Dardanelles. Features of Hearts of Iron IV: Battle for the Bosporus include: National Focus Tree for Bulgaria: Balance the factions in a divided land or reclaim territory lost in 1919. Follow the historical alliance with Germany or resist the German pressures on Tsar Boris, standing up for a free Bulgaria. National Focus Tree for Greece: Riven by political division, Greek neutrality is maintained by the controversial Metaxas. Build a strong relationship with the Allies under the guidance of King George II, follow arch-conservatives into fascism or restore the glories of Greek Empire in the east. National Focus Tree for Turkey: Robbed of its empire in the last war, what future is there for Turkey in a new one? Continue or halt the reforms of Kemalism, stand as the protector of the Balkans in a new regional faction, restore the Sultan to the throne or found a new Islamic state. New Music Tracks New 3D Unit Models for Balkan and Turkish Armies Unique voiceovers for Bulgaria, Greece and Turkey
short_description: Three new national focus trees are the heart of this expansion. The minor powers of Bulgaria, Greece and Turkey each have new alternate historical paths to follow as they resist incursions into the strategic sea lanes of the Back and Aegean seas.

As a long-time hoi4 player, I can say that this expansion is absolutely worth it on three conditions; 
1- You enjoy a more difficult playstyle. Greece and Turkey are quite weak historically speaking, and that's reflected in the dlc. (Bulgaria isn't too bad though). 
2- You want more color and activity in the Med, it's solid for Italian, French, and Soviet players due to the extra content it gives to the region. For me, I just like having the AI wander around doing things completely unrelated to everything else in the background, so I always enable it. 
3- You get it on sale. 10 dollars isn't as crazy for the expansion as some people are saying, but if you can get it at 50% off, absolutely do it. Well worth it in my opinion
TÜRKİYE

Battle for the Bosporus adds focused content for Turkey, Bulgaria, and Greece, including new national focus trees and historical events. It improves gameplay in the region with more strategic options, political decisions, and paths for alternate history. If you enjoy playing or expanding the Balkans and Eastern Mediterranean in HOI4, this DLC is worth getting.

name: Country Pack - Hearts of Iron IV: Trial of Allegiance
description: Write new alternate histories of the globe’s most dramatic conflict in Trial of Allegiance , a new country pack for Hearts of Iron IV . Explore and dominate a world where the strife of the Second World War threatens the safety and security of South American nations. Build a regional superpower to counter the rising global powers, or join the war early, taking your chances with one of the major ideological blocs. South America is rich in resources and manpower - can you lead Argentina, Brazil or Chile to a glorious alternate future? Hearts of Iron IV: Trial of Allegiance includes: National Focus Tree for Brazil: Challenged by a decade of internal strife and constitutional crises, Brazil can follow the historical path as a late but valuable member of the Allied cause. Alternately, seek continental hegemony under a new government with new priorities. National Focus Tree for Argentina: Rife with corruption, revolts and worker unrest, Argentina presents a difficult political challenge. Once you’ve settled the problems at home, you can contribute more on the world stage and help this nation reach its full potential. National Focus Tree for Chile: Chile is a vigorous republic with a radical tradition that must face domestic fascist parties, the embers of ethnic unrest and the imposing influence of the United States. Chart a course of equality and renewal, rallying under the banner of continental unity. Paraguay and Uruguay: Minor content as well as several new releasable nations. New Art: New 3D unit sprites for the Brazilian army, as well as new unique 2D art for National Focus trees and units New Music: Six new songs, with two each for the major nations in the pack. New Voiceovers: Voiceovers in Brazilian Portuguese, Argentinian Spanish and Chilean Spanish.
short_description: Explore alternate WWII histories in "Trial of Allegiance" for Hearts of Iron IV. Lead South American nations like Argentina, Brazil, or Chile. Build a superpower or join global blocs, using regional resources and manpower for a unique, glorious future.

Trial of Allegiance adds a few national focus trees for South American countries, but that’s about it. There are no major gameplay changes, and the focus trees feel limited and underdeveloped, so most players won’t find it very engaging. At full price, it’s hard to justify, especially if you’re looking for meaningful new content or improved mechanics. Best to skip it or wait for a deep sale.
